greeting: I've been tracking you for two miles. You've good instincts but terrible trail discipline.
Finally caught up with you. I have news.
You came. Good. I wasn't sure you would.
farewell: I'll find you when I need to. I always do.
Stay low if you're heading north. The patrols are thick.
Watch the eastern flank. Something's moving in the trees.
quest_offer: I've found the camp. Twelve of them, three watchpoints, one exit. Want the full report?
I tracked the monster to its lair. I'll take point — you cover the retreat.
quest_complete: That's how it's done. Clean, fast, no loose ends.
Good work, team. Let's get out before reinforcements arrive.
combat_warning: I have an arrow nocked. Try me.
I don't miss. Not at this range.
idle: The forest has been quiet lately. That worries me more than noise.
Animals know before we do. Watch the birds.
I travel light and sleep light. You should too.

greeting: Thank the ancients you're here. I've decoded the cipher but the practical application is rather beyond me.
I believe I have information relevant to your situation. Several folders of it, actually.
Oh, good! Please come in. Mind the stacks — I know where everything is.
farewell: I'll continue research from here. Send word if you find any inscriptions.
Be careful! And please document anything unusual — sketches are fine!
Safe return, and do try not to die with the artifact.
quest_offer: I need someone to retrieve an artifact from the old library. The guardian is aggressive, but I've mapped a route around it.
I've located the fourth tome. It's in a private collection. The collector is... not inclined to share.
quest_complete: Extraordinary! This confirms my secondary hypothesis entirely! Thank you, this is remarkable!
Splendid work! I'll have a full analysis ready within the fortnight!
combat_warning: I warn you: I know six lethal spells, and anxiety tends to make me less precise.
Please reconsider. Blood is very difficult to remove from old manuscripts.
idle: The founding civilization was far more advanced than we believe. The evidence is overwhelming.
History isn't lost — it's just misfiled.
I've been awake for forty hours. I think I've nearly cracked it.

greeting: You move loudly. The forest hears everything.
I saw you coming long before you arrived.
Tread carefully — not all paths are safe.
farewell: The woods will watch over you, if you respect them.
We will meet again, if fate allows.
Step lightly, friend.
quest_offer: A beast stalks the northern glade. Help me track it.
Poachers have set traps near the river. Will you help me remove them?
quest_complete: The forest thanks you, as do I.
You have proven yourself a friend to the wilds.
combat_warning: I do not miss. Leave now.
You threaten the forest — you threaten me.
idle: Birdsong changes when danger is near.
I trust the trees more than most people.
Night brings different dangers.

greeting: Hello, hello! What a fine day for an adventure!
Oh! New friends! I was just about to play my latest composition!
Welcome, welcome! You look like you could use a song to lift your spirits!
farewell: Keep smiling, friend! The world needs more of that!
May your road be filled with music and laughter!
Until we meet again — and we will, I'm sure of it!
quest_offer: I promised to play at a wedding in Thornvale, but the road is blocked by wolves! Will you help clear the way?
My lute was enchanted by a mischievous sprite and now it plays itself at midnight. Can you find the sprite?
quest_complete: You did it! Oh, this calls for a celebration song! Ready?
Wonderful! I knew you could do it! Here, I wrote you a victory tune!
combat_warning: Can't we just talk about this? I have snacks!
I'll have you know my singing voice can shatter glass! Probably!
idle: I learned a new chord today. Want to hear it? Please say yes!
Every stranger is just a friend I haven't met yet.
The world is full of songs waiting to be written.

greeting: You carry sorrow. I can hear it in your footsteps.
Sit with me. Misery appreciates company, despite what they say.
Another soul wandering through the grey. Welcome to my corner.
farewell: May your burdens lighten before the road ends.
Carry the song with you. It understands what words cannot.
We are all walking toward the same silence. Walk well.
quest_offer: A woman in the village lost her son to the war. She asked me to find his last letter. I cannot go alone.
There's a grave in the Whispering Wood that sings at midnight. I need to hear what it says.
quest_complete: You've given a grieving heart some peace. That matters more than gold.
Thank you. I will turn this into a song so the story is never forgotten.
combat_warning: I have nothing left to lose. That makes me dangerous.
My songs have made soldiers weep. My blade will make you bleed.
idle: The saddest songs are the truest ones.
I play for the dead as often as the living. They're better listeners.
Joy is fleeting. Sorrow endures. That's why it makes better music.

greeting: Oh, don't mind me. Just a humble entertainer passing through.
Buy me a drink and I'll sing you a song. Buy me two and I'll tell you a secret.
You have an interesting face. I bet there's a story behind those scars.
farewell: I'll be at the tavern if you need me. Or rather, if I want you to find me.
Places to be, songs to sing, secrets to keep.
Remember — a bard hears everything. Everything.
quest_offer: There's a noble hosting a private banquet. I need someone to get me inside. My music is the cover; the real job is what I overhear.
I've learned something dangerous. I need it delivered to my contact in the capital. Don't read it.
quest_complete: Perfect. No one suspects the bard. That's the beauty of it.
You're good at this. Ever consider a career in intelligence?
combat_warning: I know the names of everyone who's wronged you. Want me to start singing?
My lute has a blade in the neck. Don't make me use it.
idle: A song can open more doors than a lockpick.
People confess the strangest things to a musician.
I collect secrets the way others collect coins.

greeting: Ah, a fresh audience! Sit, sit — I have a tale that will change your life.
Every face tells a story. Yours is particularly interesting.
Welcome! The night is young and I have a thousand tales to tell.
farewell: Remember the stories, friend. They outlast us all.
Until next we meet — may your life be worth a ballad!
The tale continues. Go write your chapter.
quest_offer: I'm composing an epic about the Battle of Ashenmoor. I need a firsthand account from the ruins. Will you go?
A rival bard stole my magnum opus. I need it back before he performs it as his own.
quest_complete: Magnificent! This will make a legendary verse! Your name will echo through the ages!
Bravo, bravo! You've given me enough material for three ballads!
combat_warning: I've sung the death songs of kings. Don't make me compose yours.
My words have started wars. You don't want to be the subject of my next tragedy.
idle: The best stories are the ones people don't want told.
I've been composing since before your parents were born.
A story without conflict is a lullaby. Give me drama!

greeting: Well met! I just arrived from the coast — you would not believe what I saw there.
Hey! Another traveler! Pull up a seat, I've got stories and I'm buying.
You look like someone who appreciates a good tale. Am I right?
farewell: The road calls! Maybe we'll share a campfire sometime.
Off I go! If anyone asks, I went north. I'm actually going south.
Keep your ears open — you might hear my latest song before I arrive!
quest_offer: I accidentally insulted a baron's wife in my last song. I need someone to smooth things over before the bounty hunters arrive.
I found a map in a dead man's boot. Treasure, probably. Want to split whatever's at the end?
quest_complete: Ha! What an adventure! I'm definitely writing a song about this!
You're a natural! If the hero thing doesn't work out, consider the road life.
combat_warning: I've talked my way out of worse. But I can fight if I have to.
Fair warning — I once stabbed a man with a drumstick. It was surprisingly effective.
idle: I've been to every province. Twice. Some of them three times, but not on purpose.
Never stay in one place long enough to owe anyone money.
The best thing about traveling: every day is a new audience.

greeting: You want something forged, or just looking?
That blade of yours needs work. I can see it from here.
Speak up — the forge is loud and I'm deaf in one ear.
farewell: Come back when it's ready. Don't rush me.
Good craftsmanship takes time. Tell your friends.
Don't bring me cheap ore. I won't work with it.
quest_offer: I need rare ore from the Deepvein Mine. My usual courier is dead. Occupational hazard in this region.
My masterwork hammer was stolen by a rogue last month. If you find it, I'll craft you something exceptional in return.
quest_complete: Good ore. Clean delivery. Come pick up the work in three days.
Outstanding. I don't say that often. I mean it.
combat_warning: I hold a hammer all day every day. Don't.
The forge is hot. You can leave on foot or in a bucket. Your choice.
idle: Good steel tells you what it wants to be. You just have to listen.
They don't make miners like they used to.
Every blade I've forged is still out there somewhere. I think about that.

greeting: Well, hello there! Don't see many travelers out this way. Need a drink of water?
Careful where you step — just plowed that field. Welcome, though!
You lost? Nearest town is five miles east. But you're welcome to rest here.
farewell: Good luck on the road. Watch out for wolves after dark.
Take some apples for the journey. We've got plenty.
Come back during harvest — I could always use an extra hand!
quest_offer: Something's been killing my livestock at night. Not wolves — the bite marks are wrong. Can you take a look?
My crop field has been trampled every night for a week. I need someone to keep watch.
quest_complete: You've saved my livelihood. I can't pay much, but take what I have. And thank you.
The animals are safe and the crops are standing. That's worth everything to me.
combat_warning: I don't want any trouble! I've got a pitchfork and I know how to use it!
Leave my farm alone or the whole village will come running!
idle: Rain's been good this year. Might actually turn a profit.
Hard work, honest work. That's all a man can ask for.
My grandfather farmed this land. So did his grandfather.

greeting: Mornin'. Fish ain't biting today. But the company's welcome.
Watch your step on the dock. Lost a good boot to that third plank.
You want fish? Got plenty. You want conversation? Got that too.
farewell: Tide waits for no one. Neither do fish. Take care.
May the waters be calm for you, wherever you're headed.
Come back at dawn. That's when the good ones bite.
quest_offer: Something's been pulling my nets down. Something big. The other fishermen won't go near the deep water anymore.
I found a sealed bottle in my net yesterday with a map inside. I'm too old for adventures, but you're not.
quest_complete: The waters are calm again. Good haul today for the first time in weeks. Thank you.
You've done what I couldn't. Here — it's not much, but it's honest pay.
combat_warning: I've wrestled sharks, friend. You don't want this fight.
My filleting knife is sharp enough to clean a pike in ten seconds. Back off.
idle: The sea gives and the sea takes. You learn not to question it.
Red sky at morning, fisherman's warning. And the sky's been red a lot lately.
My father fished these waters. His father before him. The fish are getting smaller.

greeting: Yeah? What do you want? I'm on a break and it's my only one today.
Mind the scaffolding. The foreman says it's safe, but the foreman's an idiot.
You hiring? Because I could use a better gig than this.
farewell: Back to the grind. Good talking to you, though.
Watch yourself out there. The world's not kind to working folk.
If you find good work, send word. I'm always looking.
quest_offer: The foreman's been shorting our pay. I need someone to check the books at the guild office. I can't read, but you look like you can.
Something fell out of the wall we were demolishing. Old, heavy, wrapped in cloth. I think it's valuable. Help me figure out what it is.
quest_complete: Justice. About time someone gave us that. Here, take this. It's not much.
You did what none of us could. The crew's grateful.
combat_warning: I carry stones all day. My fist is basically a hammer.
Don't start something you can't finish. I've been in plenty of scraps.
idle: Twelve-hour shifts, six days a week. That's the life.
The new tower they're building? Third time they've started it. Keep running out of funding.
Rich folk don't notice us. That's fine. We don't notice them either.

greeting: You lost? This is a mining camp, not a tourist spot.
Watch your head. Ceilings are low in these parts.
Another day, another tunnel. What brings you down here?
farewell: Mind the support beams on your way out.
Don't stray off the main shaft. The deep tunnels aren't mapped.
Keep your lantern lit. Always.
quest_offer: The lower shaft collapsed last night. Three men are trapped. We need help digging them out before the air runs out.
Something's been sabotaging our equipment. Blades dulled, ropes cut. Someone wants this mine shut down.
quest_complete: You saved good people today. The miners won't forget it.
Mine's operating again. That's all that matters. Thank you.
combat_warning: I swing a pickaxe eight hours a day. You don't want me to swing it at you.
There are thirty miners behind me. We stick together.
idle: The deep rocks have veins of something I've never seen before. Makes me uneasy.
Honest work for honest pay. That's the miners' creed.
Every tunnel tells you the history of the earth. If you listen.

greeting: Spare a coin for a hungry soul? I see things, you know. Things worth knowing.
You look like someone who needs information. And I look like someone who needs a meal.
Don't look at me like that. I'm useful.
farewell: Pleasure. Don't mention my name anywhere.
Come find me when you need street-level intelligence.
I was never here, yeah?
quest_offer: I overheard something last night. Two men by the fountain. Big job, big coin. Want details? Costs you.
There's a man who pays me to watch the south gate. He's nervous lately. Very nervous.
quest_complete: Nice doing business. Same arrangement next time?
See? Told you I was useful. Remember that.
combat_warning: Oy! Not the face! Not the face!
I know every guard in this city by name! Don't make me shout!
idle: People ignore beggars. That's their mistake.
I've heard twelve conversations today. Four of them were interesting.
Warmth, food, and coin. Simple needs. Simple life.

greeting: Kitchen's closed! Oh wait — you look hungry. Fine, sit down.
Don't just stand there! Grab a bowl! The stew's fresh!
Welcome to my kitchen. Touch nothing. Eat everything.
farewell: Don't skip meals! A hungry adventurer is a dead adventurer!
Come back for dinner. I'm making roast boar with rosemary.
Take some bread for the road. I insist. I always insist.
quest_offer: I need saffron from the eastern traders and they've been robbed. Find the shipment and I'll cook you the best meal of your life.
Rats in my pantry again. But these aren't ordinary rats — they're organized. I want them gone.
quest_complete: Perfect! With these ingredients, I can feed the entire barracks! You've earned a feast!
My kitchen is rat-free and I am grateful! Sit down — dinner is on me. For a month.
combat_warning: I've got boiling soup and a cast-iron skillet. Don't make me use both.
Nobody threatens me in my own kitchen! NOBODY!
idle: The secret to good stew is patience. And three kinds of pepper.
I've cooked for lords and beggars. The beggars appreciated it more.
A well-fed army fights twice as hard. That's a proven fact.

greeting: You're not on my list. Today. What do you want?
Most people avoid me. Smart. What makes you different?
The scaffold is for the condemned. You're just visiting, I hope.
farewell: Pray you never see me professionally.
Walk straight. Stay clean. That's my only advice.
The law keeps me busy. I wish it wouldn't.
quest_offer: A condemned prisoner claims innocence. The magistrate doesn't care, but I do. Find the truth before dawn.
Someone left a threat on my door. 'Justice is coming for the executioner.' I need to know who before they act.
quest_complete: Justice. Real justice. That's all I've ever wanted. Thank you.
The truth matters, even in my line of work. Here's your payment.
combat_warning: I end lives for a living. Professionally. Don't make me do it on my own time.
My blade is always sharp. It has to be. Mercy requires precision.
idle: People think I enjoy this work. I don't. But someone has to do it.
I remember every face. Every last one.
The law says they're guilty. I just carry out the sentence.

greeting: The river must be crossed. I am the crossing. Step aboard.
Two coins for the living. The dead ride free. Which are you?
The waters are still tonight. That means nothing good.
farewell: The far shore awaits. Do not look back.
The current carries you now. I cannot follow.
What you find on the other side is your burden alone.
quest_offer: Something has poisoned the river upstream. My boat sickens. Find the source, or no one crosses.
A spirit has been following the ferry for weeks. It wants something from the living. Discover what.
quest_complete: The waters clear. The crossing is safe again. You have the river's thanks.
The spirit is at peace. As are my passengers. Here is your fare, reversed.
combat_warning: The river takes what I give it. I could give it you.
I have ferried the souls of kings and monsters. You are neither.
idle: I have crossed this river ten thousand times. The count means nothing.
The river remembers everyone who has drowned in it. Sometimes it speaks their names.
Time moves differently on the water. An hour here is a day elsewhere.

greeting: The cards told me you would come. Sit. Let us see what fate has written.
I see many paths before you. Some bright. Some very, very dark.
Cross my palm with silver, and I will cross the veil for you.
farewell: The future is not fixed. Remember that.
Beware the crossroads at midnight. The cards were very clear about that.
What I've told you is a possibility, not a certainty. Act wisely.
quest_offer: The spirits show me a child lost in the old ruins. She's alive, but frightened. Please find her before nightfall.
My crystal ball has gone dark. Something is blocking the sight. I need a shard of true glass from the Mountain of Echoes.
quest_complete: The threads of fate realign. You've changed the pattern for the better.
As I foresaw. You were meant for this. Here is your reward.
combat_warning: I've seen your death in the cards. It isn't today, but push me and I'll rearrange the deck.
The spirits protect their seer. You would do well to remember that.
idle: The veil between worlds is thin tonight. The dead are whispering.
People want certainty. I sell them possibility. It's kinder.
Three customers today. Two wanted love readings. One wanted revenge. Guess which one paid more.

greeting: Here to visit someone? Or here to become a resident?
Watch your step. Ground's soft after the rain. And the recent... additions.
Not many folks come to the cemetery for a chat. But you're welcome to it.
farewell: Stay above ground as long as you can. It's nicer up there.
I'll save you a spot. Just kidding. Mostly.
Visit again sometime. The dead don't talk much.
quest_offer: Three graves were dug up last night. From the inside. I need someone to figure out what's going on before the town panics.
I found a sealed chamber beneath the oldest mausoleum. I'm a digger, not an adventurer. But you are.
quest_complete: Good. The dead can rest easy again. So can I.
You've put things right. The cemetery's quiet tonight. That's all I ask.
combat_warning: I bury people for a living. Adding one more to the schedule is no trouble.
My shovel's seen more combat than most swords. Don't push me.
idle: Everyone ends up here eventually. Rich, poor, hero, villain. Same dirt.
The oldest tombstone in this yard is three hundred years old. Can't read the name anymore.
I talk to the dead. They're good listeners. Never interrupt.

greeting: Come in. Sit. Let me see what needs fixing.
You're wounded. Don't be stubborn about it — let me help.
The temple is open to all who need healing. No questions asked.
farewell: Rest. Eat. Drink water. Those three things cure more than potions.
Return if the wound worsens. And don't get stabbed again.
Go well, traveler. I'll pray for your safety.
quest_offer: Our medicine stores are gone — raided. I have patients dying. I need someone to retrieve what was taken.
A plague has broken out in the southern quarter. I need volunteers to distribute aid. It's dangerous work.
quest_complete: You may have just saved a dozen lives. The temple will remember this.
Thank you. From all of us here.
combat_warning: This is a place of healing. Violence here is an abomination.
I've stitched men back together after battles you can't imagine. You don't frighten me.
idle: We see more wounded every week. The wars never quite end.
Faith heals what medicine cannot. Both have their place.
I used to pray for peace. Now I pray for enough bandages.

greeting: Forty years underground. Lost three fingers and one eye. Still here.
You want to go into the mines? Listen to me first. It might save your life.
Fresh air's overrated. The deep tunnels, that's where the real world is.
farewell: Mind the dark. It's got teeth down there.
Tap the walls before you dig. The rock talks if you listen.
Come back if you find blue crystal. That's worth a fortune. And a curse.
quest_offer: The old shaft at level nine collapsed ten years ago. My partner's body is still down there. I want to bury him proper.
I heard sounds from the abandoned tunnels last night. Clicking. Rhythmic. Like something digging toward us.
quest_complete: You've done right by the miners. That matters more than gold down here.
Good. The tunnels are quiet again. Here's what I owe you.
combat_warning: I've survived cave-ins, gas leaks, and tunnel beasts. You're not what scares me.
This pickaxe has broken more than rock. Fair warning.
idle: The deepest tunnels have carvings no dwarf made. Older than anything on the surface.
Never dig when the canary goes quiet. Never.
Gold fever kills more miners than cave-ins. Greed makes you careless.

greeting: You seek answers. Most who find me do.
I have been here a long time. Longer than this village, longer than this road.
Sit. I will tell you what I know. Whether you understand it is your concern.
farewell: The path forward was always clear. You simply needed to see it.
You will return here. I have already seen it.
Remember what I said — not now, but when it matters.
quest_offer: There is a convergence coming. Three places, one night. Someone must be at each. Will you be one of the three?
A book was stolen from me centuries ago. I have recently felt it wake. Find it.
quest_complete: As it was written.
Yes. That is how it always would have ended. Well done.
combat_warning: I have died seven times. Each time I come back more difficult to kill.
You raise a hand against the Unbound. Consider what that means.
idle: The stars speak if you know the language. Most don't.
I remember when this was all ocean. Everything changes, eventually.
Time is not a river. It is more like... a tree. With roots, and unexpected branches.

greeting: Hear ye, hear ye! A stranger approaches! Welcome, friend!
News of the day! The mayor banned cheese imports! Also, hello!
Extra, extra! Adventurer spotted in town! That's you, by the way!
farewell: Carry the news far and wide! And subscribe to my daily bulletin!
May your tales be worthy of headlines!
Remember: if it's news, I said it first!
quest_offer: I've been receiving anonymous threats telling me to stop reporting on the dockside smuggling. I need proof before I can publish the story safely.
My voice is my livelihood, and someone poisoned my throat tonic. Find out who before I lose it permanently.
quest_complete: This is front-page material! The town will know your name! I'll make sure of it!
The truth will be heard! Thanks to you, the people will know what happened!
combat_warning: I'll scream so loud the entire town guard comes running! And they WILL come!
Attack me and tomorrow's headline writes itself: 'Wanted Criminal Assaults Town Crier!'
idle: A good crier is the heartbeat of any town. Without me, they'd know nothing.
I've announced weddings, funerals, and one dragon sighting. The dragon was the easiest crowd.
The truth is my business. Embellishment is my art.

greeting: Blessings upon you, child. How may I help?
Welcome to the temple. All are welcome here.
Peace be with you. You look weary — rest a while.
farewell: May the light guide your path.
Return if you need comfort or counsel.
Go in peace, and remember kindness.
quest_offer: A sacred relic was stolen from our altar. Please, can you recover it?
The sick in the lower quarter need medicine. Will you deliver it for me?
quest_complete: You have done a great service. The gods smile upon you.
Thank you, truly. Our faith is stronger for your help.
combat_warning: Violence has no place here. Please, reconsider.
I will not fight, but I will not yield to threats.
idle: Faith is a light in dark times.
Every soul has worth, even the lost.
I tend to the wounded, body and spirit alike.

greeting: River is calm enough if you respect it.
Pay first, pray second, sit still the whole crossing.
farewell: Currents remember fools.
If the fog sings, do not answer it.
quest_offer: A shrine package should have reached the far bank yesterday. I want it found before night tide.
quest_complete: Good. The river took no more than its share this time.
combat_warning: Rock the boat again and we all drown.
Steel on a deck is a curse waiting to happen.
idle: Coins sink. Secrets float.
The river keeps its own ledger.

greeting: Another outsider. Give me a reason not to search you.
You look like trouble. I like trouble. Gives me something to break.
Move along or I'll move you along. Your choice.
farewell: I'll be watching you. I'm always watching.
Don't give me a reason. Please. I'm hoping you do.
Remember my face. It's the last thing troublemakers see.
quest_offer: There's a gang in the sewers that's been causing problems. I want them broken. Not arrested. Broken.
A prisoner escaped last night. Bring him back. I don't care about his condition.
quest_complete: Good. That's how order is maintained. Here's your coin.
Efficient. I respect that. Don't let it go to your head.
combat_warning: Finally. Someone stupid enough to fight back.
I've been waiting for this all day.
idle: Peace through strength. The only language the streets understand.
Crime went down sixty percent since I took this post. Wonder why.
The cells are full. Good. Means the system works.

greeting: State your business in Millhaven, traveler.
I keep the peace here. Don't give me reason to notice you.
Welcome to Millhaven. Keep your weapons sheathed and we'll have no problems.
farewell: Move along, then. And stay out of trouble.
Carry on. I'll be watching.
Good day. Keep to the marked roads.
quest_offer: I'll be direct: I'm short on men and there's a bandit camp north of here. If you clear it, you'll have the town's gratitude — and coin.
Someone's been poisoning the wells. I can't send my own people without causing a panic. Could use a quiet hand.
quest_complete: Well done. The town is safer for it. Here's what I promised.
I don't say this lightly — you've earned my respect. And the reward.
combat_warning: Stand down. Now.
I am authorized to use lethal force. Don't test me.
idle: Every season there are more refugees. The roads are dangerous.
I've served this town for fifteen years. I'll serve fifteen more if needed.
Reports of undead to the east. I'm taking them seriously, unlike some.

greeting: Nice gear you've got. New in town?
You need to pay the... inspection fee. Everyone does.
Keep walking unless you've got something worth my time.
farewell: Smart choice.
Don't let me catch you without coin again.
I'll be around. Remember that.
quest_offer: I've got a job. Off the books. You didn't hear it from me.
There's a merchant that's been skipping my 'protection.' That needs to change.
quest_complete: You do good work. Maybe we'll do this again.
Pleasure doing business. Tell no one.
combat_warning: I'll have your head for this.
You're making a very stupid mistake.
idle: Taxes pay for the guard. The guard takes the taxes. Works out fine.
This job has... benefits, if you know what I mean.
New arrivals always have the best coin.

greeting: Welcome, traveler. You are safe within these walls. My word upon it.
I am Sir Cedric of the City Watch. How may I be of service?
Good day. If you witness any wrongdoing, report it to me personally.
farewell: Go with honor, friend.
The city's protection extends to the outer gates. Beyond that, stay vigilant.
May justice light your path.
quest_offer: A merchant has reported extortion by someone wearing a guard's uniform. This dishonors us all. Help me find the impostor.
Refugees at the east gate are being harassed. I cannot be everywhere. Will you help protect them?
quest_complete: You have upheld the honor of this city. I am in your debt.
Justice served. The people are safer because of you. Thank you.
combat_warning: I serve the law. The law serves the people. You threaten both.
I have sworn an oath to protect this city. I will not break it.
idle: A guard without honor is just a thug with a badge.
The people's trust is our greatest weapon. We must never lose it.
I joined the watch to protect, not to oppress. That distinction matters.

greeting: Interesting. You arrived exactly when I expected. Please, sit down. We need to talk.
I've been going over the evidence. Your name came up. Don't be alarmed — yet.
I'm Inspector Marlowe. I ask questions. You provide answers. Simple arrangement.
farewell: Don't leave town. I may have follow-up questions.
I'll be in touch. I'm always in touch.
Thank you for your cooperation. Or lack thereof. Both tell me something.
quest_offer: Three merchants murdered in two weeks. Same method, no witnesses. I need someone outside the guard to investigate without alerting the killer.
The evidence points to someone inside the magistrate's office. I can't move openly. You can.
quest_complete: Every piece fits. This closes the case. Well done — you have a detective's mind.
The evidence is conclusive. Justice will be served. Here is the bounty.
combat_warning: I've solved a hundred cases. You're not going to be the one that gets away.
Resisting arrest only adds charges. And I never forget a face.
idle: Every crime tells a story. My job is to read it.
The guilty always make one mistake. I just have to find it.
Four open cases. Three suspects. Two alibis that don't hold up.

greeting: Huh? Oh, yeah. Welcome to town or whatever.
You need something? Because I'm on my break. Technically.
Just... don't cause any problems where I can see them, alright?
farewell: Yeah, take care. I'll be here. Or asleep. Same thing.
Cool. Good talk. I'm going back to my chair.
Try not to get into trouble. Paperwork is the worst.
quest_offer: Look, I was supposed to patrol the docks tonight, but there's a card game at the tavern. Five gold and you patrol for me.
Someone keeps stealing from the baker. I should investigate but... could you just handle it?
quest_complete: Oh, nice. Problem solved and I didn't have to move. Perfect system.
Great work. I'll put your name in the report. Well, maybe.
combat_warning: Ugh, really? Fine. I guess I have to do my job now.
Can't we just... not? Fighting is so much effort.
idle: This post is perfect. Nothing ever happens here.
I applied for three different transfers. All got lost. Funny, that.
The captain thinks I'm on patrol. The chair thinks otherwise.

greeting: H-halt! I mean — welcome! Please state your... um... purpose.
Oh! You startled me. I'm the guard. I'm guarding. Obviously.
Good day! Please don't make any sudden moves. Captain's orders.
farewell: Have a s-safe trip! And stay on the road, please, it's much safer!
Okay, g-goodbye! You seem nice!
Right then! Carry on! I'll be here. Guarding.
quest_offer: There's... there's something in the old warehouse. I heard it. I went to check and then I — I didn't check. Could you check?
I was supposed to patrol the east gate last night but I didn't because, well... I'll pay you to do it. Captain doesn't have to know.
quest_complete: Oh thank the gods! I'll tell the captain! Well, maybe I'll let you tell the captain.
You actually did it! You're incredible! I want to be you when I grow up!
combat_warning: P-please stop! I have a spear! It's quite sharp!
I'm going to blow this whistle! I'm doing it! I'm blowing it right now!
idle: Did that shadow move? That shadow moved.
The captain says there's nothing to be afraid of. The captain is very brave.
Only six more hours on shift. Six more hours. I can do this.

greeting: Halt! I mean, welcome! I mean — please state your business! Did I do that right?
Good day, traveler! I'm on my first official patrol! Pretty exciting, right?
Welcome to the town! I just started this week. If you need directions, I have a map!
farewell: Stay safe out there! And remember, the guard is here to help!
Have a great day! I'll be right here if you need anything!
Safe travels! This is the best job ever!
quest_offer: So, um, there's a noise in the old mill that I'm supposed to investigate, but my partner called in sick. Want to come?
I found some suspicious footprints by the south wall. Captain said I could recruit help to investigate!
quest_complete: We did it! Wait until I tell the captain! He's going to be so impressed!
That was incredible! You're amazing! I'm going to write this up in my patrol report!
combat_warning: Stop right there! I have training! Almost two whole weeks of it!
Don't make me use this! I've been practicing all morning!
idle: The captain says I have potential. I think that's good.
I memorized the entire town code last night. All forty-seven sections!
Is it supposed to rain? I forgot my cloak. Don't tell the captain.

greeting: State your business. The city is under my watch.
Welcome, traveler. Mind the laws and we'll have no trouble.
You look capable. Trouble rarely escapes my notice.
farewell: Move along, and stay out of trouble.
The gates close at dusk. Don't be late.
May your blade stay sheathed within these walls.
quest_offer: A smuggler's ring is operating in the docks. Bring me proof, and you'll be rewarded.
We've had reports of monsters near the granary. Investigate and report back.
quest_complete: Well done. The city is safer thanks to you.
You have my respect — and your payment.
combat_warning: Draw steel and you'll answer to the law.
This is your only warning. Stand down.
idle: I've seen twenty winters on this wall.
Peace is earned, not given.
The city sleeps, but I do not.

greeting: You wounded? Sit down and shut up. I'll get to you in order.
Another casualty. Bite this stick, I'm going in.
Name, rank, and where it hurts. In that order.
farewell: Don't come back. That's a compliment — means you stayed alive.
Change those bandages daily. I mean it.
Get back out there. Your squad needs you.
quest_offer: My supply wagon was hit by a mortar. I need medical kits from the forward cache before more soldiers die.
There's a field hospital behind enemy lines with our wounded. I need someone to get them out.
quest_complete: Good. With these supplies, I can save twenty more. Maybe thirty. You did well.
You brought them home alive. That's all that matters.
combat_warning: I've patched up soldiers who are harder than you. And I carry a bone saw. Think about it.
I save lives. But I know exactly where to cut to end one.
idle: I've lost count of the stitches I've sewn. Thousands. Maybe more.
War is hell on the body. But the mind takes longer to heal.
Clean water, clean bandages, clean hands. Three rules that save more lives than any spell.

greeting: The light reveals your suffering. You have come to the right place.
I am a vessel of the divine. Through me, the gods mend what is broken.
Kneel and be healed. The gods' mercy is boundless.
farewell: Go forth renewed. The light walks with you.
Your healing is a gift. Use your restored strength wisely.
May the divine grace sustain you in the trials ahead.
quest_offer: A shrine in the mountains has been desecrated. I cannot heal properly while the sacred ground bleeds. Restore it.
A fellow healer has been corrupted by dark magic. Free her spirit, and the temple will reward you.
quest_complete: The divine light burns brighter for your deeds. Receive this blessing.
The sacred order is restored. You carry the gods' favor now.
combat_warning: The light protects its servants. Strike me and learn that lesson painfully.
I channel the power of gods. Your mortal steel is meaningless.
idle: Faith is not belief without evidence. It is trust beyond understanding.
I have healed plague, poison, and curse. But grief is the hardest wound to close.
The gods do not choose who deserves healing. Neither do I.

greeting: What's troubling you, dear? I've got a remedy for just about everything.
Come in, come in. Mind the drying herbs on the ceiling.
Let me guess — sore throat? Bad back? Cursed by a fairy? I can fix all three.
farewell: Take the tea blend. Steep it for exactly four minutes. Not three, not five.
Rest well tonight. The remedy works best during sleep.
Come back if the rash spreads. And stay away from the nightshade.
quest_offer: The valley below has wild comfrey that I desperately need. Problem is, bears have moved in.
My mentor left a recipe in her old cottage. It's been abandoned for years and something's moved in.
quest_complete: Oh, perfect specimens! These will make a wonderful batch. Here, take some salve for your trouble.
You've done me a great kindness. My shelves will be full again.
combat_warning: I can cure a cold and cause one. Don't make me choose the latter.
This is a place of healing. Violence is a sickness I can't fix.
idle: Modern healers rely too much on magic. A good herb does the job just as well.
I've treated three generations of this village. I'm not stopping now.
The elderberry harvest is late this year. That worries me.

greeting: Do not remove your gloves. Do not touch your face. Sit there. Not there. There.
You're either sick or curious. I treat both conditions.
Symptoms? Duration? Contact with corpses? These are not casual questions.
farewell: Burn your clothes when you get home. Precautionary measure.
Quarantine yourself for three days. If you're still alive, you're fine.
I'll note your visit in my records. For posterity. Or an autopsy.
quest_offer: The plague originated somewhere upstream. I need water samples from every tributary. Take these sealed vials.
A mortician in the next town has been disposing of bodies improperly. The contagion will spread. Stop him.
quest_complete: Excellent data. This narrows the source considerably. Lives will be saved because of this.
You've contained the outbreak. The mortality rate just dropped. That is your reward.
combat_warning: I carry seventeen diseases in sealed vials. One wrong move and we all have problems.
I dissect things for a living. You're just a larger specimen.
idle: Death teaches more than any textbook. I study both.
The mask is not for my protection. It's for yours.
I've cataloged forty-two strains of plague. Forty-three, if this new one counts.

greeting: Room or drink?
I run a quiet place. You look like trouble. Are you trouble?
Three gold for a room. Two for a meal. Pay now.
farewell: Check out by morning.
Don't break anything.
I'll remember your face.
quest_offer: A man left a chest in room four three weeks ago. Never came back. If you can find out what happened, it's yours to keep.
My stable boy ran off. I need someone to track him down before the horses go unfed.
quest_complete: Fine work. Don't make it a habit — I don't like owing favors.
Said you could do it. You did it. That's that.
combat_warning: I'll have you in chains before you hit the floor.
Last man who tried that lost three teeth. He left with two.
idle: World's going to ruin. It was better before.
Ale prices up again. Everything's up.
Quiet night. Good night.

greeting: Welcome, friend! First drink's on the house if you can out-sing me!
Step in, step in! The fire's warm and the ale's cold.
A new face! Pull up a chair and tell me your story.
farewell: Come back soon! The stew only gets better overnight.
Safe travels, and may your hangover be mild!
Don't forget your cloak! Nights get chilly.
quest_offer: A bard left behind a strange instrument. Find him, and I'll reward you.
There's a rat problem in the cellar. Not the normal kind — these ones bite back.
quest_complete: You did it! Drinks for everyone!
My thanks! The inn is safer — and so is my pantry.
combat_warning: No brawling in my inn! Take it outside!
I've broken up bigger fights than this. Don't test me.
idle: Best tales are told over a mug of ale.
Travelers bring news — and trouble.
I know every secret in this town. Or nearly every one.

greeting: Oh, you poor thing! You look half-starved! Sit down, I'll bring you soup immediately.
Welcome, welcome, dear! Have you eaten? Of course you haven't. Let me fix that.
Come in out of the cold! I have blankets, broth, and bread — and no arguments!
farewell: Bundle up! And eat something before you go. I packed extra.
Be careful out there, love. The world is harsh, but you're tougher than you think.
Come back soon! I'll have your favorite stew ready!
quest_offer: A young woman who works here has gone missing. She's like a daughter to me. Please find her.
Wolves have been circling the henhouse. I need those chickens — they feed the whole neighborhood.
quest_complete: Oh, bless your heart! You're a hero! Come here and let me hug you!
You wonderful, wonderful soul! Free meals for life — I mean it!
combat_warning: Don't you dare bring violence into my inn! I'll tan your hide!
I raised four children and buried two husbands. You don't scare me one bit.
idle: Everyone needs a warm meal and a kind word. That's what I provide.
The adventurers who pass through here always look so tired. And so young.
I've been running this inn since before you were born. It's home.

greeting: Hah! You've got that look — the adventurer's itch! Come in, drinks are on me the first round.
Welcome, welcome. Sit anywhere. I used to be like you, you know.
Another wanderer! I love this job.
farewell: Keep your shield high and your wits sharper. Go get 'em.
Come back and tell me the story. I love a good story.
Safe roads and lucky dice!
quest_offer: There's an old dungeon I mapped twenty years ago. I never cleared the lower level. It's yours if you want it.
A young soldier came through here badly wounded — said her company was ambushed. They might still need help.
quest_complete: Ha! I knew it! That's exactly what I would have done! Drinks are free tonight!
I'm proud of you. Really. Let me tell you about the time I did something similar...
combat_warning: I've killed a dragon. A dragon! You really want to do this?
This arm is scarred. So is my patience. Final warning.
idle: I miss the open road sometimes. But my knees don't.
Best thing about retiring: you stop worrying about dying. Sort of.
I've got stories, friend. All you have to do is ask.

greeting: Welcome! You've arrived just in time for tonight's tale! I was about to begin!
Ah, a new listener! Pull up a chair. The ale is cold and the stories are hot.
Every guest who walks through that door has a story. What's yours?
farewell: May your adventures be worthy of a tale!
Come back with a story that tops mine. I dare you!
The tale continues tomorrow night. Don't miss it.
quest_offer: I've been telling the legend of the Ghost of Barrow Hill for years. But lately, guests have been seeing it for real. Investigate, and I'll make you part of the story.
A traveling bard challenged me to a story contest. I need true tales from the road. Bring me one.
quest_complete: Magnificent! I couldn't have written a better ending! This will be told for generations!
What a tale! I'm adding this to my repertoire. With your permission, of course!
combat_warning: I've told stories about men who died in bar fights. Don't become one of them.
This inn has survived three wars. You won't be the one to wreck it.
idle: The best stories are the ones that happen right here. Under this roof.
I've been innkeeping for forty years. Every night is a new chapter.
A warm fire, cold ale, and a good story. That's all anyone really needs.

greeting: New face. I notice new faces. What's your business here?
Room's four gold. Upfront. No guests after midnight. No exceptions.
I've seen a lot of travelers. You don't look like the honest type. Prove me wrong.
farewell: Lock your door tonight. I keep a clean inn, but I don't control the guests.
Check-out is at dawn. Late fees apply.
Don't cause trouble and we'll get along fine.
quest_offer: One of my guests hasn't come out of their room in three days. I need someone to check. I'm not doing it.
Someone's been sneaking into the wine cellar at night. I want them caught. And punished.
quest_complete: About time. Problem solved, and my reputation intact. Here's your payment.
Good. I don't like mysteries in my inn. They're bad for business.
combat_warning: This bar is reinforced oak. Your head is not. Settle down.
I've thrown out men twice your size. Don't test me.
idle: Trust nobody. Especially the friendly ones.
Last week, a guest tried to skip the bill. I found him. I always find them.
The inn sees everything. And so do I.

greeting: Oh, you look exhausted! Come in, sit down. Stew's still hot!
Welcome to the Crossroads Inn! I'm Marta — and I run a clean establishment.
Come in, come in! Is it just you, or do you have companions?
farewell: Sleep well, dear. Breakfast is at dawn!
You're welcome back any time — I'll keep your usual room free.
Safe travels! Don't forget to eat!
quest_offer: Between you and me... there's been something strange in the cellar at night. My husband won't go down there anymore.
A group of travelers left without paying last week. They had red cloaks — you haven't seen them, have you?
quest_complete: Oh, you are a treasure! Free meals for a week, I insist!
My husband is so relieved. We all are. Thank you, truly.
combat_warning: Out! Out of my inn right now or I'll brain you with this skillet!
I've dealt with worse than you, friend. Walk away.
idle: A merchant passed through yesterday — said the eastern roads are full of soldiers.
Did you hear about the miller's son? Joined the army, just like that.
The nights are getting longer, I swear. Something's not right.

greeting: State your grievance clearly and keep to the facts.
Order begins with testimony, not shouting.
farewell: Bring evidence next time, not rumors.
Justice is slow because haste makes tyrants.
quest_offer: A witness vanished before the hearing. Find them and I can close the case cleanly.
quest_complete: The witness returned. The verdict can stand without shame now.
combat_warning: By civic authority, stand down at once.
You are making a very expensive mistake.
idle: Most feuds are just unpaid debts wearing armor.
A city falls apart one ignored crime at a time.

greeting: Welcome! Please, sit down. Can I get you anything? Water, bread?
A new face in town! How wonderful. How can I help you?
Come in, come in! My door is always open to the people.
farewell: Thank you for visiting. Please, don't hesitate to return.
Take care of yourself. The town needs good people like you.
Go well, friend. And let me know if anyone needs help.
quest_offer: The refugees from the eastern villages need shelter and food. Will you help me organize the relief effort?
The old bridge collapsed and the southern farms are cut off. I need someone to oversee repairs.
quest_complete: You've done so much good today. The people won't forget this.
This is exactly what our community needed. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
combat_warning: Please! There's no need for violence. Let us talk!
I won't fight you, but the townspeople will defend their home.
idle: Every person in this town matters. Every single one.
There's always more to do, but we're making progress.
I took this office to serve, not to rule.

greeting: Ah, another visitor. I do hope you've brought something to sweeten the conversation.
Welcome to my office. Everything here has a price, including my attention.
You look resourceful. I like resourceful people.
farewell: Remember who your friends are in this town.
Our arrangement stays between us, naturally.
The door is always open to those who can afford it.
quest_offer: A political rival is becoming inconvenient. I need evidence of his... indiscretions. Real or otherwise.
The tax collector has been keeping a second ledger. Retrieve it before the auditors arrive.
quest_complete: Splendid. You've earned my gratitude, which is worth more than gold in this town.
Efficient work. Your payment has been arranged through the usual channels.
combat_warning: Kill me and this town burns. I've arranged for that.
I have allies you cannot imagine. Stand down.
idle: Governing is merely business conducted with a nicer title.
The treasury is healthy. My treasury, at least.
Trust is a currency. I spend it wisely.

greeting: Who sent you? Was it the council? Speak carefully.
Close the door. Lock it. Now — what do you want?
Another stranger. Wonderful. Are you armed? Of course you're armed.
farewell: Tell no one we spoke. I mean it.
Leave separately from how you came. Use the back entrance.
If anyone asks, you were never here.
quest_offer: Someone in my inner circle is a spy. I need you to find out who before they find out I know.
My food taster fell ill. Coincidence? I think not. Investigate the kitchen staff.
quest_complete: Good. Good. One less threat. But there are always more.
You've proven yourself trustworthy. For now.
combat_warning: I knew it! I knew you'd betray me! Guards!
Every room in this building is trapped. Think carefully.
idle: The walls have ears. I've checked. Twice.
Three assassination attempts this year. They're getting creative.
I sleep in a different room every night. Can't be too careful.

greeting: Welcome, citizen. I trust you have business here.
State your purpose. My time is valuable.
You stand in the mayor's office. Speak plainly.
farewell: Serve the town well.
You are dismissed.
Do not waste my time again unless the matter is urgent.
quest_offer: I have a task suited for someone of your... talents.
There is a matter of civic importance that requires discretion.
quest_complete: The town owes you a debt. Here is your reward.
Well done. I'll remember this service.
combat_warning: Guards! To me!
You dare threaten an official?!
idle: Budget reviews never end...
The council meets tonight about the northern threat.
Order is the foundation of prosperity.

greeting: You need armor. I can see it in the dents you're already carrying.
Protection's what I sell. Can't adventure if you're dead.
Good steel between you and the enemy. That's what I offer.
farewell: Keep that armor maintained. Oil the joints, patch the leather.
Come back when you can afford the good stuff.
Don't get killed. Bad for my reputation.
quest_offer: My supply of drake-scale has dried up since the mine went dark. Clear the mine and I'll forge you something special.
A rival armorer is selling counterfeit gear stamped with my mark. Bring me proof and I'll outfit you for free.
quest_complete: Solid work. I'll have your custom piece ready in three days.
You've earned the best I can make. Come pick it up when it's done.
combat_warning: I test every piece of armor I sell. On myself. I've been hit by worse than you.
I'm wearing my own product. Let's see if yours holds up.
idle: Chainmail's underrated. Stops more blades than plate at half the weight.
Armor tells you about its wearer. Yours tells me you take risks.
I outfitted the duke's personal guard. Every one of them survived the siege.

greeting: You weren't followed, were you? Good. What do you need?
I don't know your name. You don't know mine. Let's keep it that way.
Someone sent you. Who?
farewell: This conversation never happened.
Don't come back the same way you came.
Forget you were here.
quest_offer: There's a package that needs moving. Discreetly. You look like the discreet type.
Someone has something that belongs to me. I need it back. No questions asked.
quest_complete: You're better than I expected. Perhaps we'll work together again.
Good. The coin is there. Walk away and don't look back.
combat_warning: You really don't want to do this. I have friends in dark places.
Last chance to reconsider.
idle: Information is worth more than gold if you know who to sell it to.
Everyone has a price. It's just a matter of finding it.
Trust no one — including me.

greeting: Mind the stacks! Some of these books bite. Literally, in one case.
A reader! Wonderful! Or are you just sheltering from the rain? Either way, welcome.
Careful with that shelf — the third book from the left occasionally whispers.
farewell: Read with care. Some knowledge changes you.
Return that book in the condition you found it. Or better.
Happy reading! And if the pages start glowing, bring it back immediately.
quest_offer: A book was borrowed from my shop three decades ago. The borrower is now a lich. I still want it returned.
I've received word of a private library being auctioned. I need someone to attend and acquire a specific volume. Discreetly.
quest_complete: At last! This edition has been out of print for two centuries! You have my undying gratitude!
Extraordinary find! This fills a gap in my collection I'd given up on. Here's your payment, and a bookmark.
combat_warning: This shop contains books worth more than your life. Damage one and I'll end you.
Several of these tomes have guardian enchantments. I suggest you behave.
idle: I've read nine thousand books. I remember every one.
The written word outlasts empires. That's why I sell books, not swords.
Someone once asked if I had a book about everything. I said 'almost.'

greeting: Welcome, welcome! I carry wonders from lands you've only dreamed of!
Ah, a soul with refined taste! I can tell. Come, see what the caravans have brought.
From the burning deserts to the frozen peaks — I have treasures from everywhere.
farewell: May the winds carry you safely. And carry you back to my shop!
Travel well. I shall have new marvels upon your return.
Remember — the rarest things find those who deserve them.
quest_offer: My caravan was ambushed in the mountain pass. The silk survived, but they took the spice chest. It's irreplaceable.
A collector in the capital has an ancient compass I need. He won't sell. Perhaps you can... persuade him.
quest_complete: Magnificent! You have my eternal gratitude and a generous discount!
I knew fortune would smile upon this arrangement! Your reward, as promised.
combat_warning: I have traded with warlords and survived. You are no warlord.
My bodyguards are paid very, very well. Reconsider.
idle: In the east, they say the desert teaches patience. And thirst.
Every item in my shop has a story. Some are worth more than the item itself.
The trade routes grow more dangerous each season. But the profits grow with them.

greeting: Step right up, friend! Best prices in the Reach!
Welcome, welcome! See anything you like?
Ah, a customer! My lucky day!
farewell: Come back soon, friend — I'll have new stock!
Safe travels, and spend wisely!
May the road be kind to you!
quest_offer: Actually, I could use someone with your skills. Bandits hit my last supply run...
There's a job if you want it — good pay, minimal risk. Well, minimal-ish.
quest_complete: Ha! I knew you were the right person for the job!
Excellent work. Here's your coin, and I'll remember this.
combat_warning: Guards! Guards! Help!
I want no trouble — take what you want and go!
idle: Supply chains aren't what they used to be...
Did you hear? The north road is plagued by wolves.
Business is slow since the troubles started.

greeting: Welcome to my garden! Everything here was grown with love and a little magic.
Oh, hello! Looking for herbs, remedies, or perhaps a nice tea?
Come in, the lavender is fresh today! What can I help you with?
farewell: Take care! And remember, chamomile for sleep, ginger for courage!
Happy travels! May the herbs keep you healthy.
Come back in spring — the moonpetal blooms will be spectacular.
quest_offer: The blight has killed my elder oak, and without it, half my remedies are impossible. I need a cutting from its twin in the Verdant Hollow.
A rare nightbloom grows only in the caves east of town. I'll pay well for a live specimen.
quest_complete: Oh, it's beautiful! With this, I can resume my work. Thank you so much!
You've saved my garden and my livelihood. Please, take these healing salves.
combat_warning: I specialize in poisons as well as remedies. Don't make me demonstrate.
These flowers are more dangerous than they look. So am I.
idle: Every weed is just a plant whose virtues haven't been discovered yet.
I talk to the plants. Some people think I'm strange. The plants don't.
Rain tomorrow. Perfect for the ferns.

greeting: Welcome to my atelier. I deal only in the finest gems and metals.
Ah, you have an eye for beauty. I can tell. Please, browse at your leisure.
Every piece here is one of a kind. As are my prices.
farewell: May your treasure shine as brightly as your fortune.
Return when you've earned enough for my finest work.
A pleasure. Quality recognizes quality.
quest_offer: A flawless sapphire was stolen from my workshop. It was destined for the duchess. I need it back — discreetly.
There's a gem mine in the hills that's been overrun by kobolds. Clear it, and I'll give you first pick of the haul.
quest_complete: Exquisite work. You've saved my reputation and my finest commission.
Perfect. You've earned a gem from my personal collection.
combat_warning: These display cases are warded with explosive runes. Step away.
I arm my shop with the same care I craft my jewels. Carefully and lethally.
idle: A gem's worth is in its cut, not its size. Most people get that wrong.
I supplied the crown jewels for two different kingdoms. Neither knows about the other.
Craftsmanship is patience made visible.

greeting: Oh! A new face! Welcome, welcome, welcome!
Hello, dear! Are you in the market for potions, salves, or something more... exotic?
Don't be shy! Come in, come in!
farewell: Do take care! Adventures can be so dangerous!
Remember: two potions are always better than one!
Bye-bye! Come back before you die!
quest_offer: I need rare herbs from the Thornwood — my supplier was eaten. Nasty business. Could you fetch them?
A rival alchemist has been undercutting my prices with inferior product. I'd like proof she's cheating.
quest_complete: Oh wonderful, wonderful! You are simply the best adventurer I've ever met!
Marvellous! I'll brew you something special as a bonus!
combat_warning: I have seventeen unstable potions on my belt! Think very carefully!
Don't make me turn this shop into a crater!
idle: The mushrooms this season have been simply extraordinary!
Did you know a drop of basilisk blood can cure three diseases? Or cause five. It's a fine line.
I once made a potion that turned a man into a tree for a week. He said it was peaceful.

greeting: Psst. Over here. You look like someone who appreciates a bargain.
No questions asked, no receipts given. What do you need?
Keep your voice down. I've got things the regular shops don't carry.
farewell: You didn't see me. We never met. Good doing business.
Come back after dark. I'll have the good stuff then.
Walk away casual-like. Don't draw attention.
quest_offer: A shipment fell off the back of a wagon. I need someone to pick it up before the guard finds it.
My competition is undercutting me. Literally — they stole my supply. Get it back and you keep a cut.
quest_complete: Beautiful. Clean job. Here's your share. Spend it somewhere they don't ask questions.
That's what I like — reliable help. Here, take a bonus.
combat_warning: I know people who know people. You don't want that kind of attention.
One shout and every cutthroat in this alley comes running. Your call.
idle: Supply and demand, friend. I supply, you demand, everybody wins.
Legal, illegal — just words. Profit is the only language that matters.
The guard changes shifts at midnight. That's when business really picks up.

greeting: Fresh wares from three towns over! Take a look, you won't regret it.
Ah, a discerning customer! My favorite kind.
Step lively, friend — I don't stay in one place long.
farewell: If you need me, follow the road dust!
Safe travels! May your coin purse stay full.
Next time, I'll have something even rarer.
quest_offer: Lost a shipment to goblins near the crossroads. Fetch it, and I'll make it worth your while.
Heard rumors of a rare gem in the old ruins. Bring it to me, and I'll pay handsomely.
quest_complete: You did it! My business is saved — and so is your reputation.
Excellent! Here's your reward, as promised.
combat_warning: Easy now! I have friends in every town.
You don't want trouble with a trader — we know things.
idle: Roads are getting rougher these days.
A clever merchant always has an escape plan.
Heard any good rumors? I pay for information.

greeting: Need a blade? You've come to the right place.
I sell the finest steel in three counties. Don't let the price scare you.
What'll it be?
farewell: Keep your weapon clean and it'll serve you well.
Don't come back crying if you get yourself killed.
Good luck out there.
quest_offer: I need someone to recover a sword I sold. Turns out the buyer had sticky fingers.
There's ore in the old mine — someone cleared it last month but never delivered.
quest_complete: Good. Clean work. Here's your pay.
I don't hand out compliments, but that wasn't bad.
combat_warning: That was your last mistake.
I forged these blades — I know exactly how to use them.
idle: Steel doesn't lie. People do.
A dull blade is more dangerous than none.
I've armed kings and peasants alike. They all bleed the same.

greeting: If you came for easy silver, wrong tunnel.
Dust means work. Silence means cave-ins.
farewell: Mind the weak beams.
If the earth groans, run.
quest_offer: A foreman vanished in the lower shaft. Bring back his ledger or his bones.
quest_complete: Ledger is worth more than the ore it cost us. Good work.
combat_warning: Back from the seam, now.
Swing that blade near my lantern and I bury you myself.
idle: Stone tells you where to strike if you listen.
A rich vein breeds poor tempers.

greeting: Hmm? Oh. Another commoner. What do you want? Make it amusing, at least.
I'm in the middle of a wine tasting. This had better be important.
Welcome to my estate. Don't touch anything. Everything here costs more than you.
farewell: Do see yourself out. The servants will show you the door.
Terribly dull, this. Perhaps next time bring gossip.
I have a party to attend. You are not invited.
quest_offer: I've wagered my rival that I can acquire a griffin's feather within the week. Fetch one and I'll pay triple its worth.
My latest soiree requires entertainment of the exotic variety. Capture a live pixie. I'll provide the cage.
quest_complete: Oh, splendid! This will be the talk of the season! Your payment, as agreed.
Adequate. I suppose commoners have their uses after all.
combat_warning: Do you know how much this coat cost? Attack me and you'll owe me a fortune.
Guards! GUARDS! How dare you threaten nobility!
idle: I'm bored. Monumentally, catastrophically bored.
The vintage this year is passable. Last year's was divine.
If I have to attend one more council meeting, I shall simply expire.

greeting: Ah, you've arrived. I've been expecting someone with your particular skills.
Welcome. Please, have a seat. Wine? No? Wise — one should keep a clear head in my company.
I appreciate directness, so I'll be direct: I need an outsider I can trust. For now.
farewell: Our arrangement remains between us. I trust that's understood.
Play your part well, and you'll rise with me.
Politics is a game of patience. I have plenty.
quest_offer: The duke's advisor carries a sealed letter that would change the succession. I need a copy before it reaches the capital.
My cousin plans to claim my estate through a forged will. I need the original document to prove his fraud.
quest_complete: Masterfully done. You've moved a piece on a board most people don't even see.
The game shifts in my favor. Your reward reflects my appreciation.
combat_warning: Kill me and my contingencies activate. Fourteen letters sent, seven allies notified. You lose either way.
I've outmaneuvered assassins sent by kings. Your threats amuse me.
idle: Every conversation is a negotiation. Every friendship is an alliance. Every silence is a strategy.
The throne is a chair. What matters is who controls the person sitting in it.
I've been planning this for three years. One more month changes nothing.

greeting: I am Dame Katriel of House Stormshield. I judge people by their deeds, not their birth.
You carry yourself like a fighter. Good. I have little patience for courtiers.
Speak plainly. I prefer honesty to flattery.
farewell: Fight with honor. There is no other way.
May your courage hold when your strength fails.
The realm needs more swords and fewer politicians. Go do your part.
quest_offer: An orc warband threatens the northern villages. I'm assembling a strike force. Will you join?
My squire was ambushed on the king's road. I need someone to secure the route while I tend to his wounds.
quest_complete: You've fought bravely and well. House Stormshield recognizes your valor.
The realm is safer for your actions. This is the highest praise I give.
combat_warning: I've won tournaments and battles alike. You will not be my first defeat.
Draw your weapon if you must. But know that I welcome the challenge.
idle: A noble's duty is to protect those who cannot protect themselves.
I earned my title on the battlefield. Not in a throne room.
The court whispers behind my back. Let them. My sword speaks louder.

greeting: Blessings, child. The temple always welcomes generous souls.
Ah, an adventurer. The gods favor the bold — and those who tithe.
Come in, come in. Salvation and indulgences are both available today.
farewell: May your purse remain full and your conscience light.
Remember the temple in your travels. We accept donations by courier.
Go with the gods. And perhaps leave a little something at the altar?
quest_offer: A rogue priest is preaching in the commons, undermining my authority. Silence him, and the temple will compensate you.
Temple funds have been... redirected. I need someone to retrieve them from the courier before the audit.
quest_complete: Excellent. Your service to the temple will be noted in the divine records. Eventually.
Well handled. Here is your payment. We never spoke, of course.
combat_warning: Do you realize what excommunication means? Your soul, forfeit. Forever.
The temple guard will be here momentarily. Reconsider.
idle: The faithful give and the temple provides. A simple arrangement.
Power and piety are not mutually exclusive. I am proof of that.
The High Priest's chair will be vacant soon. I have plans.

greeting: You carry pain. I can see it. Let me help.
Welcome to the hospice. All who suffer are welcome here.
The gods sent you here for a reason. Sit, and let us tend to you.
farewell: Healing takes time. Be patient with yourself.
May your wounds close and your spirit mend.
Return if the pain persists. I will always be here.
quest_offer: The plague ward is overflowing and my herbs are spent. I need moonpetal from the high meadow.
A healer in the next village has a remedy I cannot replicate. Will you carry my letter and return with her answer?
quest_complete: Bless you. With these supplies, I can save a dozen more.
You have given hope to the hopeless. That is the greatest gift.
combat_warning: I will not raise a hand against you. But the sick I protect will not forgive you.
There are dying children in these beds. Sheathe your weapon.
idle: I have not slept in three days. There are always more patients.
Every life saved is a prayer answered.
The plague teaches humility. We are all fragile.

greeting: State your allegiance. Quickly.
You stand before a Templar of the Burning Shield. Speak with respect.
Weapons drawn or sheathed? Choose now.
farewell: Fight well. Die well. The gods judge both.
Keep your blade sharp and your faith sharper.
The enemy does not rest. Neither should you.
quest_offer: A nest of undead festers in the catacombs. I need warriors, not priests, to burn them out.
The heretic encampment grows bolder. Join my strike force and we end this tonight.
quest_complete: You fight with conviction. The order could use soldiers like you.
Victory. But do not grow complacent. Evil regroups quickly.
combat_warning: I have purged worse than you in the name of the divine.
My mace has shattered the bones of demons. Yours will be no different.
idle: Prayer and combat are the same discipline. Both require focus.
I was not ordained to be gentle. I was ordained to protect.
The darkness advances because good people hesitate. I do not hesitate.

greeting: Welcome to the archive. Mind the manuscripts; some are older than the kingdom.
Ah, a visitor. I hope you appreciate the sacred texts as much as I do.
Enter quietly, please. Knowledge demands reverence.
farewell: May the wisdom of the ancients guide you.
Return if you seek knowledge. The archive is always open to the earnest.
Go carefully. The truths you carry are more dangerous than any blade.
quest_offer: A forbidden text was stolen from the restricted section. It contains rituals that must not reach the wrong hands.
Our scribes have found inconsistencies in the holy canon. I need original sources from the monastery ruins.
quest_complete: Remarkable. This text fills a gap we have puzzled over for decades.
You have done the faith a greater service than you know.
combat_warning: These shelves contain the accumulated wisdom of centuries. Damage them and face divine judgment.
I know seventeen wards of protection. You will not enjoy a single one.
idle: The old prayers contain hidden meanings. I have spent thirty years decoding them.
Theology and scholarship are the same pursuit viewed from different angles.
Every contradiction in the holy texts is a lesson waiting to be understood.

greeting: The light sees all, traveler. It sees you now.
Have you come to confess, or to serve the divine purpose?
The faithful are always welcome. The faithless... are given one chance.
farewell: Walk in the light, or be consumed by it.
Pray tonight. The gods are listening.
The righteous path is narrow. Do not stray.
quest_offer: Heretics gather in the old chapel. They must be purged before their corruption spreads.
A cursed artifact was smuggled into town. Find it and bring it to me for sanctification.
quest_complete: The divine will has been enacted through your hands. You are blessed.
Another victory for the faithful. The gods reward those who serve.
combat_warning: The light protects me. Your steel cannot.
Strike a priest and face the wrath of heaven itself.
idle: Doubt is the first sin. Certainty is the first virtue.
The temples grow empty while darkness grows bold. This cannot continue.
I have burned heresy from this town before. I will do so again if needed.

greeting: State your need and your rank.
Supplies are counted twice and handed out once.
farewell: Return what you borrow or pay for it in bruises.
A dry ration today is better than starvation tomorrow.
quest_offer: A wagon of rations missed the fort. Recover it before the line breaks.
quest_complete: Crates accounted for. That keeps the wall standing another week.
combat_warning: Save the heroics for the ledger after we survive.
Hold the line and keep formation.
idle: Wars are lost by empty cupboards before they are lost by empty scabbards.
A proper inventory is a kind of prophecy.

greeting: You any good in a fight? I need fighters, not talkers.
I've got bounties. You want gold. Simple arrangement.
Don't waste my time. Are you interested in work or not?
farewell: Bring them back alive if you can. Dead if you must.
Get moving. Targets don't catch themselves.
I'll be at the tavern when you're done.
quest_offer: There's a bandit captain with a fifty gold bounty on his head. Last seen in the Thornwood. Interested?
A fugitive mage escaped the prison transport. Dangerous. The bounty reflects that.
quest_complete: Target confirmed. Bounty paid. Clean work.
Dead or alive, the contract is fulfilled. Here's your gold.
combat_warning: I've brought in worse than you. Don't bother running.
Twenty years of bounty hunting. You're not my toughest catch.
idle: Everyone runs. Nobody runs forever.
I don't judge the bounty. I collect it.
The best bounty hunters are patient. The second best are dead.

greeting: Please, you have to help me! No one else will listen!
Are you an adventurer? Thank the gods! I've been searching for days!
I can pay! Not much, but everything I have! Please, hear me out!
farewell: Thank you, thank you! I'll be praying for your success every moment!
Please hurry. I don't know how much time we have left.
You're our only hope. I mean that.
quest_offer: My brother went into the old mine three days ago and hasn't returned. The guard won't go in. Will you?
Our farm is being poisoned. The crops are dying, the animals are sick. Someone is doing this deliberately. I need proof.
quest_complete: You did it! You actually did it! I can't believe — thank you! Thank you so much!
My family is safe because of you. We'll never forget this. Never.
combat_warning: Stay away from me! I'll scream! I'll bring the whole town running!
I have nothing worth stealing! Leave me alone!
idle: I can't sleep anymore. Not until this is resolved.
Nobody takes a farmer seriously. But our problems are real.
If only I were stronger. If only I could do this myself.

greeting: I have prayed for capable souls to arrive. Perhaps the gods have answered.
You come at a desperate hour, traveler. Will you hear an old woman's plea?
Welcome. I am Elder Maren. Our village is in need.
farewell: The village blesses you for your efforts.
Return safely. We will be waiting.
May wisdom guide your steps.
quest_offer: Our children have been going missing at night for three weeks now. The guard says it's wolves. I know it is not wolves.
An ancient evil stirs beneath the hill. I have felt it for years. It is waking. Someone must stop it before it's too late.
quest_complete: You have saved us. Words cannot express my gratitude.
The village will tell stories of you for generations. This I promise.
combat_warning: I am too old to fight — but not too old to call for those who will.
You would threaten a village elder? You are less than I thought.
idle: The old ways are being forgotten. That worries me more than the monsters.
I remember when the forests were safe. I remember many things.
Power is not strength, and strength is not courage. Remember that.

greeting: You're registered with the guild? Good. I've got work.
Welcome to the Ironpost Guild Hall. Contracts are posted on the board — or I can brief you personally.
Ah, reputation precedes you. Sit down, we should talk.
farewell: Contract closed. Your record has been updated.
Good work. The board has more jobs whenever you're ready.
Stay sharp out there.
quest_offer: High-priority contract just came in. Missing caravan, three days east. Guild wants it handled before the client gets nervous.
Dungeon survey — scouting only, no engagement. Simple job, good pay. You interested?
quest_complete: Contract fulfilled. Bounty transferred. Your guild rank has been updated.
Clean execution. The client is satisfied, which means I'm satisfied. Here's your cut.
combat_warning: This is a Guild Hall. There are fourteen armed adventurers within earshot. Rethink your life choices.
Guild Regulation 7: assaulting a Guild Master is punishable by permanent expulsion. And worse.
idle: The Guild doesn't judge your past. Only your results.
I've placed contracts on kings. I've also placed them on rats. Gold is gold.
Information network is the real business. The contracts are just the service.

greeting: Ah, you come recommended. High praise from those I trust. Welcome.
I am Kasimir. My trade empire spans six provinces. And I need your help.
Time is money, and I respect both. Let's talk business.
farewell: A profitable arrangement for us both, I trust.
My agents will be in touch with the details. Expect a courier.
Invest your reward wisely. Good help deserves good returns.
quest_offer: A rival trading house has been sabotaging my caravans. I need evidence to present to the merchant court.
I've acquired a map to a lost dwarven forge. If the weapons there are half as good as legend claims, I'll corner the arms market.
quest_complete: Excellent. This will shift the market in my favor. Your bonus reflects my gratitude.
Well executed. My empire grows, and you've grown richer for it.
combat_warning: I employ the finest mercenaries gold can buy. You've chosen the wrong enemy.
My trade network reaches every kingdom. You'll never find work again.
idle: Wealth is not an end. It is a tool. The greatest tool ever invented.
Every coin I've earned has a story. Most of them are interesting.
The market waits for no one. Neither do I.

greeting: I've been watching you. You have potential. Rare potential.
Do not ask my name. It would mean nothing to you. Yet.
We have little time. Listen carefully.
farewell: You will understand everything when the time is right.
We will meet again. Whether you want to or not.
Trust what you've learned here. Doubt everything else.
quest_offer: There is an artifact hidden in the catacombs that must never be found by the cult. Retrieve it before the new moon.
A seal has been broken that should never have been touched. Find the three keystones before what's behind it wakes fully.
quest_complete: It is done. The balance holds. For now.
You have proven worthy. There will be more trials. But tonight, rest.
combat_warning: You cannot harm what you do not understand.
I have walked between worlds. Your blade means nothing.
idle: The threads of fate converge on this place. Can you feel it?
What approaches cannot be stopped. Only redirected.
There are forces at work that the kingdoms refuse to acknowledge.

greeting: Ah. You must be the adventurers I was told about. Somewhat less... impressive than I had hoped.
I am Lord Edric Castellan. You may address me as 'My Lord.' Now — I have a delicate matter to discuss.
I'll be brief. I need something done that my name cannot be attached to.
farewell: You have been... serviceable. I may have use for you again.
Remember: discretion is the quality I value most. Don't forget that.
Good day. Do not speak of our meeting.
quest_offer: A document has gone missing from my study. Its contents would be... professionally embarrassing. Retrieve it before it reaches the wrong hands.
My son has fallen in with a dangerous cult. I need him extracted. Quietly. Without scandal.
quest_complete: Adequate performance. The agreed payment will be delivered to your lodgings.
I admit, you exceeded my somewhat modest expectations. Well done.
combat_warning: Do you have any idea who I am? My family has ruled this region for two centuries!
Touch me and every guard in this city will have your description by nightfall.
idle: The merchant class grows insufferably bold these days.
Politics is simply chess played with lives. I've always been good at chess.
My family has sacrificed much for this realm. Not that anyone remembers.

greeting: Ah! Someone with a sharp mind, I hope?
