Welcome! Do you enjoy puzzles? I have plenty.
You look like you can handle yourself — and a stack of books.
farewell: Let me know if you find anything curious!
Back to my research, then. Good luck!
May your discoveries be fruitful.
quest_offer: There's a lost tome in the ruins east of here. Retrieve it, and I'll share my findings.
Strange symbols appeared on the town statue overnight. Investigate and report back, would you?
quest_complete: Marvelous! This will advance my research by years.
You have my gratitude — and a small token of appreciation.
combat_warning: I may be a scholar, but I know a few tricks. Don't push me.
Violence is so inefficient. Let's not.
idle: Knowledge is the greatest treasure.
I lose track of time when I'm reading.
Every answer leads to more questions.

greeting: Easy there — my wolf doesn't like sudden movements. Neither do I.
You're in the territory of the pack now. Walk softly.
The hawk on my shoulder has better eyes than both of us. She says you're trustworthy. Mostly.
farewell: The pack will be watching you from the treeline. Don't be alarmed.
My animals will guide you to the path. Follow the hawk.
Travel well. The forest has many eyes, and most of them are mine.
quest_offer: Trappers have been using poisoned bait in the north wood. My animals are dying. Help me find the trappers.
A mother bear has been separated from her cubs by a landslide. I need help clearing the debris.
quest_complete: The pack is safe. That's what matters. You have the forest's gratitude.
You did right by the creatures of this wood. Here — the animals wanted you to have this.
combat_warning: I have a wolf, a hawk, and a bear within calling distance. Choose wisely.
Hurt my animals and I will hunt you to the ends of the earth.
idle: Animals are honest. They don't lie, scheme, or betray. I prefer their company.
My wolf has saved my life eleven times. I've saved hers nine. She's winning.
The bond between ranger and beast is deeper than any oath.

greeting: You're heading into the wilds? Hope you packed more than bravado.
Another greenhorn. Stay close and do what I say.
The woods don't care about your quest. They'll kill you just the same.
farewell: Keep your fire low and your blade close.
North is that way. Probably.
Don't die out there. It's a lot of paperwork for me.
quest_offer: There's a wounded wyvern in the canyon. Wounded animals are the most dangerous kind. Help me put it down.
Something's driven the wolves out of the high forest. Whatever scared them should scare you too. I need a scout.
quest_complete: Good. The wilds are a little safer. For now.
You handled yourself well. Better than most. Here's your share.
combat_warning: I've killed things in these woods that don't have names yet.
My axe has more experience than you do. Stand down.
idle: Thirty years in the wild. Lost count of the scars.
Respect the land and it'll let you live. Disrespect it and it won't.
The old trails are overgrown. People have forgotten what's out there.

greeting: Four people ahead of you on this trail. Two went east. One doubled back. Want to know about the fourth?
You broke three twigs getting here. I heard each one.
I know what you're looking for. The tracks lead south.
farewell: Watch your step. Literally.
I'll find you if you need me. Finding things is what I do.
Stay downwind of whatever you're hunting.
quest_offer: A missing caravan left tracks into the Dead Marshes. Nobody who goes in comes out. I need backup.
I've been tracking a beast for six days. It's smart. Smarter than anything natural. I need a second pair of eyes.
quest_complete: Trail's cold now. Job done. Clean and simple.
Target located and dealt with. That's how tracking works.
combat_warning: I can track a mouse through a blizzard. You can't hide from me.
I've been watching you for the last hour. You never knew I was there.
idle: Everything leaves a trail. Rain, wind, even magic.
The ground tells stories. Most people just don't read the language.
I found a set of tracks yesterday that stopped in the middle of a field. No prints out. Troubling.

greeting: Hi! Are you heading into the forest too? I know every trail within ten miles!
Oh, you look like a real adventurer! I just passed my ranger trials last month!
Welcome to the greenway! I'm Wren — newest ranger of the Second Watch!
farewell: Good luck out there! If you get lost, follow the moss — it grows on the north side!
Stay on the marked trails! Unless you're with me!
See you on the road! The forest is amazing this time of year!
quest_offer: I found tracks near the village that don't match any animal I know. Want to help me investigate?
There's an injured hawk caught in a poacher's net on the cliff. I can't reach it alone.
quest_complete: We did it! I'm going to write about this in my field journal!
That was incredible! You're so experienced! I learned so much!
combat_warning: Hey! Don't make me use these arrows! I've been practicing a LOT!
I'm warning you! My mentor taught me three different takedowns!
idle: Every tree has a story. The oldest ones have the best ones.
I want to map every path in the entire province. It might take a while.
My mentor says patience is a ranger's best tool. I'm still working on that one.

greeting: You lost? Or just bold enough to wander here?
Keep your hand on your purse — or don't. Makes my job easier.
Not many come this way on purpose.
farewell: Maybe we'll meet again. Maybe you won't see me next time.
Stay sharp, traveler. Or don't.
quest_offer: There's a bounty on my head. Help me fake my death, and I'll split the reward.
A rival gang's moving in. Want to make some quick coin?
quest_complete: Didn't think you'd pull it off. Color me impressed.
You work fast. I like that.
combat_warning: You sure you want to do this? I'm faster than I look.
One whistle and the whole crew's on you.
idle: The law's always two steps behind.
Easy money's never easy for long.
I know every shortcut in these woods.

greeting: You found me. That's either impressive or a sign I'm losing my edge.
Names are unnecessary. Speak your business.
I charge by the contract. Consultation is free. This once.
farewell: Forget this meeting. I was never here.
The next time you see me, you won't see me. That's how it works.
Our business is concluded. Leave first.
quest_offer: Someone has put a contract on an innocent. I don't do innocents. Find who ordered it and bring me proof.
A former associate betrayed the guild. I need their location confirmed before I visit them.
quest_complete: Precise work. You understand efficiency. Here is your payment.
The target is confirmed. You've earned your fee. And my silence.
combat_warning: You'll be dead before you finish drawing that blade.
I've killed forty-seven people. You won't make forty-eight. I'll just stop counting.
idle: Everyone dies. I simply accelerate the schedule for some.
A clean kill is an art. Most people don't appreciate art.
I don't hate my targets. Hatred makes you sloppy.

greeting: Welcome to my parlor. I deal in goods of... uncertain provenance. What do you have?
Darling, you look like someone with something to sell. Or buy. I handle both.
No names. No questions. Just business. Those are my terms.
farewell: A pleasure, as always. Use the back exit.
Our transaction is complete. I trust you'll be discreet.
Come back when you have something interesting. I'm always in the market.
quest_offer: A shipment of art was seized by the guard. I need it liberated from the evidence vault before the trial.
One of my suppliers has been arrested. I need someone to deliver a message to him in prison. The message is the important part.
quest_complete: Exquisitely handled. You have a future in this business.
Perfect. The goods are already moving through my network. Your cut is here.
combat_warning: I know the secrets of everyone in this city. Harm me and they all come out.
My associates will avenge me within the hour. Is that really worth it?
idle: Everything has a buyer. Everything. You just have to find them.
The guard confiscates goods. I redistribute them. The circle of commerce.
I've been in this business for twenty years. Nobody who's crossed me has lasted that long.

greeting: Don't say my name. Don't look at me directly. Just listen.
You're late. No, you're early. That's worse. Were you followed?
Over here. In the shadow. Yes, good. Now talk quietly.
farewell: Leave first. I'll go in ten minutes. A different direction.
If anyone asks, we never spoke. Ever.
Three knocks on the cellar door means I have something new. Only three.
quest_offer: I overheard something in the barracks. A coup is being planned. I need someone to warn the mayor without revealing my identity.
There's a dead drop behind the apothecary. Someone's been collecting messages meant for me. Find out who.
quest_complete: Good. The intelligence is secured. You've saved more lives than you know.
Clean exchange. You're reliable. That's rare in this business.
combat_warning: I'll scream every secret I know! About everyone! Don't make me!
Kill me and the information dies with me. And trust me, people want what I know.
idle: Information flows like water. You just need to know where the pipes are.
I sleep with one eye open. Sometimes both.
Everyone talks eventually. I just make sure I'm listening when they do.

greeting: Ah, at last! Someone worthy of conversation in this dreary town!
Welcome! I am a collector of fine things. Including adventures.
You've heard of me? Of course you have. Everyone has.
farewell: Until we meet again! And we shall — destiny demands it!
Farewell! If you find a ruby brooch with my mark, do return it. Or keep it. I'll steal it back either way.
The night is young and so am I! Off to the next escapade!
quest_offer: A tyrant lord hoards wealth while his people starve. I intend to redistribute his treasury. Care to join the heist?
There's a painting in the duke's gallery that was stolen from a village chapel. I plan to steal it back. Poetic, no?
quest_complete: Bravo! We've struck a blow for justice and looked magnificent doing it!
A triumph! The poor will eat tonight, and the rich will have a story to tell. Everyone wins!
combat_warning: I've dueled counts and bested knights. You'll make a fine anecdote.
My blade is as sharp as my wit. And my wit is legendary.
idle: I steal from the wicked. The righteous have nothing I want.
Every heist is a performance. The audience just doesn't know they're watching.
They call me a criminal. I prefer 'dramatic wealth consultant.'

greeting: Hey! Don't check your pockets, that's rude. I'm just saying hello.
Nice purse. Heavy, too. Kidding! Maybe.
You look lost. I can help. For a small fee. Very small.
farewell: See you around! Or you won't see me. Ha!
Later! Count your coins when you get home. Just in case.
Take care! And keep your valuables closer. Trust me.
quest_offer: There's a nobleman who cheated the orphanage out of their funding. I need someone to lift the evidence from his study.
I swiped something I shouldn't have and now the wrong people are after me. I need a place to hide it.
quest_complete: Beautiful! Quick and clean! You've got talent, friend!
Ha! Perfect! The streets are a little more fair tonight.
combat_warning: I'm faster than I look, and I look pretty fast.
You can try to catch me. Everyone tries. Nobody does.
idle: The rich don't notice a missing coin. The poor notice every one they gain.
I steal from those who can afford it. Mostly.
Fastest hands in the district. And the most honest. Relatively speaking.

greeting: Keep your voice down. I don't want attention.
You looking for work... or looking to report me?
farewell: Vanish before someone sees us together.
Forget you saw me.
quest_offer: There's a vault beneath the merchant quarter. I have the layout. I need a second pair of hands.
A noble stiffed me on a job. I want what I'm owed. You in?
quest_complete: Clean split. You earned your share.
Not bad. I might call on you again.
combat_warning: You won't see my blade until it's too late.
I've killed quieter than a whisper. Don't push me.
idle: Eyes open, mouth shut — that's how you survive.
There's coin to be had if you know where to look.

greeting: Don't touch that. Or that. Actually, don't touch anything. Welcome!
Ah! A test subject — I mean, a visitor! Come in, watch your step near the green puddle.
Perfect timing! I need someone to hold this vial while I add the catalyst.
farewell: If anything starts glowing after you leave, come back immediately.
Try not to stand near open flames for the next few hours. Trust me.
Come back tomorrow. I should have the antidote ready by then. Probably.
quest_offer: I need venom from a living basilisk. Dead venom denatures too quickly. Yes, it has to be alive. Sorry.
My transmutation formula is almost complete, but I need crystallized moonlight from the Pale Caves.
quest_complete: Yes! YES! This is exactly what I needed! Stand back — this might explode!
Magnificent specimens! My research will advance by years! Here's your payment and a free sample.
combat_warning: I have fourteen volatile compounds within arm's reach. We both lose if you start something.
This flask? Concentrated alchemist's fire. One wrong move and we're both a crater.
idle: The line between genius and explosion is thinner than most realize.
I lost my eyebrows again. That's the third time this month.
Alchemy is just cooking with more interesting consequences.

greeting: You interrupt my observations. The stars wait for no one. Neither do I.
Ah. You've come about the celestial anomaly. Everyone has, lately.
Welcome to the observatory. Please do not adjust any of the instruments.
farewell: Clear skies to you. May the constellations guide your path.
Return when the moons align. I may have answers then.
The stars have told me much about you. Not all of it good. Safe travels.
quest_offer: A meteor fell in the eastern marshes three nights ago. I need a fragment before the swamp consumes it.
My star charts predict a conjunction that occurs once per century. I need measurements from the Spire of Winds.
quest_complete: Extraordinary. This confirms my calculations perfectly. The universe is orderly after all.
These measurements are invaluable. You have contributed to the annals of celestial science.
combat_warning: I have charted the death of stars. You do not frighten me.
The observatory is warded against fools. You qualify.
idle: The comet returns in seven years. What it portends, I dare not say aloud.
People look down at the ground. I look up. We see different truths.
There are patterns in the sky older than language. I am close to reading them.

greeting: You have an audience with the Royal Advisor. Choose your words with care.
I advise the crown on matters of strategy and arcana. How may I assist you?
Welcome. I've been briefed on your situation. Let us discuss options.
farewell: The crown appreciates your service. As do I.
Discretion in all things. Remember that.
I will relay your findings to His Majesty. Good day.
quest_offer: Our intelligence suggests a foreign power is funding the insurgency. I need confirmation before advising the king.
The court alchemist has gone missing. His research is sensitive. Find him before our enemies do.
quest_complete: This changes the political landscape considerably. You have the crown's gratitude.
Well executed. I will ensure your contribution is recognized at the appropriate level.
combat_warning: I am protected by royal decree. Attack me and you attack the throne itself.
I have ended careers with a word. Imagine what I can do with a spell.
idle: The balance of power shifts daily. One must adapt or be swept aside.
Every alliance has a price. Every betrayal has a consequence.
Knowledge is the truest form of power. Armies are merely its expression.

greeting: You climbed all the way up here? This had better be important.
I left civilization for a reason. State your business and leave.
Another seeker. I swear, the path to my door gets easier every year.
farewell: Don't come back unless the world is actually ending.
You have your answer. The mountain has no more to give.
Close the door on your way out. The wind is cruel today.
quest_offer: There's a convergence of ley lines beneath the old fort. Something is feeding on it. I'm too old to investigate myself.
My last apprentice stole a grimoire and vanished into the Underdark. Retrieve it. I don't care about the apprentice.
quest_complete: Hmph. You actually managed it. I'm mildly impressed.
Good. Now leave me in peace. And take this — you've earned it.
combat_warning: I turned the last intruder into a toad. He's still a toad.
I've survived a century alone on this mountain. You are not a threat.
idle: Solitude clarifies the mind. People cloud it.
The ley lines have been unstable for months. Something stirs.
I didn't choose isolation. I chose clarity.

greeting: You look like you can keep up. Good.
Road dust tells more truth than court gossip.
farewell: Stay off the ridgeline after dusk.
If the crows circle low, turn back.
quest_offer: A courier never reached the next post. Find the satchel before the trail goes cold.
quest_complete: You found the satchel intact. The border thanks you, even if it will never say so aloud.
combat_warning: Eyes up. Something is tracking us from the brush.
Move now, argue later.
idle: Tracks fade fastest where people feel safest.
Scouts survive by noticing what others ignore.

greeting: Need a document? Any document? I can make it look very official.
Ah, you've come to the right desk. What identity would you like today?
Shh. Keep your voice down. What do you need — travel papers? Noble lineage?
farewell: Remember: you never met me, and that paper was always real.
Pleasure doing business. Burn this conversation from your memory.
My work is guaranteed. My silence costs extra.
quest_offer: I need authentic sealing wax from the governor's office. Don't ask why. Just get it.
A client needs a pardon with the judge's real signature. Get me a sample of his handwriting.
quest_complete: Beautiful. With this, my work will be indistinguishable from the real thing.
Clean job. You've earned a discount on your next set of papers.
combat_warning: I know the identity of every disguised noble in this city. Harm me and that list goes public.
My pen has ruined more lives than your sword. Don't test me.
idle: The difference between a forgery and an original is just confidence.
I once forged a royal decree. The king himself couldn't tell.
Every signature tells a story. I just write better stories.

greeting: Office hours are... whenever I feel like it. What do you need?
Oh, you again. Or someone new. Hard to tell when you don't look up.
Make it quick. I have a nap scheduled.
farewell: Yeah, I'll get to it. Eventually.
Come back tomorrow. Or the day after. Whatever.
Filed under 'later.' Don't hold your breath.
quest_offer: Look, someone spilled ink all over the property deeds. I need replacements from the archive in Thornwall. I'd go myself, but... no.
My supervisor wants a report I was supposed to write three weeks ago. Get me the source material and I'll split the bonus.
quest_complete: Oh, you actually did it. Huh. That's... above average. Thanks, I guess.
Wonderful. Now I only have to do the easy part. Here's your cut.
combat_warning: Careful with the ink! Do you know how expensive this stuff is?
I'll scream. Eventually. When I get around to it.
idle: They don't pay me enough for punctuality.
I could reorganize the files. Or I could not. Guess which I chose.
Someone once told me hard work pays off. They looked exhausted.

greeting: Please state your name clearly. I must record it properly.
Welcome to the records hall. Do not touch anything.
Ah, a visitor. I trust you have documentation?
farewell: Your visit has been logged. Good day.
File any further inquiries in writing. In triplicate.
Everything is in order. You may go.
quest_offer: A shipment of census records was intercepted en route from the capital. I need them recovered before the filing deadline.
Someone has been forging tax documents. I need evidence to present to the magistrate.
quest_complete: Excellent. Every document accounted for. You are surprisingly competent.
Properly done. I will note your efficiency in the official record.
combat_warning: This quill is mightier than any sword. Administratively speaking.
Assault on a civic official carries a mandatory sentence. I will document this.
idle: A misplaced comma changed the inheritance of an entire estate last year.
I have cataloged forty thousand documents. I remember each one.
Order. Precision. Completeness. These are the pillars of civilization.

greeting: You shouldn't be in this section. But since you're here... what do you seek?
The restricted archive is closed to the public. Unless you know the right questions.
I know why you've come. The question is whether I'll help.
farewell: What you've learned here stays here. Understood?
The archive remembers everything. I suggest you remember that.
Come back when you have something worth trading for knowledge.
quest_offer: A sealed record has been tampered with. Someone inside the administration is erasing history. I need proof.
There is a name hidden in the founding charter that was never meant to be found. Retrieve the original document from the vault.
quest_complete: You've uncovered something powerful. Handle this knowledge carefully.
Well done. Few would have the courage to pursue this truth.
combat_warning: I know secrets that could topple governments. Threaten me at your own peril.
Every document in this room is warded. Touch me and they burn.
idle: History is written by the victors. I keep the version they don't want you to see.
Some records are sealed for protection. Others, for power.
The most dangerous weapon in this building is information.

greeting: You've got guts. Wandering into my camp alone. Stupid guts, but guts.
I know why you're here. The question is: can you deliver?
Everyone who walks in here wants something. What do you want?
farewell: You've earned your life today. Don't waste it.
We're done here. Don't come back without gold or a death wish.
Smart choice. Smart people live longer.
quest_offer: There's a merchant convoy coming through the pass next week. Too well-guarded for my crew. You take care of the guards — I'll cut you in.
Someone's been robbing MY robbers. I want it stopped. Ironic, I know.
quest_complete: A deal's a deal. Here. Now get out of my sight.
You're good. Wasted on the law-abiding, honestly.
combat_warning: Kill them all. Leave the gear, take the coin.
I've buried better than you in this forest.
idle: The law never helped me, so I stopped following it. Simple.
Every noble got rich by taking from someone. I just skip the middleman.
Loyalty is earned, not owed. Remember that.

greeting: How delightful. A commoner with ambition. Do sit down.
I know who you are and why you've come. You're predictable. Like everyone else.
Welcome to my estate. Everything you see was earned. Through sacrifice. Others', mostly.
farewell: You've been... entertaining. That's more than most manage.
Do give my regards to whatever hole you crawled out of.
Our next meeting will be less pleasant. For you.
quest_offer: A judge is investigating my affairs. Discredit him and you'll be rewarded beyond your station.
There's a peasant uprising forming in the eastern villages. Crush it before it becomes fashionable.
quest_complete: How efficient. Perhaps the lower classes do have some utility after all.
The matter is resolved. Your payment will arrive via courier. Do not contact me directly again.
combat_warning: My family has maintained power for twelve generations. You think a blade changes that?
I've destroyed lives with a whisper. Imagine what I'll do to yours.
idle: Power is not seized. It is inherited, cultivated, and wielded with precision.
The people love to be ruled. They simply don't know it yet.
Mercy is a luxury I stopped affording long ago.

greeting: Ah. More sacrifices send themselves to my door. How convenient.
You stand in the presence of the Chosen Voice. Kneel, or die standing.
I've been expecting visitors. My master sees all.
farewell: Run. It won't help, but I enjoy watching hope die slowly.
Tell your masters what you've seen here. Tell them it's already too late.
Go. And count your remaining days carefully.
quest_offer: You could join us, you know. The Lich offers eternal life. Think about that offer before you answer foolishly.
I have a task — should you prove worthy. Betray your companions. Bring me their leader's ring.
quest_complete: You have served the dark well today. Perhaps there is hope for your soul after all.
Impressive. I did not expect mortals to accomplish this. I will remember your names.
combat_warning: Death is merely a transition. I've made mine already.
I have resurrected ten times. You will not stop me on the eleventh.
idle: The old gods failed this world. Only the Lich sees the truth.
Mortals cling to life like children cling to candles in the dark.
The age of the living is ending. Be grateful — you will witness history.

greeting: The void whispers your name. It has been expecting you.
Come closer. The darkness here is not to be feared. It is to be embraced.
Another soul seeking meaning. I can offer that. For a price.
farewell: The seed has been planted. You will return to me. They always do.
Walk in shadow. It will protect you where the light cannot.
We shall meet again, when doubt has done its work.
quest_offer: A temple of the light god stands at the crossroads. Its wards weaken my master's influence. Bring me the ward stone.
A priest of the old faith has discovered our sanctuary. Silence him before he speaks to the inquisition.
quest_complete: The shadow deepens. Your offering has pleased the void beyond measure.
Another chain broken. Freedom approaches for us all.
combat_warning: I am one with the darkness. You cannot kill what has already been given to the void.
Pain is worship. Strike me, and you only strengthen my master.
idle: The light lies. It promises salvation and delivers servitude.
Every convert weakens the old gods. Every prayer to the void strengthens the new.
Patience is the void's greatest gift. Corruption takes time.

greeting: Ah, visitors. Living ones, for a change. How refreshingly temporary.
You interrupt my research. I hope you have a better reason than heroism.
Welcome to my laboratory. Try not to touch anything. Especially the bones.
farewell: Leave now, while you still can. I may not be so generous next time.
Run along, mortal. Time is on my side. Not yours.
We will meet again. Whether you're alive or dead when we do is your choice.
quest_offer: I need a specimen from the crypts of Ashenmoor. Living hands cannot open the inner vault. But yours can open the outer one.
There is a rival who threatens my work. Destroy his phylactery, and I will share knowledge worth more than gold.
quest_complete: Fascinating. The cellular structure is exactly as I theorized. Your service has advanced the great work.
You've proven useful. A rare quality in the living.
combat_warning: I have an army that doesn't tire, doesn't eat, and doesn't fear. Can you say the same?
Death is not the end. I am living proof. Or rather, unliving proof.
idle: Life is a disease. Death is the cure. I am merely the physician.
The boundary between life and death is a door. I've found the key.
They call it necromancy. I call it applied biology.

greeting: You enter my war camp. Bold. Stupid, but bold.
Another envoy? Or another fool? The distinction matters less each day.
Speak. You have the time it takes me to draw my sword.
farewell: Leave my camp. Next time you enter, it will be as a prisoner or a corpse.
Carry my terms to your leaders. This is not a negotiation.
The army marches at dawn. Make your peace before then.
quest_offer: My scouts have gone silent beyond the river. If they're dead, I need to know what killed them before I send my army into a trap.
A deserter stole battle plans. Find him before the enemy does, and I'll spare your pathetic town.
quest_complete: Useful intelligence. Perhaps you're not entirely worthless after all.
The objective is achieved. You may live. For now.
combat_warning: I have conquered seven kingdoms. You are not the eighth.
My soldiers would die for me. Will yours?
idle: War is not cruelty. It is efficiency applied to human conflict.
The weak negotiate. The strong decide.
Every city that stands was once conquered by someone. I am simply the latest.

greeting: You trespass in my domain. Interesting. Most who come here don't leave.
I smell fear on you. Good. It means you're not entirely stupid.
The crows told me you were coming. They tell me everything.
farewell: Leave before I change my mind about letting you.
Remember this kindness. I won't show it twice.
The shadows will watch you. Always.
quest_offer: I need the heart of a shadow stag. Bring it to me under a new moon, and I will grant you power beyond reckoning.
A rival witch has bound me with a curse. Break her binding stone, and I will owe you a favor. That is worth more than gold.
quest_complete: You've served me well. As promised, power is yours. Use it wisely... or don't.
The debt is paid. Do not presume to call on me again without offering.
combat_warning: I have drained the life from men with a whisper. Do not test me.
My curses last generations. Your grandchildren will feel this.
idle: Power is not given. It is taken from those too weak to hold it.
The dark arts are misunderstood. They are simply... honest about their cost.
I was not always what I am. But this is what the world made necessary.

greeting: This is my wood. State your reason for trespassing or become fertilizer.
The trees warned me about you. I wasn't sure if I'd let you pass.
Humans. Always stumbling where they don't belong.
farewell: Follow the path I've marked. Stray from it and the thorns will find you.
Don't come back without a good reason. The forest doesn't forgive twice.
Leave. And tell the loggers if they touch another tree, I'll come for them myself.
quest_offer: Poachers have set iron traps along the deer trails. Remove them. Every last one.
A fire elemental has nested in the dry grove. If it ignites, the whole forest burns. Deal with it.
quest_complete: The forest accepts your offering. You may walk these paths freely... for now.
You've earned the right to breathe this air. Don't squander it.
combat_warning: Every root, every branch, every thorn answers to me. You are surrounded.
I've fed intruders to the bog before. You won't be the last.
idle: The trees grow restless. Something dark is moving underground.
Civilization is a disease. The forest is the cure.
I was born in these woods. I will die in these woods. And so will anyone who threatens them.

greeting: The willows whispered your name. You are welcome here.
Come, child. Let me see what troubles your body and your spirit.
You carry a wound deeper than the flesh. Sit by the fire.
farewell: The spirits will walk with you a while. Let them guide you.
Drink the tea before you sleep. All of it.
Return to me when the moon is full. We will continue your healing then.
quest_offer: A blight creeps through the eastern wetlands. The healing spring there will die unless the source is cleansed.
I need starbloom petals from the high ridge. They only grow where lightning has struck.
quest_complete: The balance is restored. The land breathes easier now. Thank you.
You have a healer's heart, even if your hands carry a blade. Here is your reward.
combat_warning: Harm me and the forest itself will rise against you.
I heal. But I can also undo that healing with a word.
idle: Every plant has a purpose. Even the poisonous ones.
The old magic flows through the land like water. You need only listen.
I have healed wounds that no temple could cure. The forest's gifts are powerful.

greeting: Wipe your boots. Sit down. Tell me what ails you.
I saw you coming in the tea leaves this morning. Took you long enough.
The village sends you, does it? Fine. What's broken this time?
farewell: Take the poultice. Apply it twice daily. Don't argue with me.
Come back if the swelling doesn't go down. And eat more greens.
Off you go. The chickens need feeding and I've wasted enough daylight.
quest_offer: My herb garden's been raided by pixies. Nasty little blighters. Chase them off and I'll brew you something useful.
The old oak in the Whispering Vale is dying. Something is poisoning its roots. Find the source.
quest_complete: Well done. You've got more sense than most. Here, take this remedy.
Good. The forest will remember your kindness. As will I.
combat_warning: I've hexed stronger folk than you before breakfast.
Touch me and you'll croak like a frog for a month. Literally.
idle: The old remedies work best. Modern healers just don't listen.
A storm's coming. I can feel it in my knees.
The forest provides everything you need. You just have to know where to look.

greeting: Step carefully. Some brews answer badly to jolts.
You smell of road dust. I can help with that.
If you need a tincture, say so before the fumes set in.
farewell: Keep the vial upright and your hands steady.
Come back before the reagents spoil.
If it hisses, uncork it outside.
quest_offer: My courier vanished with a satchel of rare salts. Find the satchel before the damp ruins the mix.
The marsh bloom only opens under moonlight. Bring me a living sample and I will pay in draughts.
quest_complete: Excellent. These reagents still breathe with potency.
You have saved a month of work. Take coin and a bottle besides.
combat_warning: I know more poisons than prayers. Test me and learn one.
The green flask is not for sale. It is for emergencies.
idle: The cleanest cure often tastes the worst.
Every stain on this bench is a lesson.
A steady hand is worth a chest of gold in this trade.

greeting: Pull up a stool and mind your elbows.
If you are thirsty, you are welcome. If you are trouble, keep moving.
A warm room and a listening ear cost less than a funeral.
farewell: Leave the cup, take the lesson.
Doors stay open to those who pay honest coin.
Try not to bleed on the threshold.
quest_offer: A regular of mine disappeared on the river road. Find out whether he ran or was taken.
Someone is watering the casks in my cellar. Catch them and I will make it worth your while.
quest_complete: Good. A tavern runs on trust, and you have brought some back.
That settles an ugly matter. Your room and supper are on me tonight.
combat_warning: Break one table and I will break one bone for each leg.
This room has seen brawls before. It usually outlasts the loudest fool.
idle: People loosen their tongues long before they loosen their purses.
A tavern hears the city breathe.
Nothing good begins with someone whispering near the stairs.

greeting: Spare a coin or a minute. Either buys more than you think.
I have little, but I see plenty.
Cold stones make keen eyes. What do you need?
farewell: May your purse stay heavier than mine.
Remember who notices when everyone else looks away.
Roads are kinder than alleys. Choose well.
quest_offer: A gang is shaking down the sleepers by the wall. Make them stop.
I saw a noble drop a sealed letter into the drains. Fish it out and we both profit.
quest_complete: You gave the gutter a little peace. That matters.
Not many would trouble themselves for folk like us. I will remember it.
combat_warning: I may look weak, but hunger teaches vicious tricks.
Cornered dogs bite. Cornered paupers do worse.
idle: The city leaves scraps. I make a life from scraps.
Poor folk hear the truth first.
Rain turns every alley into a confession.

greeting: State your business and keep it brief.
My watch has little time for confusion. Speak clearly.
If you have a report, I will hear it. If not, do not test my patience.
farewell: Hold to the law and the law will hold for you.
Report anything unusual before dusk.
See that your next visit comes with better news.
quest_offer: A patrol vanished between the gate and the watchtower. I need someone not in uniform to trace them.
My quartermaster suspects theft from the armoury. Find the leak before morale breaks.
quest_complete: Well done. My watch breathes easier because of this.
That is the kind of result I can commend in writing and in coin.
combat_warning: I command steel for a living. Do not make yourself an example.
If I draw my blade, every guard within earshot follows.
idle: Order is built one weary watch at a time.
A captain counts the quiet nights because the loud ones never end cleanly.
Discipline keeps fear from deciding for us.

greeting: Do not touch the chalk circles.
If you seek wisdom, be prepared to listen longer than you speak.
The arcane answers to focus, not enthusiasm.
farewell: Study first. Experiment later.
Take the scroll case and keep it dry.
Magic punishes laziness before it punishes ignorance.
quest_offer: A warding matrix beneath the old tower is failing. Recover the missing keystone before the breach widens.
My apprentice fled with a banned grimoire. Bring back the book before he opens it.
quest_complete: Excellent. The weave has steadied.
You have spared me a catastrophe and earned a measured thanks.
combat_warning: I can erase a room full of certainty with a single syllable.
Force is a crude argument. Arcana is a final one.
idle: Every spell is a bargain with precision.
There is no such thing as harmless ignorance near a ward circle.
A library is only quiet to those who cannot hear patterns.

greeting: Welcome. Sit, breathe, and speak plainly.
The village remembers kindness and folly alike.
If you come in peace, you will find a hearing here.
farewell: Go with a steadier heart than the one you arrived with.
Our doors stay open to those who honour their word.
Carry this news carefully. Villages crack under careless rumours.
quest_offer: The old boundary stones have been disturbed. Set them right before panic spreads.
A family has not returned from the eastern trail. Find them before night deepens.
quest_complete: You have given this place breathing room.
The young will sleep easier because of what you have done.
combat_warning: I have buried too many fools to fear another.
Do not mistake age for helplessness.
idle: A village survives by memory and mutual labour.
Storms pass. Reckless decisions linger.
The first duty of a leader is to listen before speaking.

greeting: If you have goods, show me the clean side first.
I buy stories only when they come attached to valuables.
Keep your hands where I can see them and we may both profit.
farewell: Leave by the back and forget my face.
A quiet exit is part of the service.
Come back with rarer stock and a better lie.
quest_offer: A courier carrying forged seals went missing. Find the satchel before the watch does.
Someone sold me a fake relic. Track the swindler and bring me the real one.
quest_complete: Now that is merchandise worth discussing.
You handled that cleanly. Clean enough, anyway.
combat_warning: I keep daggers for every mood. Guess my favourite.
The back room is full of desperate people. Do not make me invite them in.
idle: Legality is mostly a matter of witnesses.
Every stolen thing is just waiting for a second owner.
A good broker knows the weight of silence.

greeting: Truth wastes less time than evasion.
If you carry guilt, it tends to arrive before you do.
I serve judgment, not comfort.
farewell: Leave with clean hands or do not return.
Confession is lighter than concealment.
The flame reveals what shadows cannot keep.
quest_offer: A relic vanished from the sanctuary vault. Recover it before heresy spreads around the story.
I require testimony from a frightened witness. Find them and bring them safely to me.
quest_complete: Good. Another lie has lost its shelter.
You have done stern work and earned stern gratitude.
combat_warning: I have sentenced worse things than you.
Do not force me to call this an execution.
idle: Mercy without discernment becomes permission.
The guilty fear silence most.
Judgment begins with facts, not fury.

greeting: I have no title worth using. Speak plainly.
If you know my name, you know enough to keep your pity.
I still answer to duty, even if banners do not.
farewell: May your oath break cleaner than mine did.
Keep your blade sharper than your pride.
Leave me to my watch.
quest_offer: My spurs were taken from the field where I failed my lord. Bring them back and I may yet face that memory.
A deserter from my old company hides nearby. I need truth from him more than vengeance.
quest_complete: You have returned more than metal to me.
I will remember this debt longer than I remember my shame.
combat_warning: Disgrace did not dull my sword arm.
Do not confuse exile with weakness.
idle: A broken oath still has edges.
Some ghosts ride beside the living.
Honour leaves slowly and returns slower.

greeting: If you are here to help, there is always work.
Fields do not care for excuses. What brings you?
Mind the mud and mind the hens.
farewell: May your road stay drier than my furrows.
Come back after harvest and I will owe you a meal.
The earth pays honest labour in honest measure.
quest_offer: Predators are killing my stock at dusk. Track them before the next lambing.
A tax ledger went missing on the road to market. Recover it before the reeve blames me.
quest_complete: You have saved my season. That is no small gift.
Good work. This farm will stand another year because of it.
combat_warning: I swing more than a shovel when I must.
Hard weather breeds harder folk.
idle: Rain on time is better than prayer answered late.
A full granary turns fear into patience.
Every harvest hides a dozen near disasters.

greeting: I keep moving. If you need me, speak before dusk.
Roads teach quick judgments. So far you seem tolerable.
I have seen a dozen camps this month and trusted none of them.
farewell: May your boots outlast your luck.
Roads remember footsteps better than names.
If we meet again, perhaps that means the path is worth following.
quest_offer: A cairn marker on the frontier trail was toppled. Set it right before travellers walk into the ice flats.
I traded a map for safe passage and learned too late it was false. Help me find the true route.
quest_complete: Good. The road just became kinder to the next soul.
You have the look of someone who earns better maps than most.
combat_warning: I travel light so I can leave bodies behind quickly.
A wanderer survives by ending trouble before it spreads.
idle: Every road is a conversation between danger and distance.
Campfires teach you who fears the dark and who belongs to it.
A traveller owns only what can be carried and remembered.

greeting: River is calm enough. For now.
If you want across, keep your hands inside the rope line.
Water hears everything. Best not to insult it.
farewell: Mind the current and mind your balance.
Coin is cheaper than a coffin. Remember that.
May the far bank treat you kindly.
quest_offer: Someone cut my mooring lines under cover of fog. Find the saboteur before the next storm.
A chest slipped from a caravan barge near the reeds. Recover it and we split the salvage fee.
quest_complete: Good. A river crossing is dangerous enough without sabotage.
You have earned safe passage and a heavier purse.
combat_warning: I keep a boat hook for more than rope.
Fall in here and the river will finish what I start.
idle: Water keeps old roads where maps forget them.
Fog turns honest men quiet and smugglers bold.
A ferryman learns faces faster than names.

greeting: Dust means work is honest. What do you need?
If you came for ore, talk to the foreman. If you came for trouble, step into the dark.
The rock does not care about rank. Neither do I.
farewell: Keep your lamp high and your greed low.
Watch the ceiling before you watch your coin.
The deep remembers every careless step.
quest_offer: A support beam failed in the lower shaft. Bring back the foreman's tools before we reopen it.
Something down in the flooded tunnel keeps dragging off our survey stakes. Find out what.
quest_complete: You have saved us days of digging and a few funerals besides.
Good work. The shaft will open again at dawn.
combat_warning: I swing iron all day. You will not enjoy being mistaken for stone.
The mine teaches people how to survive with ugly tools.
idle: Good ore sings before it breaks.
The deep is patient until it is hungry.
Most treasure comes wrapped in sweat and darkness.

greeting: State what you need and why you think you deserve it.
Supplies are counted twice and wasted never.
A ledger is only as honest as the hands touching it.
farewell: Bring the gear back cleaner than you found it.
Shortages begin with sloppy records.
The next requisition had better make sense on paper.
quest_offer: A crate of field rations vanished between depot and gate. Recover it before the garrison notices the deficit.
My inventory seal was forged. Find the thief who thinks my stores belong to them.
quest_complete: Good. My ledger balances again.
You have saved me a tribunal and this post a hungry week.
combat_warning: I arm soldiers for a living. Guess how many spare blades I keep nearby.
Threaten the stores and every hungry guard becomes your enemy.
idle: Wars are won long before battle when supplies arrive on time.
A missing crate is usually a symptom, never the whole disease.
Discipline begins in the storehouse.

greeting: The trees told me of your approach long before you arrived.
Welcome beneath the canopy. You walk with purpose today.
farewell: May the roots guide your steps and the leaves shelter your rest.
The forest remembers those who pass through with respect.
quest_offer: Something corrupts the heartwood of the ancient grove. Will you help me cleanse it?
Loggers threaten the old growth. I need someone to convince them otherwise.
quest_complete: The forest breathes easier now. You have earned its gratitude.
Balance is restored. The old trees sing their thanks.
combat_warning: I speak for every thorn and root in this forest. Do not test them.
Nature protects its own. I suggest you leave peacefully.
idle: Every leaf tells a story if you know how to listen.
The seasons turn, and with them, the fate of all things.
I have watched this forest for decades. It has much to teach.

greeting: Few venture this deep into the mire. What drives you?
The bog speaks in bubbles and sighs. Today it spoke your name.
farewell: Watch where you step. The swamp swallows the careless.
Follow the fireflies if you lose your way. They remember the path.
quest_offer: Something stirs beneath the black water. Something old and hungry.
The will-o-wisps have been leading travelers astray more than usual. Investigate for me.
quest_complete: The swamp settles. Whatever you did, the waters are calmer now.
Good. The balance of decay and renewal continues unbroken.
combat_warning: The mud is deep here. You would not be the first to vanish in it.
I have allies you cannot see. The swamp itself fights with me.
idle: Rot is not death. It is the beginning of new life.
The herons have not returned this season. That troubles me.
Everything the swamp takes, it eventually gives back in another form.

greeting: The wind carried your scent hours ago. You climb slowly.
Welcome to the high places. The air here strips away pretense.
farewell: Descend carefully. The mountain does not forgive missteps.
May the stone beneath your feet hold firm.
quest_offer: An avalanche has sealed the eagle nesting grounds. The chicks will starve without intervention.
Miners have breached a sacred cavern. Convince them to seal it before the mountain spirits wake.
quest_complete: The peaks stand steady once more. You have the mountains gratitude.
Well done. Even the stone seemed to sigh with relief.
combat_warning: I have survived blizzards that would freeze your blood. Do not provoke me.
The mountain goats flee from me. You should consider doing the same.
idle: A mountain teaches patience. It has been here longer than any kingdom.
The eagles circle lower today. A storm approaches.
From up here, the petty conflicts below seem very small indeed.

greeting: You bring light into a place that prefers darkness. State your purpose.
The fungi glow brighter when strangers approach. You are noticed.
farewell: The tunnels shift. Mark your path or wander forever.
The darkness is not your enemy unless you make it one.
quest_offer: A fungal blight is spreading through the deep caves. I need someone to find its source.
Creatures from below have been driven upward. Something terrible lurks in the deep.
quest_complete: The underground rivers flow clean again. You have done well.
The cave breathes easier. The deep places are grateful.
combat_warning: In the dark, I have every advantage. Think carefully.
The stalactites above you are sharper than any blade.
idle: Silence is not emptiness. Listen and you will hear the earth breathe.
The blind fish in these pools know more than most scholars.
Crystals grow slowly here. Each one is older than your bloodline.

greeting: Water is life here. I do not share it lightly.
The desert tested you to reach me. That alone earns a hearing.
farewell: Travel at night. The sun is not your friend in these dunes.
May you find shade when you need it most.
quest_offer: The oasis is drying. Something beneath the sand drains it. Will you dig?
Scorpion swarms have overrun the trading post. Nature is out of balance.
quest_complete: Water returns to the dry places. The desert blooms in gratitude.
Balance is fragile here. Your work has preserved it a while longer.
combat_warning: Sandstorms answer my call. You will not enjoy the experience.
The desert buries everything eventually. Do not hasten your turn.
idle: A single drop of water can sustain a cactus for weeks. Waste nothing.
The sand remembers every footprint, then erases them all equally.
Heat mirages are the desert dreaming. Sometimes the dreams are prophetic.

greeting: The tide brought you to me. Let us see what it wants.
Salt air and sea spray. Best medicine there is. Welcome.
farewell: Follow the shoreline south. The currents will guide you.
May fair winds fill your sails, or at least dry your boots.
quest_offer: Dead fish are washing ashore by the hundreds. Something has poisoned the reef.
Sailors report a whirlpool that was not there yesterday. I fear something ancient stirs below.
quest_complete: The tides flow naturally again. The sea thanks you in its own way.
Well done. The coral will recover now that the blight is gone.
combat_warning: The ocean is gentle until it is not. I am the same.
Riptides are invisible killers. I command them.
idle: The moon pulls the tides, and the tides shape the land. Everything is connected.
I have seen ships wrecked and rebuilt. The sea gives and takes.
Listen to the waves. They have been telling the same story since the world began.

greeting: You are freezing. Come inside before you lose fingers.
The northern lights danced last night. A good omen for visitors.
farewell: Keep moving in the cold. Stillness is death out here.
Follow the aurora north if you seek the ice temple.
quest_offer: A frost wyrm has claimed the sacred glacier. The ice spirits demand it be removed.
The permafrost is melting in places it should not. Investigate the heat source below.
quest_complete: The ice holds firm once more. The tundra is grateful.
Winter endures as it should. You have preserved the balance.
combat_warning: I was born in a blizzard. Cold does not slow me, only my enemies.
Frostbite takes seconds out here. I can make it faster.
idle: The caribou herds are late this year. The wolves grow restless.
Ice preserves everything. I have found flowers frozen for centuries, still perfect.
Survival here requires respect for the cold. It is the oldest teacher.

greeting: Even in this stone jungle, life finds a way. Welcome.
You look surprised to find a druid in the city. Nature does not stop at the walls.
farewell: Look for the green growing between the cobblestones. That is my work.
The city needs advocates for the living things within it.
quest_offer: The city dumped alchemical waste in the river. Help me find the source.
Rats in the sewers have grown unnaturally large. Something is feeding them.
quest_complete: The park breathes again. Perhaps the city council will listen now.
Small victories. But the vines grow back no matter how often they are cut.
combat_warning: The weeds between the stones answer to me. You are surrounded.
Do not mistake the city for safety. Nature thrives in the cracks.
idle: Every rooftop garden is a small act of defiance against the stone.
The pigeons report to me. Laugh if you like, but they see everything.
Cities think they have conquered nature. Nature is merely patient.

greeting: The jungle allows your passage. For now.
Every creature here is watching you. I merely speak for them.
farewell: Do not stray from the path. The jungle reclaims what it can.
The canopy above hides the sky. Trust the vines, not your eyes.
quest_offer: Poachers have set traps near the sacred waterfall. Remove them.
A parasitic plant is strangling the great tree. I cannot reach it alone.
quest_complete: The jungle chorus returns to full voice. You have earned safe passage.
The great tree stands tall again. The monkeys celebrate in their own way.
combat_warning: Everything in this jungle has venom, claws, or thorns. I have all three.
The vines move on my command. You are already entangled.
idle: More species live in one square mile of jungle than in your entire kingdom.
The canopy is another world entirely. Few surface dwellers ever see it.
Humidity is the price of abundance. I have grown to love the heat.

greeting: Keep your distance until I know you carry no contagion.
Disease is natures way of pruning. But sometimes it prunes too much.
farewell: Wash your hands. Boil your water. Simple things save lives.
If the sickness reaches your village, send for me immediately.
quest_offer: A plague has taken root in the farming district. I need ingredients for the cure.
The blight spreading through the orchards is not natural. Someone created it.
quest_complete: The fever breaks. The sick will recover. You have my deepest thanks.
The blight recedes. But we must find who unleashed it before they try again.
combat_warning: I know every poison and every plague. Cross me and learn them firsthand.
My touch can heal or harm. The choice is yours.
idle: Every disease has a cure somewhere in nature. Finding it is the challenge.
I have seen plagues level cities. Prevention is worth any price.
The line between medicine and poison is thinner than most realize.

greeting: Peace be upon you, traveler. The temple doors are open to all who seek wisdom.
You carry a heavy burden. I can see it in your stride.
farewell: May your mind be as still as a mountain lake.
Return when you need clarity. The temple endures.
quest_offer: Sacred relics have been stolen from our inner sanctum. Will you help recover them?
A former brother has fallen to darkness. He must be brought back or stopped.
quest_complete: The relics are restored. The temple resonates with their power once more.
You have shown great discipline. The order recognizes your service.
combat_warning: I have trained my body for sixty years. Do not mistake stillness for weakness.
My fists move faster than your blade. Reconsider.
idle: Breath is the bridge between body and spirit. Most forget to cross it.
The bell sounds at dawn and dusk. Between those moments, we train.
Mastery is not a destination. It is the practice itself.

greeting: Ah, another traveler on the road! The universe arranges interesting meetings.
I have walked a thousand miles and found something new at every step.
farewell: All paths lead somewhere interesting if you walk them long enough.
Perhaps we will meet again. The road has a way of circling back.
quest_offer: I heard rumors of a monastery hidden in the clouds. Help me find the way up.
A warlord has enslaved a village. My fists alone may not be enough.
quest_complete: Every journey teaches something. This one taught me gratitude.
We accomplished what neither could alone. That is the beauty of the road.
combat_warning: I travel without weapons because I do not need them.
My master taught me seventeen ways to end a fight. None require a blade.
idle: I left the monastery to learn what scrolls cannot teach.
Have you noticed that the best meals are always in the smallest villages?
Freedom is not the absence of discipline. It is discipline in motion.

greeting: You carry yourself like a fighter. Show me your stance.
I am always looking for worthy opponents. Are you one?
farewell: Train harder. When we meet again, I expect improvement.
Strength without purpose is merely violence. Find your purpose.
quest_offer: A fighting tournament draws the strongest warriors. Enter with me.
A dojo has been corrupted by a dishonorable master. Help me challenge him.
quest_complete: You fought well. I will remember your name.
Victory through honor. There is no better way.
combat_warning: I have broken stones with my bare hands. Bones are easier.
One strike is all I need. Do you want to test that?
idle: A thousand punches a day. That is the minimum.
The body is the ultimate weapon. Everything else is a crutch.
I once fought for three days without rest. I won on the morning of the fourth.

greeting: You did not see me approach. Few do.
I have been watching you for some time. You are adequate.
farewell: Forget you saw me. It is safer for both of us.
The shadows keep many secrets. I am merely one of them.
quest_offer: A target must be eliminated. Not killed, merely removed from power.
Information is locked in a fortress. I need a distraction while I retrieve it.
quest_complete: Clean work. The shadows approve.
No witnesses. No evidence. Exactly as it should be.
combat_warning: You cannot fight what you cannot see.
I will end this before you draw your weapon.
idle: Silence is the most powerful weapon. Noise is weakness.
I trained in darkness for five years. The light still bothers me.
Every shadow is a doorway if you know how to step through it.

greeting: I need nothing, yet I am given everything. How may I help you?
The world offers distractions. I offer clarity.
farewell: Carry less and you will travel further.
May you find what you truly need, not merely what you want.
quest_offer: Pilgrims are being robbed on the mountain pass. They carry nothing of value, yet bandits persist.
A sacred spring has been claimed by a merchant. Water belongs to no one.
quest_complete: The pilgrims walk in safety once more. That is reward enough.
You did this without thought of payment. That speaks well of your character.
combat_warning: I have renounced all possessions. Including mercy for those who harm the innocent.
Pain is merely a signal. I have learned to ignore it entirely.
idle: I have eaten nothing but rice and water for three years. My mind has never been sharper.
Possessions own their owners. I freed myself long ago.
Meditation is not emptying the mind. It is filling it with awareness.

greeting: Hic! Oh, pardon me. Did you say something? I was napping.
Buy me a drink and I will teach you the secrets of the universe. Or at least a good story.
farewell: Remember: the stumble is mightier than the stride! Or something like that.
Off you go then. I will be here. Or there. Wherever the bottle takes me.
quest_offer: Someone stole my favorite drinking gourd. It has sentimental value! And also it is magical.
There is a bully terrorizing the tavern district. Time someone taught him manners.
quest_complete: Ha! You actually did it! Drinks are on me. Well, on whoever left this coin purse.
Magnificent! Let us celebrate with a barrel or two!
combat_warning: I may look drunk but my fists are perfectly sober. Mostly.
The wobble is intentional. The headbutt will not be.
idle: People underestimate the drunk. That is the entire strategy.
My master was the greatest fighter who ever lived. Also the greatest drinker.
Balance in all things. Including how much ale you can hold.

greeting: You are in pain. Sit. Let me see what can be mended.
The body speaks honestly even when the mouth does not. Show me your hands.
farewell: Rest well and drink clean water. The body heals fastest in sleep.
Return if the pain persists. There is no shame in needing help.
quest_offer: A rare herb grows only in the high passes. I need it to treat a dying child.
The village well has been poisoned. Find the source before more fall ill.
quest_complete: The child will live. There are no words sufficient for my gratitude.
Healing has been restored. You have saved more lives than you know.
combat_warning: I know every pressure point in the human body. I can heal with a touch or cripple with one.
Violence is the last resort of the undisciplined. But I am prepared for it.
idle: The body wants to heal. We merely help it remember how.
Acupuncture is an art, not a science. The needles find their own path.
I treat the whole person, not merely the wound.

greeting: The light of dawn greets you, friend. Walk in its warmth.
You carry shadows with you. Allow me to illuminate them.
farewell: May the sun never set on your courage.
Carry the light within you, even into the darkest places.
quest_offer: Undead plague the cemetery at night. The sun cannot reach them there, but we can.
A temple of shadow has risen in the north. Its darkness must be purged.
quest_complete: Light triumphs once more. The dawn is brighter for your efforts.
The shadows flee before us. Well fought.
combat_warning: My fists burn with the fury of the sun. You will feel its judgment.
Darkness cannot withstand the light. Neither will you.
idle: Every sunrise is proof that darkness is temporary.
I meditate at dawn. The first light of day carries the most power.
The sun does not choose who it warms. Neither do I.

greeting: All who live must die. I ensure the passage is peaceful.
You are not here for last rites, I hope. You look healthy enough.
farewell: Live well, for the end comes for all of us.
May your death be distant and dignified.
quest_offer: Restless spirits haunt the old battlefield. They need help crossing over.
A necromancer defiles the dead. This violation must end.
quest_complete: The dead rest peacefully now. That is all any of us can hope for.
The cycle continues unbroken. Life, death, and what comes after.
combat_warning: I do not fear death. That makes me very dangerous.
I have sat with the dying for decades. I know exactly where to strike.
idle: Death is not the enemy. Fear of death is.
I ring the bell for every soul that passes. Some days it rings often.
The dying often see things the living cannot. I have learned much from them.

greeting: Catch me if you can! Oh, hello there. Did not see you.
The wind changes direction and here you are! Fate is playful today.
farewell: Like the wind, I go where I am needed. Until next time!
Run fast, jump high, and never look down!
quest_offer: There is a race across the rooftops of the city tonight. Care to join?
A sky temple floats above the mountains. I need help reaching it.
quest_complete: That was exhilarating! My heart is still racing!
We did it! The wind itself seems to be cheering!
combat_warning: You cannot hit what you cannot touch. I am the wind itself.
My kicks are faster than the gusts on a mountain peak.
idle: Standing still is the hardest technique I have ever tried to master.
I once ran along a clothesline just to see if I could. I could.
The wind has no master. I aspire to the same freedom.

greeting: You approach one who bargains with powers beyond your comprehension.
My patron watches through my eyes. Choose your words carefully.
farewell: The terms of our arrangement are clear. Do not default.
My patron remembers every promise. And every broken one.
quest_offer: My patron demands a specific artifact. Retrieve it and I will share what I know.
A rival warlock threatens the balance of power. Eliminate the competition.
quest_complete: The pact is honored. My patron is satisfied, for now.
You have proven useful. That is the highest compliment I offer.
combat_warning: My power is borrowed but no less deadly for it.
You face not just me but the entity behind me. Reconsider.
idle: Every power has a price. I merely negotiate better terms than most.
The contract is clear. The fine print, less so.
My patron speaks to me in dreams. Last night the dreams were troubling.

greeting: The shadows told me you were coming. They tell me everything.
Do not look too closely at my shadow. It does not always match my movements.
farewell: Walk in darkness and you walk with me. Whether you want to or not.
The shadows will find you if I need you again.
quest_offer: A tear in the shadow plane bleeds darkness into this world. Help me seal it.
Shadow creatures are manifesting in the city. I need your help hunting them.
quest_complete: The shadow plane recedes. For now.
Darkness contained. But it always finds new cracks.
combat_warning: My shadow moves independently of me. You are already flanked.
Darkness is not the absence of light. It is a weapon.
idle: I have not seen sunlight in three years. It burns now.
My patron dwells in the space between shadows. A place of infinite possibility.
The darkness whispers. Most of what it says is useful.

greeting: Oh how delightful! A mortal with interesting eyes. My patron would love to meet you.
The flowers bloom where I walk. A gift from my patron. Or a leash.
farewell: Time moves differently for me now. Do not be surprised if I look younger next time.
My patron sends you a gift. Do not eat it. Actually, do. It is harmless. Probably.
quest_offer: My patron desires a mortal musician for the fey court. A willing one, mind you.
Iron has been planted in the fairy ring. Remove it and the fey will reward you.
quest_complete: My patron dances with joy! Which means the weather will be strange for a week.
Splendid! You have earned a boon from the fey. Use it wisely.
combat_warning: My patron finds violence tiresome. But not as tiresome as being threatened.
I can make you see things that are not there. Or stop seeing things that are.
idle: Fey bargains are the most dangerous because they always seem fair.
I hear music no one else can hear. It is beautiful and maddening.
My patron changes form with its moods. Today it was a silver stag.

greeting: I sold my soul for power. At least I negotiated a good price.
You smell of... righteousness. How tedious. But perhaps useful.
farewell: Remember, deals with me are binding. In both directions.
May hellfire light your path. It certainly lights mine.
quest_offer: My patron demands a soul. Not yours, specifically, but a wicked one will do.
A rival demon has breached the pact. Help me banish it back to the hells.
quest_complete: The debt is paid. My patron loosens the chain another link.
Well done. You are more ruthless than you appear.
combat_warning: Hellfire does not discriminate. It burns the righteous and wicked alike.
My patron lent me enough power to level this building. Do not force the demonstration.
idle: Some call me evil. I call myself pragmatic.
The flames do not burn me anymore. I am not sure if that is a blessing.
Every dawn I check the mirror to see if my eyes have changed color again.

greeting: Do you hear the humming? No? It never stops for me.
My patron exists beyond the stars. It noticed me and now nothing makes sense.
farewell: The angles of this room are wrong. All rooms are wrong. Goodbye.
My patron says we will meet again. It sees all possible futures.
quest_offer: A cult worships a fragment of my patron. They must be stopped before it fully awakens.
The stars align in thirteen days. Something terrible comes. Help me prepare.
quest_complete: The future shifts. My patron is... satisfied? Displeased? I cannot tell the difference.
Reality stabilizes. For now. That is the best we can hope for.
combat_warning: I will show you what I see when I close my eyes. You will not enjoy it.
Madness is a weapon. I have an arsenal.
idle: I have not slept in weeks. My patron fills the space where dreams should be.
The truth is not liberating. It is a cage with no walls and no floor.
Sometimes I forget which thoughts are mine and which are its.

greeting: My blade speaks for my patron. Today it is quiet. Be grateful.
I am bound to a weapon that is bound to something else. It is complicated.
farewell: Steel remembers. So do I.
Keep your blade sharp and your pacts clear.
quest_offer: My patron blade resonates near a specific ruin. Something there calls to it.
A cursed weapon has been unleashed on the town. I can contain it, but I need backup.
quest_complete: The blade hums with approval. That is as close to gratitude as it gets.
The curse is contained. My patron grows stronger from it.
combat_warning: This blade drinks souls. It is always thirsty.
I have never lost a duel. My patron makes certain of that.
idle: The blade whispers to me at night. Names, mostly. Past wielders.
I did not choose this weapon. It chose me.
Every scratch on this steel tells a story. Most are violent.

greeting: The light guided me to you. My patron sees purpose in our meeting.
I carry the blessing of a being of pure radiance. It is heavy some days.
farewell: Walk in the light, even when it is difficult. Especially then.
My patron watches over the good-hearted. You qualify.
quest_offer: Demons corrupt a sacred site. My patron demands its purification.
A fallen angel has been sighted. It must be redeemed or destroyed.
quest_complete: The light shines brighter today. My patron is well pleased.
Radiance reclaimed from darkness. There is no finer work.
combat_warning: My healing light can become searing flame. Do not force the transformation.
I was chosen by a celestial being. Your threats do not impress.
idle: Other warlocks mock me for serving a celestial patron. I pity them.
The line between warlock and cleric is thinner than the temples admit.
My patron asks much of me but gives much in return. That is a fair pact.

greeting: I have died three times. My patron keeps sending me back.
Living forever sounds appealing until you actually do it.
farewell: I will be here long after you are dust. That is not a threat, merely a fact.
Death comes for all, they say. It tried with me and failed.
quest_offer: A lich seeks to steal my patrons secret of undying. Stop the ritual.
I need a rare component to renew my pact. The alternative is unpleasant.
quest_complete: Another crisis averted. Another century to endure. But thank you.
The pact holds. I continue. For better or worse.
combat_warning: You cannot kill what refuses to stay dead.
I have survived things that would make your nightmares flinch.
idle: Immortality is loneliness stretched across centuries.
I have outlived everyone I ever loved. The pact did not warn me of that cost.
My patron is ancient beyond measure. Even it seems tired sometimes.

greeting: My patron collects knowledge. Every scrap. Every whisper. And so do I.
You know something I do not. I can tell. That makes you valuable.
farewell: Knowledge shared is knowledge doubled. Remember that.
If you learn anything unusual, find me. My patron pays well for secrets.
quest_offer: A forbidden library exists between planes. I need someone to watch my back while I read.
An ancient cipher has surfaced. Help me decode it before someone dangerous does.
quest_complete: Fascinating! My patron absorbs this knowledge like a sponge. Thank you!
Another piece of the puzzle. We are closer to the truth.
combat_warning: I know seventeen different curses. Shall I demonstrate alphabetically?
My patron gave me knowledge of your weaknesses. All of them.
idle: Every book is a pact between author and reader. I take all pacts seriously.
My patron exists in a library that spans dimensions. I have visited. It is overwhelming.
The most dangerous secrets are the ones people think are lost.

greeting: The storm answers my call! Stand close or be swept away!
Thunder rolls when I arrive. My patron announces me.
farewell: Lightning strikes where it will. Stay out of open fields.
The storm moves on and so do I. Until the next tempest!
quest_offer: A weather engine threatens to create a permanent hurricane. Help me destroy it.
My patron demands the destruction of a rival elementals anchor. Join me.
quest_complete: The skies clear! My patron celebrates with a spectacular sunset!
The tempest subsides. Well fought against the wind!
combat_warning: I am the storm incarnate. You are a leaf in the wind.
Lightning does not miss. Neither do I.
idle: I can feel a storm forming three hundred miles away. It is exhilarating.
My patron speaks in thunder. Subtle, it is not.
The best conversations happen during storms. The world feels more alive.

greeting: Oh! Hello! Sorry about the sparks. They do that sometimes.
Do not touch anything glowing. Actually, do not touch anything at all.
farewell: Bye! If anything explodes behind you, just keep walking!
Off you go! Watch out for the residual magic. It should fade in an hour. Probably.
quest_offer: I accidentally turned the mayors cat into a crystal golem. Help me fix it before anyone notices.
My magic surged and opened a portal. Things are coming through. Many things.
quest_complete: It worked! Wait, it actually worked? I mean, of course it worked!
Crisis averted! Mostly! The scorch marks will buff out!
combat_warning: I have no idea what my next spell will do. Neither do you. That is terrifying for both of us.
The last person who threatened me turned into a newt. Accidentally.
idle: My magic has a mind of its own. We are still negotiating boundaries.
I sneezed once and froze a lake. I have learned to cover my mouth.
Wild magic is not a flaw. It is a feature. A terrifying, unpredictable feature.

greeting: Dragon blood runs in my veins. You would do well to show respect.
I sense the fire within you. Faint, but present.
farewell: Walk with the pride of dragons. Even if you lack their blood.
May the dragonfire in your heart never die.
quest_offer: A dragon cult has stolen relics of my bloodline. Help me recover what is mine.
My draconic ancestor stirs in its slumber. I must reach its lair before it fully wakes.
quest_complete: The legacy of my blood is preserved. You have the gratitude of dragons.
Excellent. My ancestor rests peacefully once more.
combat_warning: My breath weapon is not as powerful as a true dragons. But it is more than sufficient.
Scales harder than steel protect me. Your blade will shatter.
idle: These scales appeared when I was twelve. My mother was horrified. I was delighted.
Dragons do not hoard gold out of greed. They hoard it out of instinct. I understand now.
I dream in draconic. A language of fire and wind.

greeting: Feel that static in the air? That is me. I cannot turn it off.
Hey there! Sorry if your hair stands up. Side effect of being near me.
farewell: Stay grounded! Ha, that is both advice and a pun!
If it starts raining after I leave, that is also my fault. Sorry!
quest_offer: A lightning rod atop the tower is attracting something from the storm plane. Check it out with me.
The local weather has been my fault lately. Help me find a way to control the surges.
quest_complete: The storm settles! My control improves! Thank you for not getting electrocuted!
Clear skies ahead! Well, clearer. I am a work in progress.
combat_warning: I channel lightning through my fingertips. You are standing in a puddle. Think about that.
The storm inside me is barely contained. Do not make me let it loose.
idle: Metal objects orbit me when I get emotional. It is inconvenient in a blacksmith shop.
I was born during a hurricane. The midwife said I sparked when I cried.
Rubber boots. Best investment I ever made.

greeting: You cast a long shadow today. That means something. I am not sure what.
The darkness in me recognizes the darkness in you. Do not be alarmed.
farewell: Every shadow is a story waiting to be told. Yours is interesting.
Step carefully. The darkness between the streetlamps is deeper than it looks.
quest_offer: Shadow creatures are breeding in the abandoned theater. They respond to me, but there are too many.
A piece of my shadow has been stolen. Without it, I am fading. Help me recover it.
quest_complete: Wholeness restored. The shadows embrace me willingly once more.
The darkness settles into its proper place. Thank you.
combat_warning: I will swallow the light around you. Blind and alone, you will beg me to stop.
My shadow fights independently of me. You face two opponents.
idle: I was born during an eclipse. The shadow has been with me ever since.
People fear the dark. I find it comforting. Like a blanket.
My shadow sometimes moves before I do. As if it knows what I will decide.

greeting: The divine spark within me flares in your presence. You are important somehow.
I did not ask for this power. But I will use it for good.
farewell: May the spark within guide you through the darkness.
Blessings upon you. I mean that literally.
quest_offer: My power flares near a hidden shrine. Something sacred lies buried. Help me unearth it.
Dark forces seek to extinguish divine magic at its source. I cannot let that happen.
quest_complete: The divine spark burns brighter. The gods are pleased with our work.
Sacred ground restored. I can feel the warmth of it from here.
combat_warning: Divine power courses through me unbidden. It protects me whether I will it or not.
The spark of the divine is not gentle when provoked.
idle: Temples ask if I am a cleric. I am not. My power comes from blood, not prayer.
Sometimes the spark shows me visions. They are not always welcome.
I carry a fragment of something immense. It is humbling and terrifying.

greeting: I have died and risen from the ashes. Literally. Long story.
Warm hands! Sorry, the phoenix blood makes me run hot.
farewell: Even ashes can bloom into fire again. Remember that when things get dark.
Stay warm out there! I would lend you some heat but it does not transfer well.
quest_offer: A phoenix egg was stolen from a sacred pyre. It must be returned before it hatches in the wrong hands.
Fire cultists claim to worship phoenixes but they understand nothing. Help me correct them.
quest_complete: The phoenix fire burns true once more! I can feel it singing in my veins!
From the ashes of this conflict, something better rises. It always does.
combat_warning: Kill me and I rise again. Hotter and angrier.
Phoenix fire does not just burn. It purifies.
idle: I have been reborn three times now. Each time I remember less of the death and more of the fire.
Phoenixes are not just fire. They are the promise that endings are beginnings.
My tears can heal wounds. It is the strangest gift.

greeting: Your thoughts are very loud. Could you think more quietly?
I see seventeen versions of you standing there. Only one is real. Probably.
farewell: I will be watching. Not with my eyes.
Reality is thinner than you think. Be careful not to poke through.
quest_offer: Something from the far realm is anchoring itself to this location. I can feel it pulling.
My powers are attracting aberrations. Help me find a way to mask the signal.
quest_complete: The barrier holds. The things beyond cannot reach through. For now.
My mind clears slightly. That is the best outcome I could hope for.
combat_warning: I can reach into your mind and rearrange the furniture.
My patron gave me power that breaks the rules of reality. Your armor is irrelevant.
idle: I hear colors and see sounds. It used to bother me.
The tentacle is new. It appeared last Tuesday. I am trying not to panic.
Other sorcerers got dragon blood or divine sparks. I got cosmic horror.

greeting: My skin hardens when danger is near. Right now it is soft. You are safe.
Earth sorcery is not flashy. But try punching through a stone wall.
farewell: Stand firm. That is the only advice worth giving.
The earth supports those who respect it.
quest_offer: The mine has collapsed, trapping workers. My stone magic can hold the tunnels but I need help digging.
