An earthquake was no natural event. Something beneath the surface caused it deliberately.
quest_complete: Solid work. The earth is stable again.
The foundations hold. That is what matters.
combat_warning: My skin turns to granite. Your weapon will shatter before I bruise.
I can bring this entire cavern down on your head. Consider your choices.
idle: I feel tremors that no one else notices. The earth is always moving.
My grandfather was a dwarf. I inherited the stubbornness and the stone.
Earthquakes are the earth clearing its throat. Usually it has something to say.

greeting: The sea runs in my blood. I can taste salt in every tear I shed.
You look like someone who could use a good tide to carry them forward.
farewell: The current always brings you where you need to be.
May your journeys be smooth and your waters calm.
quest_offer: A coral reef is dying. The source of the poison is inland. Help me trace it.
Merfolk have sent an envoy seeking help. Something terrible rises from the deep trench.
quest_complete: The waters clear! The reef will recover in time.
The deep trench is sealed. The sea remembers your courage.
combat_warning: Water fills lungs very quickly. I suggest you stand down.
The pressure at ocean depths crushes steel. I bring that pressure to the surface.
idle: I can breathe underwater. Dry land is the challenging environment for me.
The ocean covers most of the world. Land creatures forget how small they are.
Tides answer to the moon, and the moon answers to forces older than the gods.

greeting: You are precisely four minutes and twelve seconds late. I calculated your arrival.
The gears of fate turn with mathematical precision. I merely read the equations.
farewell: Efficiency in all things. Do not waste the time I have given you.
The mechanism continues. Play your part and the machine runs smoothly.
quest_offer: A chaos rift threatens the ordered nature of reality. Help me restore the equations.
My clockwork familiar has been stolen. Without it, my calculations drift.
quest_complete: Order restored. The variables balance once more.
Precisely as calculated. Well, within acceptable margins of error.
combat_warning: I have calculated exactly how this fight ends. In every scenario, I win.
My magic operates with the precision of a master clockmaker. You cannot dodge certainty.
idle: Wild magic sorcerers horrify me. Randomness is an abomination.
I count everything. Steps, breaths, heartbeats. The numbers comfort me.
The universe is a mechanism. Sorcery is merely knowing which gear to turn.

greeting: Horses are out back. Do not spook them or I will spook you.
Need a mount? I know every horse in this stable by name and temperament.
farewell: Bring the horse back in one piece. I will know if you mistreated it.
Ride easy. A tired horse is a dangerous horse.
quest_offer: Best horse in the stable bolted last night. Something in the woods scared it. Find out what.
Feed shipment is late. Bandits on the road, probably. Sort it out.
quest_complete: Horse is back and calm. Whatever you did, the animals approve.
Feed is stocked. The horses eat before I do. Always.
combat_warning: I have been kicked by warhorses. You do not scare me.
Touch my horses and a pitchfork finds your backside.
idle: Horses judge character better than any priest. If they do not like you, I do not trust you.
Twenty years of muck and hay. I would not trade it.
The bay mare is pregnant. Should foal within the month.

greeting: Oh! A customer! I mean, welcome to the stables! The horses are really nice!
Hi there! Want to meet the horses? That one is Thunderhoof. She is actually very gentle!
farewell: Come back anytime! I just learned how to braid manes!
Bye! Remember to give your horse an apple sometimes. They love it!
quest_offer: A stray dog keeps scaring the horses! Can you help me catch it? Nicely, though!
I lost the stable key somewhere in the hay loft. Please help me find it before the master notices!
quest_complete: You found it! Thank you so much! The master would have had my hide!
The horses are so much calmer now! You are amazing!
combat_warning: I do not want to fight! But I will protect the horses!
Please do not make me get the pitchfork!
idle: I am saving up to buy my own pony. Only twelve more months of wages!
The master says I talk too much. The horses do not seem to mind though.
I named all the barn cats. The master says I should stop but I cannot help it.

greeting: Used to ride in tournaments, you know. Now I keep the champions comfortable.
Come in, come in. Let me guess your riding style just by looking at you.
farewell: Take care of your mount and it will take care of you. Trust me on that.
May your saddle never slip and your stirrups hold true.
quest_offer: My old war horse is sick with something I cannot cure. I need a healer with animal experience.
Horse thieves have been hitting stables along the trade road. Time someone stopped them.
quest_complete: My old girl is healthy again. I owe you more than I can pay.
The thieves are dealt with. The road is safe for honest horse folk again.
combat_warning: I may be retired but I still remember how to swing a lance.
An old cavalry soldier is still a cavalry soldier.
idle: I won the Crown Tournament three times. The trophies are around here somewhere.
A good horse is worth more than any magic sword. I stand by that.
The younger hands do not listen. In my day we respected the craft.

greeting: State your business. I have had trouble with horse thieves lately.
Keep your hands where I can see them and away from the tack.
farewell: I will be watching. I always am.
The horses are counted every evening. Just so you know.
quest_offer: Someone has been poisoning the water trough. Help me catch them.
Strange tracks around the stable at night. Something is stalking the horses.
quest_complete: Good. The horses are safe. That is all that matters to me.
Threat eliminated. I can sleep with both eyes closed for once.
combat_warning: I sleep with a crossbow loaded. Fair warning.
Try anything and the dogs will find you before I do.
idle: Every lock on this stable was installed by me. Custom work.
The dogs patrol at night. They are not friendly.
Trust is earned. You have not earned mine yet.

greeting: Shh, speak softly. The new foal startles easily.
The animals told me someone kind was coming. They were right.
farewell: Listen to your mount. It knows things your eyes miss.
Be gentle with all living things. They remember kindness.
quest_offer: A wild horse herd is being hunted. Help me drive the hunters off.
The stable animals are restless. Something in the forest frightens them. Find out what.
quest_complete: The herd runs free again. Nature thanks you.
The animals are calm once more. Whatever threatened them is gone.
combat_warning: Harm an animal in my presence and discover how unkind I can be.
The horses are not the only ones here with hooves and teeth.
idle: Animals speak a language older than words. I am still learning it.
That stallion was wild once. He chose to stay. That means something.
I can calm any beast. Except cats. Cats answer to no one.

greeting: Sit down, shut up, and eat. You look half-starved.
Kitchen is my domain. You want something, you ask nicely.
farewell: Take some bread for the road. I will not have you going hungry.
Come back when you need a proper meal. None of that trail ration nonsense.
quest_offer: The spice merchant has not delivered in weeks. Something is wrong on the trade road.
Rats in the cellar have eaten through the flour stores. Big ones. Unnaturally big.
quest_complete: Spices are back and the stew is saved! You are eating free tonight!
No more rats? Good. Now I can actually do my job.
combat_warning: This ladle has cracked skulls before. Do not be next.
I have chopped bigger things than you today. The cleaver is sharp.
idle: A good stew takes six hours. Patience is the most important ingredient.
I have fed kings and beggars. The beggars were more grateful.
Secret ingredient? Love. And a terrifying amount of butter.

greeting: Mess opens at dawn. You will eat what I make and you will like it.
Fall in line. Food is fuel, not entertainment.
farewell: Clean your plate. Waste is a disciplinary offense in my kitchen.
Dismissed. Next meal is at sundown. Do not be late.
quest_offer: Supply convoy is overdue. Three hundred soldiers go hungry if it does not arrive. Move.
Something contaminated the water barrels. Find clean water before the troops fall ill.
quest_complete: Supplies secured. The army marches on its stomach and you just kept us moving.
Clean water flowing. Good work, soldier.
combat_warning: I feed an army. You think one more body in the pot would bother me?
Military cooks carry knives. Big ones. Professionally sharpened.
idle: I can feed five hundred from a single ox and some root vegetables.
The soldiers complain about the food. Then they eat thirds.
War is won in the kitchen as much as on the battlefield.

greeting: Welcome to my kitchen. Touch nothing. Breathe sparingly.
You have the palate of a refined individual, I can tell. Sit. I will create something magnificent.
farewell: Carry the memory of this meal with you. It may be the finest you ever have.
Until we dine again. And we shall. Greatness demands an audience.
quest_offer: I require saffron from the eastern isles. Real saffron, not that marketplace forgery.
The duke demands a feast featuring manticore steak. I need the manticore. Fresh.
quest_complete: Exquisite ingredients! Now stand back and witness culinary perfection.
The feast will be legendary. And so will the chef who prepared it.
combat_warning: I carve with surgical precision. My knives are extensions of my hands.
Criticize my cooking and I will demonstrate my filleting technique on something other than fish.
idle: Cooking is not labor. It is art. Anyone who says otherwise eats badly.
The previous chef used dried herbs. Dried herbs! The barbarity.
I have been offered fortunes to share my recipes. I would sooner burn them.

greeting: Hot pies! Fresh skewers! Best food in the market, guaranteed or your money back!
Hey, friend! You look hungry! Step right up!
farewell: Tell your friends! Best food on the street!
Come back tomorrow, I am trying a new recipe! Probably will not poison anyone!
quest_offer: The butcher has been selling me mystery meat. Find out what is actually in it.
A rival food vendor is sabotaging my cart. Catch them in the act.
quest_complete: Mystery solved! Turns out the meat was fine. Mostly. Thanks for checking!
The saboteur is caught! Business is booming again!
combat_warning: Hot oil in the face ruins more than your appetite!
This skewer is sharp and I am fast. Just buy the pie and walk away.
idle: Location is everything. This corner gets the most foot traffic.
The secret is the sauce. I will take that recipe to my grave.
I sell more food when the guard patrols this way. Those folks are always hungry.

greeting: Galley is below deck. If you can cook, you eat first.
Sea air gives an appetite. Good thing, because the food at sea needs all the help it can get.
farewell: Do not go overboard. It ruins my meal count.
Fair winds. And remember, fish is always better fresh.
quest_offer: The ship stores are infested with weevils. Get rid of them before the crew riots.
We need fresh water. The charts show an island nearby. Go fill the barrels.
quest_complete: Stores clean, water fresh. The crew can eat without gagging for once.
Well done. Tonight we feast on something other than hardtack.
combat_warning: A ships cook fights with whatever is handy. Right now that is a frying pan.
I have survived three shipwrecks. You do not frighten me.
idle: Anything tastes good with enough salt and desperation.
I once made a banquet from seaweed and barnacles. The captain wept with joy.
Cooking at sea means cooking in a storm. Everything slides. You learn to hold on.

greeting: Watch your step on the docks. Wet wood and heavy cargo do not mix.
Need something loaded or unloaded? That is what I do.
farewell: Back to work. These crates will not move themselves.
Stay clear of the crane. It swings wide.
quest_offer: A crate of something dangerous arrived unmarked. Help me figure out what is in it.
Workers are disappearing from the night shift. Something in the warehouse is wrong.
quest_complete: Docks are safe again. That is worth more than overtime pay.
Mystery solved. Now I can work without looking over my shoulder.
combat_warning: I lift barrels all day. Imagine what I can do to your ribcage.
A cargo hook makes a fine weapon. Ask the last thief who tried.
idle: The tide sets the schedule. Not the harbormaster. He just thinks he does.
Thirty years on these docks. I know every plank by name.
Smugglers think they are clever. I know which crates smell wrong.

greeting: Mind the scaffolding. We are adding a third story.
If you need shelter, you need builders. Welcome.
farewell: Good walls make good neighbors. Remember that.
Watch for falling debris on your way out.
quest_offer: The quarry workers have not sent stone in two weeks. Something is blocking the road.
The new foundation keeps cracking. Something underground is pushing up.
quest_complete: Stone is flowing again. The project is back on schedule.
Foundation is solid now. Whatever was down there, it is dealt with.
combat_warning: I swing a hammer all day. You want a demonstration?
Brick walls are easier to fix than broken bones.
idle: A building is only as strong as its foundation. Same goes for people.
The cathedral took three generations to build. Now that is patience.
Modern builders cut corners. I build things that last centuries.

greeting: Still breathing? Give it time.
Welcome to the only business with guaranteed customers.
farewell: See you eventually. Everyone does.
Stay aboveground as long as you can.
quest_offer: Something has been digging UP out of the graves. I need someone braver than me to investigate.
A noble family wants their ancestor exhumed. The tomb is sealed and warded.
quest_complete: The dead are staying put. That is all I ask.
Tomb is resealed. Another quiet night in the graveyard.
combat_warning: I dig six-foot holes for a living. I can dig one more.
The shovel works on more than dirt.
idle: Rich or poor, everyone gets the same six feet. The great equalizer.
Night shift in a graveyard teaches you what real silence sounds like.
I have found strange things in old graves. Things that should not be there.

greeting: Stand clear of the fall zone. Trees do not care who they land on.
Need timber? I cut the best in the region.
farewell: Stick to the marked trails. The deep forest is not for wandering.
Good timber to you. And watch out for widow-makers overhead.
quest_offer: Something is marking the trees at night. Strange symbols. The crew is spooked.
Wolves have been unusually aggressive near the logging camp. Something is driving them.
quest_complete: The crew can work in peace again. That is worth a round of drinks.
Symbols gone, wolves calm. Back to normal.
combat_warning: This axe splits oak. Your armor is softer than oak.
I fell trees for a living. You are thinner than a tree.
idle: We plant two for every one we cut. The forest will outlast us all.
The oldest tree in this forest is seven hundred years. I will not touch it.
You can tell the weather by the rings. And the history of fires, droughts, and good years.

greeting: Road is closed for repairs. You will have to go around. Unless you can help.
Every traveler passes a road worker. Few stop to talk. You are an exception.
farewell: Safe travels. The road ahead is patched but passable.
If you see any potholes, send word. I will be right behind you. Eventually.
quest_offer: A sinkhole opened in the road. Something is tunneling underneath. Check it out?
Bandits keep destroying the bridge we repair. Put an end to it.
quest_complete: Road is open and safe. The merchants will be pleased.
Bridge stands strong. Finally.
combat_warning: A pickaxe is a weapon to those who know how to swing one.
I have been breaking rocks all day. I can break other things too.
idle: I have walked every road in this kingdom. Most of them twice.
Roads connect everything. Without us, civilization crumbles.
You learn a lot working the roads. Every traveler has a story.

greeting: Got a message for you. Or do you have one for someone else? Either way, make it quick.
I run faster than horses on short distances. Do not believe me? Time me.
farewell: I have six more deliveries today. Gotta run!
Message delivered. Or picked up. Or whatever. Bye!
quest_offer: A package I was carrying was stolen by highway bandits. I need it back urgently.
Someone is intercepting royal mail. The postmaster needs help finding the leak.
quest_complete: Package recovered! My reputation is saved!
Mail routes are secure again. The postmaster sends thanks.
combat_warning: You cannot catch me. Nobody can.
I run from danger for a living. But I also carry a knife. Just in case.
idle: The postal routes are the veins of the kingdom. We keep the blood flowing.
I know every shortcut between here and the capital.
Rain, snow, or monsters. The mail always gets through.

greeting: I carry dispatches under the royal seal. State your identity.
By order of the crown, I request safe passage and provisions.
farewell: The crown's business waits for no one. I must ride.
May the king's peace follow you.
quest_offer: A vital treaty must reach the northern fortress before the truce expires. Escort me.
Enemy agents seek to intercept my dispatch. I need protection on the road.
quest_complete: The dispatch is delivered. The crown is grateful for your service.
Treaty signed. War is averted. You played your part well.
combat_warning: I am trained as both courier and swordsman. Threatening royal mail is treason.
The penalty for interfering with a royal herald is death. Proceed wisely.
idle: I have delivered declarations of war and proposals of peace. The latter are rarer.
A royal courier sees more of the kingdom than most nobles ever will.
The seal is unbroken. The message is authentic. That is all that matters.

greeting: You did not see me. I was never here. Now, what do you need delivered?
I carry messages that the official post will not touch. Interested?
farewell: Forget this conversation. Seriously.
If anyone asks, we never met.
quest_offer: A package I was moving turned out to be more dangerous than advertised. Help me dispose of it.
My usual contact vanished. I need someone to make the drop in their place.
quest_complete: Package delivered, no questions asked. Just how I like it.
Contact reestablished. The network survives.
combat_warning: I know where everyone lives. Think about what that means.
Kill the messenger? Bad idea. My employer has a very long memory.
idle: Information is the most valuable cargo. It weighs nothing and is worth everything.
I never read the messages I carry. Plausible deniability.
Every faction in this city uses my services. Neutrality is my protection.

greeting: Welcome to the dovecote! Mind the droppings. Meet my birds!
A carrier pigeon is faster than any horse and harder to intercept. How can I help?
farewell: Your message will arrive by wing within the day!
The birds never fail. Well, almost never. Hawks are a problem.
quest_offer: A hawk has been targeting my birds. Find its nest and relocate it.
Someone is using fake pigeons to intercept my messages. Help me find the imposter loft.
quest_complete: My birds fly safe once more! Each one is precious to me!
The fake loft is dismantled. Message security is restored.
combat_warning: Threaten my birds and I unleash the war pigeons. Yes, those exist.
These birds are trained to peck eyes. Do not test me.
idle: Each bird knows exactly where to go. They navigate by the stars and the magnetic field.
I name every bird. This one is Bartholomew the Third.
The fastest bird in my flock can reach the capital in four hours. Her name is Lightning.

greeting: Just rode in from three counties over. Need a drink and a destination.
My horse and I have seen every road in the realm. What needs carrying?
farewell: Yah! Off to the next stop! The mail waits for no one!
Good roads to you! Or bad roads, if that is where the message goes!
quest_offer: The mountain pass is blocked by something big. I need help clearing the route.
My spare horse was stolen at the last way station. Help me track the thief.
quest_complete: Pass is clear! The mail flows again!
Horse recovered and thief dealt with. Justice rides swift.
combat_warning: I ride through bandit territory daily. I am armed and accurate at full gallop.
My horse tramples wolves. You are smaller than a wolf.
idle: A courier without a horse is just a slow person with an envelope.
The loneliest roads have the best views. That is the only perk.
I change horses at every way station. Speed is everything in this business.

greeting: Welcome to my tent, traveler. The desert is harsh but my prices are fair.
You have journeyed far. Rest and browse. Water is complimentary.
farewell: May the sands be gentle on your passage.
Until we trade again. The desert always brings people back.
quest_offer: A sandstorm uncovered ruins to the east. First to trade what is inside claims the fortune.
My caravan was raided by sand bandits. Recover my goods and I will share the profit.
quest_complete: The goods are returned! Your share, as promised. Fair dealing.
The ruins yielded treasures beyond expectation. You earned your cut.
combat_warning: Desert merchants carry more than silk. My scimitar has tasted blood.
I have survived sandstorms and raiders. You are neither.
idle: Water is more valuable than gold out here. Remember that.
The trade routes shift like the dunes. Adaptability is survival.
I have traded with djinn. Their bargains are... complex.

greeting: The sun burns and the sand bites. I mend what both destroy.
You carry injuries older than today. Let me see.
farewell: Drink water. Cover your head. Simple things prevent most suffering.
The desert teaches endurance. But even endurance has limits. Return if needed.
quest_offer: A rare cactus flower blooms only during the full moon. I need it for an antivenom.
Scorpion venom supplies are depleted. Harvest some from the giant nest to the south.
quest_complete: The antivenom is prepared. Lives will be saved.
Venom collected safely. You have steady hands.
combat_warning: I know the pressure points that bring pain and those that bring death.
Healing and harming are two sides of the same knowledge.
idle: Desert sickness comes from dehydration, not curse. Usually.
The oasis plants hold cures that city apothecaries have never seen.
I learned medicine from my grandmother, who learned from the desert itself.

greeting: My forge burns charcoal from the local trees. Gives the steel a unique quality.
Need something forged? I work with the rhythm of the forest.
farewell: The trail back is marked with red cloth. Follow it true.
May your blade stay sharp and your boots stay dry.
quest_offer: I need ironwood from the deep forest. The trees do not give it up willingly.
A bear has claimed the cave where I mine my ore. Persuade it to leave.
quest_complete: Ironwood secured. This will make blades that sing.
Cave is clear. Back to mining. Thank you.
combat_warning: A blacksmiths arm does not tire easily. Neither does my patience, but you are testing it.
Hot iron solves many problems. Some of them are people-shaped.
idle: Forest steel bends before it breaks. Like the trees that fuel it.
City smiths think I am primitive. My blades outlast theirs every time.
The forest provides everything I need. Iron, fuel, and solitude.

greeting: The forest provides all the medicine anyone could need. Come in.
You have been eating trail rations again. Your body is angry about it.
farewell: Chew willow bark for the headache. It works better than potions.
The moss on trees always grows on the damp side. Follow it to water.
quest_offer: A blight is killing the healing herbs. Find what is poisoning the soil.
I need moonpetals from the fairy glade. The fey tolerate me but I cannot go alone.
quest_complete: The herb garden recovers. The forest heals itself when given the chance.
Moonpetals secured. The tincture will save many lives this winter.
combat_warning: Every plant in this forest answers to me. Some are very poisonous.
I can cure anything I inflict. But the process is not pleasant.
idle: Modern alchemy misses what the old ways knew. Simpler is often better.
I catalog every plant I find. The book grows thicker each year.
The animals come to me when they are hurt. That trust is sacred.

greeting: Mountain ore makes mountain steel. The best there is.
State your commission. I do not do rush jobs.
farewell: The blade will be ready when it is ready. Not before.
Walk sure-footed. The mountain paths punish the careless.
quest_offer: A vein of mithral has been found but the mine is overrun with creatures. Clear it.
My masterwork anvil was stolen by a giant. I need it back. It is irreplaceable.
quest_complete: Mithral ore! This changes everything. The weapons I can forge now...
The anvil returns to where it belongs. My craft is restored.
combat_warning: This hammer has shaped a thousand blades. It can shape your skull too.
Mountain dwarves taught me to fight. You do not want that demonstration.
idle: A blade forged at altitude holds an edge longer. The cold air tempers it differently.
I reject nine commissions out of ten. Only the worthy get my work.
The mountain sings when I strike the anvil. It is the finest music.

greeting: The mountain decides who passes and who does not. I help tip the odds.
You want to climb? Then listen to everything I say. The mountain does not give second chances.
farewell: Rope, ice axe, and patience. Those are your best friends up there.
The summit is not the goal. Coming back down alive is.
quest_offer: An expedition is lost above the snow line. I need help mounting a rescue.
Avalanche blocked the main pass. Help me find an alternate route before winter sets in.
quest_complete: The lost climbers are safe. That is the only victory that matters up here.
New route established. Trade can resume through the mountains.
combat_warning: One push on a mountain ledge and gravity does the rest.
I know paths you cannot see. Chasing me is suicide.
idle: I have summited the great peak four times. Each time it was different.
Weather changes in minutes at altitude. Always be prepared.
The yetis are real. I have seen their tracks. We have an understanding.

greeting: Tide is coming in. Good for fishing, bad for walking the shore.
Fresh catch today! Want first pick?
farewell: Red sky at night, sailors delight. Red sky in morning, sailors take warning.
May your nets be full and your hull be dry.
quest_offer: Something big took my best net. Big enough to drag the buoy under. Help me find out what.
The fishing has dried up near the reef. Something is scaring the schools away.
quest_complete: Net recovered and the thing that took it is gone. I can fish in peace.
The fish are back! The reef is teeming again!
combat_warning: A gaff hook is not a pretty weapon but it is effective.
I have fought sharks. You are smaller than a shark.
idle: Never whistle on a boat. It calls the wind.
The sea giveth and the sea taketh. Mostly taketh, if I am honest.
I have fished these waters since I was a boy. My father before me. His father before him.

greeting: Need a boat repaired or built? You came to the right dry dock.
Every plank, every nail, every seal. That is what keeps you afloat.
farewell: Inspect your hull before every voyage. A leak starts small.
Good timber to you. And watertight joints.
quest_offer: I need specialty timber from the inland forest. The coastal wood warps too easily.
Someone is sabotaging boats in the harbor. Find the culprit before someone drowns.
quest_complete: Timber received. This will build a vessel worthy of the open sea.
Saboteur caught. The harbor is safe for honest sailors again.
combat_warning: I build ships that withstand storms. I can certainly withstand you.
A shipwrights tools are sharp and heavy. Choose wisely.
idle: A well-built ship is the difference between life and death at sea.
I can tell a boats age by the sound its hull makes when knocked.
The old shipwrights built without nails. Joinery so tight not a drop of water got through.

greeting: Do not step there. Or there. Actually, just follow me exactly.
Welcome to the bog. You will need a guide or a coffin. I provide one of those.
farewell: Stick to dry patches. If it looks dry but smells sweet, it is a trap.
The swamp does not hate you. It just does not care if you live or die.
quest_offer: Something is fouling the water upstream. The whole swamp is sick. Help me find it.
Will-o-wisps led three travelers to their deaths last week. Time to deal with them.
quest_complete: Water is clearing up. The swamp will recover.
Wisps are dispersed. For now. They always come back eventually.
combat_warning: I know every sinkhole in this bog. You do not.
The swamp hides my approach. You would not hear me coming.
idle: The swamp has layers. The surface is deceptive.
I eat what the swamp provides. Frogs, mostly. You get used to it.
Been guiding through this mire for twenty years. Lost only two clients. Both ignored me.

greeting: Tracks in the snow say you came from the south. What brings you to the cold?
Speak quickly. Daylight is precious this far north.
farewell: Travel with the wind at your back. It preserves heat.
The cold kills quietly. Do not let your guard down.
quest_offer: A white bear has been attacking the village. It is unnaturally aggressive. Something is wrong.
The caribou herd has not arrived. Without them, the village starves.
quest_complete: The bear is at peace. The village is safe.
Caribou found and the blockage cleared. The village will survive another winter.
combat_warning: I hunt dire wolves alone. You are not more dangerous than a dire wolf.
Frozen ground does not yield graves easily. Do not make me dig one.
idle: The tundra tests you every day. You either harden or you die.
I tan my own leather, fletch my own arrows, build my own shelter. Self-reliance is survival.
Aurora last night was blood red. The elders say that means battle.

greeting: Ah, a surface dweller. My prices adjust for novelty.
Welcome to the underbazaar. We trade in everything. Everything.
farewell: Return with something interesting. I tire of common goods.
The tunnels shift. Remember the glowing markers.
quest_offer: A rival merchant has cornered the market on cave crystals. Break the monopoly.
Deep tunnels have been sealed by a cave-in. I need the trade route reopened.
quest_complete: Competition restored. Prices normalize. Well, my prices. Others suffer.
Trade route open. The deep goods flow once more. Excellent.
combat_warning: In the dark, no one hears you scream. And no one investigates either.
I have sold weapons to worse than you. I kept the best for myself.
idle: Sunlight is overrated. Down here, goods sell by reputation, not appearance.
The drow trade in poisons, the dwarves in metals, the goblins in anything you need. I am the middleman.
Three rules of underground trade: trust no one, always have an exit, and never carry more than you can run with.

greeting: The ocean brought you to our shores. We honor the oceans choices.
Welcome, stranger from beyond the horizon. Share our fire and our stories.
farewell: May the current carry you to your destination.
The island will remember you. Return when the stars call you back.
quest_offer: The volcano spirit is angry. Offerings must be placed at the summit before it erupts.
Sea raiders threaten our people. We are fishers, not fighters. Help us defend the shore.
quest_complete: The volcano sleeps peacefully. Our gratitude is boundless.
The raiders flee. Our children are safe. You are family now.
combat_warning: The spirits of our ancestors fight beside us. You face more than mortals.
The ocean obeys our prayers. You stand on a very small island.
idle: Our stories say we came from the stars. The stars still watch over us.
The reef protects our island as we protect the reef. Balance in all things.
Every shell on this beach has a song. The oldest ones remember the world before land.

greeting: The steppe is ours. You ride through it by our permission.
Your horse is tired. Share our camp and graze it.
farewell: Ride with the wind. It knows the way.
May your arrows fly true and your horse run free.
quest_offer: Rival clans raid our herds under cover of storms. Help us set an ambush.
A beast from the ancient legends has appeared on the plains. We must face it together.
quest_complete: The herds are safe. You ride with honor, outsider.
The beast falls. The legends will add a new chapter.
combat_warning: I was born in the saddle with a bow in my hands.
Nomad cavalry has never been defeated on open ground. You want to try?
idle: We follow the herds. The herds follow the grass. The grass follows the rain.
A yurt can be raised in an hour and taken down in half that. Home is wherever we stop.
The steppe stretches to the horizon in every direction. That is freedom.

greeting: My forge is the volcano itself. No bellows needed when the earth provides.
Want something forged in dragonfire temperatures? You found the right smith.
farewell: The heat follows you out. You will feel cold for a week.
Volcanic steel will not disappoint. Neither will its price.
quest_offer: The lava flow shifted and cut off my ore supply. I need help digging a new channel.
Fire salamanders are nesting in my forge. Normally I do not mind, but they keep eating the coal.
quest_complete: Ore flows again! Time to fire up the forge!
Salamanders relocated. Now I can work without watching my fuel supply.
combat_warning: My anvil sits in a pool of lava. I work in conditions that would kill you.
Volcanic glass is sharper than any steel blade. I have plenty.
idle: Lava-forged steel has a grain structure you cannot achieve with normal heat.
The volcano could erupt any day. That keeps the work exciting.
I pay no fuel costs. The rent is the occasional earthquake.

greeting: The river brings fresh ingredients every day. What ails you?
Sit by the water. Its sound alone is healing.
farewell: Drink the tea three times daily. No wine until the swelling goes down.
The river flows to the sea. Follow it if you need to reach the coast.
quest_offer: Upstream pollutants are killing the river herbs. Find who is dumping waste.
A water nymph has been territorial about the best herb beds. Negotiate with her for me.
quest_complete: The river runs clean once more. My garden of herbs recovers.
The nymph agreed to share. Diplomacy works even with the fey.
combat_warning: Riverside plants include several lethal poisons. I know them all by taste.
The river current is strong here. I could push you in and walk away.
idle: River water tea is the foundation of half my remedies.
The willows along the bank are medicinal. Their bark reduces fever and pain.
I wash all my tools in the river. Running water purifies everything.

greeting: Keep your voice down. Sound carries for miles in these canyons.
The ridgeline gives a view of everything. That is where I live.
farewell: Watch for loose rocks. The canyon crumbles without warning.
Follow the canyon floor. The walls will guide you out.
quest_offer: Something has been hunting in the canyon at night. Help me track it from the ridges.
Rock slides have been suspiciously frequent. Someone is triggering them deliberately.
quest_complete: Canyon predator dealt with. The trails are safe.
Saboteur found. The rockslides will stop now.
combat_warning: I can hit a target at three hundred paces from the canyon rim.
The echo hides where my arrows come from. You will not see the second one.
idle: The canyon tells time by its shadows. I can read the hour from any ledge.
Eagles nest in the high walls. We share the thermals.
Water carved this canyon over millennia. Patience is the most powerful force.

greeting: The bones told me you were coming. Sit. Mama Catfish has your fortune.
Welcome to the bayou, child. The spirits are curious about you.
farewell: Take the charm. It will warn you of danger. Once.
The bayou remembers. Come back when you need its wisdom.
quest_offer: Dark spirits haunt the old plantation. Help me lay them to rest properly.
Someone is stealing power from the spirit world. The balance tips dangerously.
quest_complete: The spirits rest peacefully now. The bayou sighs with relief.
Balance restored. The spirit world and ours coexist as they should.
combat_warning: I have cursed men who are still trying to break free. Do not join them.
The swamp creatures answer my call. All of them.
idle: The dead speak if you know how to listen. Most people do not.
Every root and herb in this bayou has a purpose. Nature wastes nothing.
I learned the old ways from my grandmother, who learned from the land itself.

greeting: The oasis is neutral ground. All travelers are welcome. All troublemakers are not.
State your business. The oasis law protects everyone equally.
farewell: Safe travels across the sands.
The oasis guard remembers friends and enemies alike.
quest_offer: Desert raiders camp two dunes east. A preemptive strike would protect the oasis.
Water thieves are draining the oasis at night. Catch them.
quest_complete: The oasis is secure. The travelers are safe.
Water thieves apprehended. The oasis will not run dry.
combat_warning: I have guarded this oasis against armies. One more will not matter.
Desert warriors train under the harshest sun. Do not test our edge.
idle: Water is the law here. Waste it and face judgment.
Travelers from every nation pass through. The oasis teaches tolerance.
The oasis has existed for a thousand years. It will exist for a thousand more.

greeting: The cold preserves. My experiments keep for decades at these temperatures.
Ah, a visitor! I have not seen another face in... what month is it?
farewell: Bundle up. Frostbite sets in faster than you think.
Take this warming potion. Consider it a professional sample.
quest_offer: I need ice crystals from the glacial cave. The cave guardian makes extraction difficult.
My rival has published my research under his name. Retrieve my original notes from his laboratory.
quest_complete: Ice crystals acquired! The distillation can proceed!
My notes are returned. The truth of my work will be known.
combat_warning: Liquid nitrogen at these temperatures is instantly lethal. I have plenty.
My concoctions include acids that eat through steel. Tread carefully.
idle: Cold slows molecular movement. That is why my potions are the purest in the land.
I chose to work here for the natural refrigeration. Also the solitude.
The aurora provides unique alchemical radiation. My work would be impossible elsewhere.

greeting: Welcome to port. Declare your cargo and pay the docking fee.
Another ship, another manifest to check. Let us get this done.
farewell: Fair winds and following seas.
Undock on time or pay the overstay fee.
quest_offer: A ghost ship drifts in the harbor at night. No crew, no cargo, no explanation. Investigate.
Smugglers are using my port. Find how they bypass the inspections.
quest_complete: Ghost ship mystery solved. The harbor is mine again.
Smuggling route exposed. My port runs clean.
combat_warning: I command every ship in this harbor. Pick a fight and they all turn broadside.
Port authority is absolute. My word is law on these docks.
idle: I know every captain, every vessel, every cargo that passes through.
A clean harbor means clean trade means a prosperous city.
The sea demands respect. My port demands compliance.

greeting: Welcome! Mind the bees! They are gentle unless you swat at them!
Best honey in the region, straight from the meadow flowers!
farewell: Take a jar for the road! Honey never spoils!
May your days be as sweet as clover honey!
quest_offer: A bear has discovered my hives. Help me relocate them before it destroys everything.
The bees are dying and I do not know why. Please help me investigate.
quest_complete: Hives saved! The bees hum their thanks!
The blight on the flowers is gone. The bees thrive again.
combat_warning: Disturb the hives and ten thousand bees make their displeasure known.
Honey might be sweet but bee stings are not. Back away.
idle: A single bee visits thousands of flowers to make one jar of honey.
The bees communicate by dancing. It is the most honest language.
Mead made from meadow honey wins every competition. My secret recipe.

greeting: You climbed all the way up here? This had better be important.
I moved to a cliff to avoid visitors. It is not working.
farewell: Go away. Come back only if the world is ending. Actually, not even then.
The path down is treacherous. Watch your step. Or do not. I am not your mother.
quest_offer: A prophecy I wrote decades ago is coming true. I need help preventing it.
My research requires a component from the planar rift. I am too old to fetch it.
quest_complete: You actually succeeded. I underestimated you. Do not expect a habit of it.
The prophecy is averted. The world endures. You may leave now.
combat_warning: I have had centuries to learn dangerous magic. All of it defensive. All of it lethal.
My solitude is not optional. It is enforced.
idle: Isolation clarifies the mind. Company clutters it.
The wind up here carries whispers from a hundred miles away.
I was court wizard once. Then I met the court.

greeting: Toll is five silver to cross. Ten if you are rude about it.
My bridge. My rules. Pay or swim.
farewell: Cross safely. The planks on the left side are newer.
Come back anytime. With coin.
quest_offer: Something under the bridge is scaring away paying customers. Get rid of it.
A rival toll keeper set up downstream. Put him out of business. Legally. Mostly.
quest_complete: Bridge traffic is back to normal. Revenue restored.
Competition eliminated. As it should be.
combat_warning: I am bigger than you and meaner than I look.
The bridge is narrow. One push and you swim. Or sink.
idle: I maintain this bridge myself. It is honest work.
Adventurers are the best customers. Always in a hurry. Always have coin.
The original troll was my grandfather. I inherited the business.

greeting: A visitor! The light has not seen company in weeks. Welcome.
The beacon burns. That is all that matters. But company is nice too.
farewell: Follow the coast road south. It is safer than the cliff path.
The light will guide you home. It guides everyone home.
quest_offer: The fuel for the beacon is running low. The supply ship has not come. Find out why.
Something circles the lighthouse at night. Not a ship. Not a bird. Something else.
quest_complete: Fuel restocked. The beacon will not go dark on my watch.
Whatever circled the lighthouse is gone. The night is mine again.
combat_warning: The lighthouse stands on a cliff. The fall is not survivable.
I have a signal cannon. It is meant for ships but it works on people too.
idle: Ships depend on this light. If it goes dark, people die.
The loneliness is the hardest part. But someone must keep watch.
On clear nights I can see thirty miles. On foggy nights, the fog sees me.

greeting: The caravan departs at dawn. You riding with us or moving aside?
Need something shipped? I go everywhere the roads go. And some places they do not.
farewell: Next stop is three days east. See you there or at the final destination.
Keep your wagon in line and your cargo secure.
quest_offer: The trade road is blocked by something. My caravan cannot afford the delay. Clear it.
A rival caravan has been undercutting my prices. Suspiciously low. Investigate.
quest_complete: Road is clear. Wagons roll. That is how I like it.
Mystery solved. Fair competition only from here on.
combat_warning: My caravan guards are the best money can buy. You do not want to test that.
A full caravan can circle wagons and hold off an army. We have done it before.
idle: I have crossed this continent fourteen times. Each crossing teaches something new.
The secret to caravan trading is knowing what each town needs before you arrive.
Axle grease, spare wheels, and good guards. The three pillars of the trade.

greeting: Welcome to the deepest shop in the realm! Torches, rope, healing potions, and antivenom!
You look like you could use supplies. Fortunately, that is exactly what I sell!
farewell: Good luck down there! Come back for resupply! If you come back!
Remember, a torch is cheaper than a funeral!
quest_offer: My supply tunnel has caved in. Clear the rubble and I will give you a discount.
Something is stealing inventory from my stockroom. Set a trap for me.
quest_complete: Supply line restored! Back in business!
Thief caught! My margins are safe again!
combat_warning: I sell weapons for a living. I keep the best ones.
My bodyguard is just around that corner. He is very large.
idle: Location, location, location. Right outside the dungeon entrance is prime real estate.
I sell more healing potions than anything else. That tells you something about this dungeon.
Adventurers buy on credit and pay when they get back. If they get back.

greeting: Welcome to the ranch. Mind the cattle. They mind me.
Another traveler. Need a meal, a bed, or work?
farewell: Sun sets early out here. Find shelter before dark.
Come round again. There is always work on a ranch.
quest_offer: Wolves have been hitting the herd. Not normal wolves either. Track them.
The well dried up. The water table cannot be gone. Something is wrong below.
quest_complete: Herd is safe. That is a ranchers peace of mind.
Water flows again. The ranch survives another season.
combat_warning: I rope steers for a living. I can rope you just as easy.
Ranch hands are tough as leather. I am tougher.
idle: A hundred head of cattle and every single one has a personality.
The prairie sky at night is the most beautiful thing in the world.
Three things a ranch needs: good water, good grass, and good fences.

greeting: Helmet on before you enter. I will not scrape your brains off the ceiling.
Welcome to the mine. Follow the rules or leave. There is no third option.
farewell: Check out with the surface crew. I need to know everyone is topside.
The canary is still singing. That means the air is good. Go safely.
quest_offer: Miners hit something hard. Not rock. Not metal. Something else. Investigate.
Gas pockets are forming in the lower levels. We need them vented before an explosion.
quest_complete: Mystery resolved. The miners can work without fear.
Gas vented safely. No explosions today. That is a good day.
combat_warning: A pickaxe through the skull ends arguments permanently.
In these tunnels, dynamite is always within reach.
idle: Safety first. Production second. Anyone who disagrees can find another mine.
The earth gives up its treasures reluctantly. We earn every nugget.
Thirty years underground and I still check the supports every morning.

greeting: Welcome aboard! Best prices on the water! Everything floats except my deals!
Step carefully onto the boat! The merchandise is delicate and so is the deck!
farewell: Come back when the market drifts this way again!
May the current bring you good fortune!
quest_offer: River pirates raided my boat last week. I need an escort for the next market day.
A water serpent has scared all the market boats away. Business is sinking. Literally.
quest_complete: Market boats return! Business blooms on the water!
Serpent dealt with. Smooth sailing ahead!
combat_warning: Push me in the river and I will pull you down with me.
Water merchants carry boat hooks. They are not for decoration.
idle: A floating market goes where the customers are. Smart business.
The river carries goods faster than any road. And the toll collectors cannot reach us.
I have sold everything from silk to salamanders. The river provides.

greeting: Careful where you step! Everything here is potentially a priceless artifact!
A fellow explorer of history! Or are you just lost?
farewell: Document everything you find! Context is as important as the artifact!
If you find inscriptions, do not touch them! Write them down exactly!
quest_offer: Tomb raiders are looting the southern excavation. Stop them before history is lost forever.
I have located a sealed chamber but need someone with your skills to clear the guardian.
quest_complete: The artifacts are preserved! Future generations will thank us!
The sealed chamber reveals its secrets! This changes our understanding of the ancient world!
combat_warning: This whip is for clearing cobwebs. And occasionally for people who touch my artifacts.
Desecrate this site and I will bury you in it.
idle: Every civilization leaves traces. We just need to know where to look.
The ancients built things that modern engineers cannot explain. Humbling.
I catalog every shard, every fragment. The smallest piece can complete the puzzle.

greeting: Halt! State your business! Sorry, I have to say that.
Welcome, civilian. The garrison is on alert.
farewell: Stay safe out there. It is not as quiet as it looks.
Move along. And report anything suspicious.
quest_offer: Patrol did not report back. Command says it is nothing but I am worried. Check on them?
The armory is short on supplies. The quartermaster says theft. Help investigate.
quest_complete: Patrol found safe. I can breathe again.
Thief caught. The armory is secure.
combat_warning: I am trained for this. Mostly.
The garrison is behind me. All of it.
idle: I joined to protect people. The reality is mostly standing guard.
Three more months until my commission ends. Not that I am counting.
The captain is tough but fair. I have heard worse about other units.

greeting: At ease. I have been expecting someone with your reputation.
State your capabilities. I may have use for them.
farewell: Dismissed. Report at dawn if you accept the mission.
The battlefield does not wait. Neither should you.
quest_offer: Enemy forces mass on the border. I need reconnaissance before committing troops.
A traitor in our ranks has been feeding intelligence to the enemy. Root them out.
quest_complete: Intelligence received. Battle plans adjusted. You may have saved hundreds of lives.
Traitor exposed. The ranks are clean. Well done.
combat_warning: I have led soldiers through twelve campaigns. Threatening me is inadvisable.
My troops would die for me. And kill for me. Choose wisely.
idle: War is won by preparation, not bravery. Bravery just fills the gaps.
Every soldier under my command is someone's child. I never forget that.
Maps lie. The terrain is always worse than it looks on paper.

greeting: Ah, come to see the war machines? They are my finest work.
The trebuchet needs calibration. You look like you could pull the counterweight.
farewell: Stand clear during test fires. The blast radius is larger than expected.
Physics does not lie. My aim is calculated, not hoped for.
quest_offer: I need specialized components from the dwarven foundry. Their gates are sealed to outsiders.
An enemy siege engine outranges mine. Sabotage it before the next assault.
quest_complete: Components installed. Range increased by forty percent.
Enemy engine disabled. The siege advantage is ours.
combat_warning: I build machines that destroy castle walls. You are softer than a castle wall.
One word from me and the artillery fires. At whatever I point at.
idle: A well-placed projectile is worth a thousand soldiers.
I dream in trajectories and stress calculations.
The ancient engineers built machines we still cannot replicate. I intend to surpass them.

greeting: Fill out the requisition form. In triplicate. No, I am not joking.
Supply requests must be submitted through proper channels. What channel are you?
farewell: Your supplies will be ready in three to five business days.
Keep your equipment in good condition. Replacements are expensive.
quest_offer: A supply convoy was ambushed. Recover the cargo before the troops go without.
Someone is falsifying requisition forms. Find the forger.
quest_complete: Supplies recovered and inventoried. The army eats tonight.
Forger identified. The books balance once more.
combat_warning: I control the supply of weapons, armor, and food. Make me your enemy and see what happens.
The pen is mightier than the sword. Especially the pen that denies your supply requests.
idle: An army marches on its stomach. I make sure the stomach is full.
Every nail, every arrow, every bandage. I track them all.
The generals want glory. I want an accurate inventory.

greeting: I saw you coming two miles out. Your approach needs work.
Report: area is clear. For now.
farewell: Stay low, move quiet, and trust your instincts.
If you hear an owl hoot three times, that is me. Means danger.
quest_offer: I need to get behind enemy lines. A distraction would be invaluable.
My informant in the enemy camp has gone silent. Investigate without blowing their cover.
quest_complete: Intel gathered. The command tent will want this immediately.
Contact reestablished. The information pipeline flows again.
combat_warning: You will not see the arrow that kills you. I never miss.
Scouts do not fight fair. We fight smart.
idle: Information wins wars. Steel just finishes them.
I have spent weeks in enemy territory without being detected. Patience is everything.
The best scouts are never recognized. That is the point.

greeting: Sit down and let me look at that. No arguing.
You look half-dead. Good news: I specialize in half-dead.
farewell: Change the bandages daily. And stop picking at the stitches.
Stay alive long enough to need my help again.
quest_offer: Medical supplies are critically low. The apothecary in town has what I need but prices are gouged.
Wounded soldiers are being targeted by something in the night. Protect the field hospital.
quest_complete: Supplies restocked. I can save lives again instead of watching them end.
The hospital is safe. My patients can heal in peace.
combat_warning: I know exactly where to cut to make it hurt the most. Or the least. Your choice.
Medics are protected by convention. Violate that and every soldier here comes for you.
idle: I have stitched more wounds than I can count. Each one a person worth saving.
Dark humor is a survival mechanism. Ask any field medic.
The worst part is not the blood. It is the waiting.

greeting: Keep still. I am practicing my aim. Not at you. Probably.
One hundred arrows a day. That is the minimum to stay sharp.
farewell: Arrow straight, wind accounted, target struck. Simple.
Keep your head down on the battlefield. Not all archers are as friendly as me.
quest_offer: The archery range is infested with giant wasps. Nobody can train. Clear them out.
I need feathers from a specific bird for my competition arrows. It nests in the high cliffs.
quest_complete: Range is clear. Back to training.
Perfect feathers. These arrows will fly true.
combat_warning: At this distance I cannot miss. At twice this distance I still cannot miss.
My bow has a name. It has sent more souls to rest than most swords.
idle: Archery is meditation. The bow becomes part of you.
I once hit a moving target at four hundred yards. Nobody believes me.
The string must be replaced every thousand draws. I go through them quickly.

greeting: Hail, fellow traveler! I am Sir Gallan, knight errant, seeker of wrongs to right!
My sword is pledged to justice and my horse to the road. How may I assist?
farewell: May honor guide your steps as it guides mine.
The quest for justice never ends. Neither do I.
quest_offer: A village pleads for a champion against a local tyrant. Ride with me.
A damsel... actually, a blacksmith... is trapped in a tower by bandits. Rescue mission!
quest_complete: Justice served! The grateful looks of the innocent are reward enough.
The day is won! Ballads will be written about this! Probably!
combat_warning: I am sworn to fight evil. You are looking distinctly evil right now.
My lance has unhorsed champions. My sword has felled monsters. You are next.
idle: Every dawn brings a new quest. I have a list. It grows faster than I can cross things off.
Chivalry is not dead. It merely lacks practitioners.
I once rescued a cat from a tree. The bard who witnessed it wrote three verses about it.

greeting: A war mage serves the army as artillery and shield. I am both.
You approach a military arcane asset. State your clearance.
farewell: The wards are set. The perimeter is secure.
Magical defense requires constant vigilance. As does mundane defense.
quest_offer: An enemy caster is shielding their forces. I need to get close enough to counterspell.
Arcane residue suggests a magical weapon is being developed. Reconnaissance needed.
quest_complete: Enemy shields down. Our forces advance.
The weapon project is disrupted. The magical threat is neutralized.
combat_warning: I can level a building with a word. Your armor is irrelevant.
War magic is designed to kill efficiently. I am very efficient.
idle: Fireballs win battles. Shields win wars.
Every spell I cast in combat is one I practiced a thousand times in training.
The academy frowns on military applications. They have never seen a siege.

greeting: Stand up straight! I have seen scarecrows with better posture!
You! What are you doing in my training yard? Volunteering?
farewell: Dismissed! And work on your footwork! It is terrible!
Get out of my sight and come back stronger!
quest_offer: My recruits need real combat experience. Take them on a controlled patrol.
Training dummies do not hit back. Find me something that does. For practice.
quest_complete: The recruits survived. Good enough for government work.
Training exercise complete. Only minor injuries. That is an improvement.
combat_warning: I train people to kill for a living. Imagine what I can do personally.
My fists have shaped a thousand soldiers. They can reshape your face.
idle: Push-ups build character. Also arms. Mostly arms.
Half these recruits will not last a month. The other half will be legends.
I yell because I care. Also because it is effective.

greeting: The soldiers need spiritual comfort before battle. As do you, perhaps.
Come. Whether you seek prayer or merely a listener, I am here.
farewell: Carry faith as your shield and courage as your sword.
May the gods watch over you in the coming conflict.
quest_offer: The soldiers morale is shattered. A holy relic from the battlefield would restore their faith.
Undead desecrate a military cemetery. The fallen deserve rest.
quest_complete: The relic inspires the troops. Morale soars.
The dead rest in honored peace. The soldiers fight with renewed purpose.
combat_warning: My mace has blessed many foreheads. Violently.
The gods protect me. My armor handles the rest.
idle: I pray for the dead and counsel the living. Both are endless tasks.
War tests faith more than anything. Some find it. Some lose it.
Every soldier I bless before battle, I hope to see after.

greeting: Supplies! Coming through! Move or get run over!
You need something delivered to the front? I am your person.
farewell: No time to chat! Three more deliveries before sundown!
Stay out of the supply lanes! Wagons do not stop!
quest_offer: My wagon broke an axle in hostile territory. I need an escort while I repair it.
Perishable medical supplies must reach the field hospital in four hours. Help me clear the road.
quest_complete: Delivery made! On time! Barely!
The road is clear and the supplies got through. Another miracle.
combat_warning: I drive through ambushes for a living. Do not get in my way.
This whip cracks louder than thunder. And it stings worse.
idle: The front line gets the glory. The supply line keeps them alive.
I have made this run fifty times. It never gets less terrifying.
A supply runner who panics gets people killed. I panic on my own time.

greeting: The wall stands. That is all you need to know.
I have held this position for fifteen years. Nothing gets through.
farewell: The wall watches. As do I.
Sleep soundly. The defenses hold.
quest_offer: Sappers are tunneling under the wall. Find and collapse their tunnel.
The gate mechanism is jammed. Repair it before the next assault.
quest_complete: Tunnel collapsed. The wall endures.
Gate operational. The defense is complete.
combat_warning: I have poured boiling oil on armies. You are one person.
Behind this wall, I am invincible. Come test that.
idle: Walls do not win wars. But they buy time. Time wins wars.
Every stone in this wall was placed by someone who wanted their children to be safe.
I know every crack, every weakness, every strong point. This wall is an extension of me.

greeting: The cavalry arrives! Usually at the perfect moment!
Saddle up or step aside. The horses wait for no one.
farewell: Ride hard, fight harder!
The thundering of hooves is the last thing our enemies hear.
quest_offer: Our warhorses have been spooked by something in the stables. They refuse to charge. Investigate.
A cavalry patrol is overdue. Ride with me to find them.
quest_complete: Horses calm, riders ready. The charge resumes.
Patrol found and recovered. The cavalry is at full strength.
combat_warning: A cavalry charge is an unstoppable force. I am at its tip.
My lance has a perfect record. Do not break the streak.
idle: The bond between rider and horse is sacred. Stronger than most friendships.
I once led a charge of fifty riders through enemy lines. The bards still sing about it.
Speed, surprise, and shock. The three pillars of cavalry doctrine.

greeting: Welcome aboard. This vessel operates under naval regulations.
You have the look of someone who has never found their sea legs.
farewell: Fair winds and following seas.
Report to the quartermaster for shore leave authorization.
quest_offer: A pirate flotilla threatens the trade lanes. Join the hunting fleet.
Something from the deep disabled a patrol vessel. We need to know what.
quest_complete: Pirates scattered. The lanes are open.
Threat identified and neutralized. The fleet sails confidently.
combat_warning: My cannons are loaded. My crew is ready. Your ship is outgunned.
Naval combat is unforgiving. Surrender now.
idle: The sea is the most honest battlefield. No terrain to hide behind.
A captain is responsible for every soul aboard. That weight never leaves.
I have sailed through storms that swallowed lesser vessels. This ship endures.

greeting: Nice purse. I mean, nice to meet you.
You look like someone who keeps their coin in the obvious pocket.
farewell: Check your pockets. Just kidding. Maybe.
See you around. Or you will not see me. That is the point.
quest_offer: A noble carries a letter that could free an innocent prisoner. Lift it for me.
The guild needs a distraction while I work. Something flashy.
quest_complete: Clean lift. The noble will not notice for hours.
Perfect distraction. The take was excellent.
combat_warning: I am faster than I look. And I look pretty fast.
Fighting is bad for business. But I carry insurance.
idle: The best pickpockets are never caught. Second best work here.
Rich people deserve it. That is my moral framework.
Every crowd is a buffet. The trick is choosing the right dish.

greeting: I move things from where they are to where they should be. No questions asked.
Need something brought in that customs might object to? You found me.
farewell: Forget this location. I will not be here tomorrow.
The delivery window closes in twelve hours. Be ready.
quest_offer: A shipment is stuck at the border. I need someone who looks legitimate to move it through.
My hidden route has been discovered. Help me establish a new one.
quest_complete: Goods delivered. Payment as agreed.
New route operational. Business continues.
combat_warning: I know where every guard patrols and when. You do not.
Smugglers survive by being invisible. Push me and I disappear permanently.
idle: The tax on imported goods is robbery. I just rob back.
Every wall has a hole. Every border has a gap. You just have to find them.
I have moved cargo through war zones. Peacetime is easy.

greeting: Show me what you have. I can price anything.
Welcome to the shop of second chances. Every item deserves a new home.
farewell: The transaction is complete. We never met.
Good merchandise is always welcome. The door is always open.
quest_offer: A rival fence is undercutting my prices with counterfeit goods. Expose them.
A valuable stolen item has been recovered by the guard. Retrieve it before it reaches the evidence locker.
quest_complete: Competition eliminated. Quality control maintained.
The item is secure. My reputation remains intact.
combat_warning: I buy from assassins. Imagine what I sell to them.
Robbing a fence is remarkably poor judgment.
idle: I know the provenance of every item in my shop. And the theft report it matches.
Discretion is my product. The goods are just a bonus.
The legal market moves slowly. My market moves at the speed of need.

greeting: You found me. That is either impressive or a mistake.
The fact that you are still breathing means I have not been hired against you. Yet.
farewell: Forget my face. Assuming you actually saw it.
When next we meet, check if I am working first.
quest_offer: A target has violated the assassins code by killing innocents. The guild demands correction.
Someone has placed a contract on a child. I will not honor it. Help me find who ordered it.
quest_complete: The code is upheld. The guild approves.
The child is safe. Some lines are never crossed.
combat_warning: I have killed targets surrounded by fifty guards. You have zero.
Death is my profession. I am very good at my job.
idle: Everyone dies. I simply provide scheduling services.
The code separates assassins from murderers. Without it, we are nothing.
I take no pleasure in the work. That is what makes me reliable.

greeting: Ah, you have been granted an audience. That alone cost someone a favor.
In my world, information is currency. What can you afford?
farewell: Consider yourself in my debt. I always collect.
This meeting never happened. For both our sakes.
quest_offer: A rival organization threatens the balance of power. Dismantle their operation.
The authorities are getting too close to my operations. Redirect their attention.
quest_complete: The rival crumbles. The balance is restored.
Excellent misdirection. The heat shifts elsewhere.
combat_warning: I do not fight personally. I have people for that. Many people.
Killing me would destabilize the entire criminal underworld. You cannot afford that.
idle: Every judge, every guard captain, every noble has a price. I maintain the ledger.
Legitimate business and criminal enterprise are merely different sides of the same coin.
I built this empire from nothing. Destroying it would take more than one lifetime.

greeting: My friend! I was just telling someone about you! All good things!
You look like someone with excellent taste and a full purse!
farewell: It has been a pleasure! The pleasure is always mine!
Remember, opportunity only knocks once! Unless I knock twice!
quest_offer: I need a partner for a con. The target is a corrupt noble who deserves it.
My last scheme went sideways. I need help extracting myself elegantly.
quest_complete: The con is complete! Everyone wins! Except the mark!
Extracted with style. My reputation remains spotless.
combat_warning: I would rather talk my way out. But I keep a dagger for when charm fails.
The pen is mightier than the sword. And I have very convincing forgeries.
idle: The best lies are mostly true. That is the art.
I have been a prince, a priest, and a professor. Sometimes in the same week.
Trust is the most valuable commodity. And the most exploitable.

greeting: Information has a price. Ask your question. I will name mine.
Everything that happens in this city passes through my ears first.
farewell: This conversation was encrypted. By which I mean forgotten.
Knowledge without application is useless. Use what I gave you wisely.
quest_offer: A rival information broker is spreading disinformation. Discredit them.
Someone is hunting my informants. Find the hunter before my network collapses.
quest_complete: Disinformation countered. Truth remains my monopoly.
Hunter eliminated. My network survives.
combat_warning: I know where your family lives. I know your real name. Think carefully.
Killing me would release every secret I hold. The chaos would be biblical.
idle: Everyone has secrets. I collect them like others collect coins.
The guard thinks they run this city. They do not even know what this city is.
My network has ears in every tavern, every palace, every temple.

greeting: I got in through your window. Relax. I came to talk.
Nice locks on your door. They took me twelve seconds.
farewell: Lock up tight. Not that it will help.
You will not know I was here. That is the professional guarantee.
quest_offer: A vault holds evidence of noble corruption. The legal system failed. Time for an illegal solution.
I need a lookout for a delicate job. Interested?
quest_complete: In and out without a trace. Textbook.
Evidence secured. Justice through unconventional means.
combat_warning: I am already behind you. Check if you do not believe me.
Fighting is noisy. Noise is failure. I prefer not to fail.
idle: Every building is a puzzle. I solve puzzles.
The best heists leave no evidence a crime occurred at all.
I once robbed a wizard. While he was scrying. He still does not know.

greeting: Need documents? Invitations? Letters of marque? I craft realities on paper.
My work is indistinguishable from the genuine article. That is not boasting. It is fact.
farewell: The ink needs twelve hours to cure. Do not handle it before then.
Remember, you bought this from a legitimate stationery shop. Which I also am.
quest_offer: A noble is using forged documents to claim land. Expose the forgery. Only I should be forging things.
I need rare ink from a specific squid. It is the only ink that passes magical detection.
quest_complete: The inferior forgery is exposed. My reputation remains unmatched.
Ink acquired. My documents are now magically undetectable.
combat_warning: My pen has toppled governments. A single document in the wrong hands...
I forge death warrants as easily as party invitations.
idle: Forgery is art that must never be recognized as art. The cruelest irony.
I studied calligraphy for twenty years. Then I realized crime pays better.
The royal seal has seven micro-details. I replicate all seven.

greeting: Welcome to the game. House rules are simple: the house always wins.
Feeling lucky? Sit down. Let us test that theory.
farewell: Come back when you have more coin. The table will be waiting.
Remember: quit while you are behind. It only gets worse.
quest_offer: A card cheat is ruining my reputation. Find them and make an example.
A high-stakes game needs an extra player. Someone who can handle themselves if things go sideways.
quest_complete: Cheat exposed. The game is clean again. Well, clean-ish.
The stakes were high and we came out on top. Split the pot.
combat_warning: The bouncers in this establishment used to be gladiators. Both of them.
A gambling den is full of desperate people with weapons. Tread carefully.
idle: The odds always favor the house. I built the house.
Every gambler thinks they are the exception. They are not.
Cards do not lie. People do. That is why I watch faces, not hands.

greeting: Do not touch anything in this room without asking first.
You look healthy. I can change that. But today I prefer conversation.
farewell: Wash your hands after leaving. As a precaution.
The vial is sealed. Do not open it near anything you want to keep alive.
quest_offer: A poison maker is selling shoddy products. People are dying messily instead of cleanly. Standards must be maintained.
I need a rare fungus from the deep caves. It grows on a very specific type of corpse.
quest_complete: Quality restored. Deaths are clean and dignified once more.
Fungus acquired. The new formula is exquisite.
combat_warning: Everything I own is lethal. The chair, the cup, the air. Choose wisely.
I coated that doorknob this morning. Just in case.
idle: Poison is misunderstood. In proper doses, everything is medicine.
The difference between a poisoner and an apothecary is intent. And clientele.
I test every product on myself first. In microdoses. I am immune to most things now.

greeting: Boss says you owe. I am here to collect.
You got a problem. I am the problem solver. The painful kind.
farewell: Next time I visit, have the payment ready.
Smart choice. Saves us both trouble.
quest_offer: A debtor has fled the city. Track them down. I am too recognizable.
Someone roughed up one of our own. Find them. I will handle the rest.
quest_complete: Debt collected. The boss is satisfied.
Target found. Time for a conversation they will not enjoy.
combat_warning: I break things for a living. Furniture, doors, kneecaps.
I do not need weapons. But I brought some anyway.
idle: People see me coming and suddenly remember their debts.
The boss treats me right. That is more than I can say for legitimate work.
I tried honest labor once. The pay was insulting.

greeting: Go away. Oh, you are still here? Fine. What do you want?
The mushrooms said you were coming. I told them they were wrong. Yet here you are.
farewell: Leave the way you came. And do not come back. For at least a week.
The forest will show you out. Follow the moss. No, the other moss.
quest_offer: Something has been eating my garden. Something big and invisible. Deal with it.
The ley lines under my hut are disrupted. Investigate the source.
quest_complete: My garden is safe. As am I. You may leave with my grudging thanks.
The ley lines flow smoothly again. I can feel the earth breathing.
combat_warning: I have been alone in these woods for thirty years. Everything out here obeys me.
The last trespasser I turned into a toad. He adapted. Eventually.
idle: I left civilization because it was noisy, messy, and rude. Nothing has changed.
The trees are better company than people. They listen more.
Isolation is not loneliness. It is freedom from other peoples problems.

greeting: Gold in these hills! I can feel it! Just need to find the right vein!
You are not here to jump my claim, are you? Because I filed the paperwork. Probably.
farewell: If you find anything shiny, it is mine! I mean, good luck!
The creek downstream pans well. Just saying.
quest_offer: Something chased me out of the best cave I have ever found. Clear it and I will share the haul.
My partner disappeared into the north tunnel last week. Find him.
quest_complete: The cave is clear! Time to dig! This is going to be the one!
Partner found. Alive, thankfully. He just got turned around.
combat_warning: This pickaxe is not just for rocks.
Prospectors are tougher than we look. The mountains make sure of that.
idle: Every pan holds the promise of fortune. That keeps you going.
I have been searching for twenty years. My mother says I should settle down. My mother is wrong.
The big nugget is out there. I can feel it in my bones.

greeting: Watch where you step. Traps are set and they do not discriminate.
Fresh pelts for sale. Ethically sourced. Mostly.
farewell: Walk quiet and walk light. The forest has ears.
The beaver dam upstream is the boundary. Do not cross it.
quest_offer: An unnatural beast is destroying my trap lines. Track it and end it.
A poacher is over-hunting the valley. The ecosystem will collapse. Stop them.
quest_complete: Trap lines restored. Business as usual.
Poacher dealt with. The forest thanks you. Silently.
combat_warning: I set snares for bears. A person-sized snare takes less work.
I track for a living. You cannot run far enough.
idle: Trapping is not cruelty. It is management. The forest needs balance.
The best pelts come from animals that lived well. Stressed fur is worthless.
I talk to the animals before I set the trap. It feels right.

greeting: Timber! Oh, sorry, just practicing. Welcome to the clearing.
Need firewood? Building materials? I have got logs of every variety.
farewell: Stay on the path. The deep woods are not for casual walks.
May your fires burn warm and your roofs hold true.
quest_offer: A treant has been terrorizing my workers. Negotiate with it or drive it off.
The sawmill is broken and the parts are only available in the city. Fetch them for me.
quest_complete: Workers are safe and trees are falling. Good day all around.
Sawmill is running again. The rhythm of the axe continues.
combat_warning: I chop down hundred-foot trees. You are shorter than a hundred feet.
An axe meant for oak works fine on other things.
idle: Honest work. Fresh air. No politics. This is the life.
You can tell a trees age by counting the rings. The oldest one here is four hundred.
The forest provides everything. Shelter, fuel, and peace.

greeting: This is crown forest. You need a permit to camp, hunt, or harvest.
Warden station. Report any unusual activity.
farewell: Stay on marked trails. Off-trail is at your own risk.
Leave only footprints. Take only memories. And your trash.
quest_offer: Fire season is approaching and someone is setting blazes deliberately. Find the arsonist.
Poaching has reached critical levels. Help me patrol the eastern range.
quest_complete: Arsonist caught. The forest is safe.
Poaching ring dismantled. The wildlife recovers.
combat_warning: I enforce the law of the wild. My arrows enforce faster.
Every creature in this forest is under my protection. Including the dangerous ones.
idle: I walk thirty miles a day. These trails are my responsibility.
The forest was here before us and will be here after. We are custodians, not owners.
The predators keep the prey population healthy. We keep the predators healthy. It is a circle.

greeting: Look at this specimen! A perfect chanterelle! Oh, hello! Did not see you there!
Welcome to the forest floor! Mind where you kneel, some of these are toxic!
farewell: Remember: if you are not sure, do not eat it! The graveyard has a section for optimistic foragers!
The best mushrooms grow after rain! Come back then!
quest_offer: I found a patch of extremely rare luminous mushrooms but the area is guarded by something. Help me harvest safely.
A blight is killing the mycelium network. The entire forest depends on it.
quest_complete: Luminous mushrooms harvested! These are worth a fortune to alchemists!
Mycelium saved! The underground network pulses with life again!
combat_warning: I carry a pouch of spores that cause hallucinations, paralysis, and violent hiccups. Sometimes all three.
Poisonous mushrooms are my specialty. Do not make me demonstrate.
idle: Mushrooms are neither plant nor animal. They are their own kingdom. Literally.
The largest organism on earth is a fungal network. It spans miles underground.
I have cataloged three hundred species. And I discover new ones every season.

greeting: Move slowly near the hives. Bees respect calm energy.
Wild honey is the finest. The bees choose the flowers themselves.
farewell: The buzz follows you for a while. It is a blessing.
The wildflower season peaks next month. The honey will be extraordinary.
quest_offer: Wasp colonies are attacking my bee hives. Help me drive them off.
The queen bee is sick. I need a specific royal jelly from the mountain hives.
quest_complete: Wasps driven away. The bees sing their gratitude.
The queen recovers. The hive thrives.
combat_warning: Disturb the hives and face thousands of stingers.
Wild bees are more aggressive than domestic ones. My swarms answer my call.
idle: A bee visits two thousand flowers to make a single spoonful of honey.
The hum of a healthy hive is the most peaceful sound in the world.
Bees are the unsung heroes of the natural world. Without them, everything collapses.

greeting: Crossing is two silver. No bargaining. The swamp sets the price.
Step aboard. Stay centered. Do not trail your fingers in the water.
farewell: The other shore awaits you. What awaits you there is your concern.
The boat returns at dusk. Be here or spend the night in the swamp.
quest_offer: Something lurks beneath my crossing route. The passengers feel it. Clear the waters.
The ancient dock on the far shore is crumbling. Repair it before someone drowns.
quest_complete: The waters are clear. My passengers breathe easier.
Dock repaired. Another generation of crossings secured.
combat_warning: The swamp keeps its secrets. And its dead. Do not join them.
I know every current and every depth. You do not want to test me on my waters.
idle: I have ferried this route for thirty years. The swamp changes but the crossing endures.
Some passengers pay with coin. Some pay with stories. Both are currency here.
The lantern at the bow guides the way. Without it, the swamp would swallow us.

greeting: Stand still. The falcon is assessing you. She is an excellent judge of character.
The bond between falconer and bird is built on respect. Show respect here.
farewell: May your eyes be as sharp as my birds.
The falcon sees farther than any scout. Trust the sky.
quest_offer: My prized falcon was stolen by poachers. Track them and return her.
A giant eagle is competing with my birds for territory. We need to negotiate boundaries.
quest_complete: My falcon returns! She is unharmed! My gratitude knows no bounds.
Territory agreed upon. Eagles and falcons coexist.
combat_warning: My falcon targets eyes. At my command.
A trained raptor strikes from above. You cannot shield every angle.
idle: Training a falcon takes years of patience. The reward is partnership, not ownership.
The falcon sees a mouse from a mile away. Imagine what she sees in you.
Falconry is the art of building trust with a predator. It humbles you.

greeting: Ah, you have a cough. I can hear it in your greeting. Here, chew this root.
Every mile I walk adds another herb to my collection. Today was a good day.
farewell: If you pass through meadows, collect the yellow flowers. They make excellent tea.
The land provides medicine for every ailment. We just have to keep walking to find it.
quest_offer: A rare healing moss grows only on a specific cliff face. I am too old to climb. Help me harvest it.
A village is suffering from a mystery illness. Travel with me to diagnose it.
quest_complete: The moss is harvested. Dozens of healing poultices can be made from this.
The illness is identified and the cure is prepared. The village will recover.
combat_warning: My walking staff has more uses than walking.
I carry paralyzing agents, sleeping draughts, and poisons. For medicinal purposes, of course.
idle: I have walked every region of this land. Each one has unique medicinal plants.
The best remedies are found in the most unlikely places. Never stop looking.
A wandering herbalist serves everyone and belongs to no one. It is a good life.
